item.title: Archon：统一多模态数字人类生成模型
item.content:encoded:
Archon是一个统一多模态的数字人类生成模型，提升交互体验。



摘要： 本文介绍了Archon，一个基于多模态数据（文本、音频、动作和视觉内容）的统一预训练模型，用于生成高质量的数字人类。Archon通过模态特定的分词器和自回归模型，整合七种模态，并在72个任务上进行预训练，以建模联合分布。为解决高保真视频中的令牌爆炸问题，研究提出了一种高效的语义视频重参数化方法，实现4倍令牌减少并保留细节。此外，引入了“跨模态思考”机制，逐步提升生成质量与可控性。实验表明，Archon在多种数字人类生成任务中表现优异，验证了其统一框架的有效性。

item.title: 经典与现代后置水印技术在真实场景下的比较研究
item.content:encoded:
经典水印技术在安全性上优于现代方法。



摘要： 随着生成模型的广泛应用，数字水印成为识别AI生成图像的重要手段。现代后置水印方法利用神经网络实现极低误报率，同时对常见图像变换具有鲁棒性。然而，现有研究缺乏对现代方法与传统方法在真实场景中的对比，特别是在安全性和鲁棒性方面。本文对不同类型的经典增强和近期攻击进行了公平比较，实验表明，在现实场景中，传统水印技术在保持鲁棒性的同时，安全性优于现代方法。

item.title: 跨模型链式推理转移机制研究
item.content:encoded:
研究跨模型链式推理转移机制及其对答案生成的影响。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型推理模型生成的链式推理痕迹（CoT）如何被其他模型用于解决相同任务，分析了不同模式下CoT对推理效果的影响。通过对比强制回答和自由生成两种方式，发现完整CoT在多数情况下能有效转移，但不同模型依赖不同的机制。例如，AIME主要依赖答案的显性提供，MMLU-Pro则更依赖接收者能力，而ZebraLogic依赖部分结构化信息。此外，部分CoT可以引导继续推理，提升性能。研究还表明，接收者的答案一致性可作为停止推理的信号。跨模型CoT转移并非单一现象，可能涉及答案提取、推理支撑或接收者能力等不同机制。

item.title: 基于多项逻辑函数近似的强化学习理论分析
item.content:encoded:
本文提出新的理论分析方法，提升MNL强化学习性能。



摘要： 本文研究了基于多项逻辑函数近似的强化学习框架，提出了新的理论分析方法，以更精确地衡量学习者与环境交互中的方差对性能的影响。该方法获得了适应方差的后悔界，并在计算效率和最优后悔率上表现出色。实验验证了该方法在学习最优策略方面的有效性，优于传统方法。

item.title: 基于粗到细层次目标的离线强化学习方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CFHRL方法解决长时任务中的目标条件强化学习问题。



摘要： 本文针对长时任务中离线目标条件强化学习（GCRL）的挑战，提出一种自适应的目标细化框架CFHRL。该方法通过从最终目标开始递归生成中间目标，并利用经验回放进行训练，直到当前目标被估计为局部可执行。关键思想是中间目标无需精确位于路径中点，只需提供可靠进展并降低剩余难度，从而在较短时间范围内进行后续细化。实验表明，在OGBench数据集上，CFHRL在多个长时任务中表现出显著提升，消融实验验证了细化和停止机制的有效性。

item.title: AI/ML领域应摒弃‘正向后门’标签，采用‘秘密对齐’概念
item.content:encoded:
文章主张AI社区停止使用‘正向后门’标签，改用‘秘密对齐’概念，并强调需严格评估其安全性。



摘要： 本文指出，AI/ML领域应停止过度宣传‘正向后门’，并将其视为一种‘秘密对齐’机制。作者认为，基于秘密对齐的保护性声明默认不安全，除非有严格的标准化评估支持。随着私有AI时代的到来，语言模型成为私人资产，带来未经授权访问、模型窃取和行为滥用等安全风险。近期一些被称作‘正向后门’的工作旨在应对这些挑战，但本文将这些方法统一为隐含触发-行为关联，并在六个核心属性上进行了评估，发现其存在显著脆弱性，尤其是在保密性、完整性和可用性方面。文章还探讨了行为密度与决策复杂度的关系，提出从行为角度理解部署风险，并呼吁社区进行可验证的秘密对齐评估。

item.title: 基于自然语言查询的因果效应预测框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出Query2Effect框架，提升因果效应预测准确性。



摘要： 本文介绍了Query2Effect，一个包含72,000个自然语言问题的大规模基准数据集，用于模拟真实的信息检索场景。研究提出了一种两步框架，首先生成查询的结构化表示，再通过监督编码模型预测因果效应大小。实验表明，微调显著提升了预测性能，绝对误差降低了27%至71%，且该框架在跨领域泛化方面表现优异，证明了语义解释与数值估计分离的优势。

item.title: 监督微调模型在PiSAR数据集上的性能基准测试
item.content:encoded:
微调模型在PiSAR数据集上表现优于零样本基线。



摘要： 研究对三种监督微调模型与前沿的零样本基线模型在PiSAR数据集上进行了基准测试。该数据集包含661行屏幕锚定行为推理数据。结果显示，微调后的Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct模型在语义相似度上达到0.783，远超Claude Opus 4.7和GPT-5.5的0.459和0.482。而使用相同训练数据的Gemma-4-26B-A4B-IT模型仅获得0.441，表明存在模型与训练方法之间的不匹配问题。

item.title: OrcaRouter：一种基于上下文强化学习的大型语言模型路由系统
item.content:encoded:
OrcaRouter通过强化学习优化模型选择，提升部署效率与成本效益。



摘要： 本文介绍了OrcaRouter，一个用于大型语言模型（LLM）部署的高效路由系统。该系统结合了基于LinUCB的上下文强化学习算法，利用词法和句子嵌入特征进行模型选择，并采用离线-在线混合学习策略。在离线阶段，OrcaRouter通过评估候选模型在特定提示上的表现，生成奖励矩阵并训练回归模型；在部署阶段，它从预训练参数初始化，并可继续根据带宽反馈进行在线学习。在RouterArena测试中，OrcaRouter-Adaptive以72.08分位列第二，准确率达75.54%，每千次查询成本为1美元。

item.title: 分子混合物性质预测的评估框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种分解混合物性质误差的新评估框架。



摘要： 本文针对分子混合物性质预测中普遍存在的问题，提出了一种新的评估框架，该框架能够将混合物性质的误差分解为纯组分误差和非理想相互作用误差。研究强调了绝对准确性无法全面反映混合物行为的局限性，并通过七个匹配的纯物质与混合物物理化学性质数据集进行了验证。实验结果表明，模型在绝对准确性高的情况下可能仍无法准确捕捉非理想混合行为，且在面对未见分子时性能显著下降。这揭示了分子混合物机器学习中的关键挑战，并呼吁超越绝对准确性的评估方式。

item.title: GONDOR：一种高效的贪心最佳优先搜索算法
item.content:encoded:
GONDOR提升低内存环境下的搜索覆盖率。



摘要： GONDOR是一种改进的贪心最佳优先搜索算法，旨在解决在严格内存限制下路径规划和搜索问题。它通过定期压缩搜索树并保留少量锚点状态，实现内存高效搜索，并在到达目标后通过重新搜索锚点状态重建路径。研究分析了不同锚点选择策略的变体，并引入布隆过滤器以优化重复状态检测。实验表明，在多种数值规划领域和启发式配置中，GONDOR相比传统GBFS显著提升了搜索覆盖范围。该算法的实现已公开，便于后续研究。

item.title: 基于非高斯核的全局优化平滑框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的全局优化平滑方法，提升鲁棒性和性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种通用的平滑框架，结合灵活的对称单峰核与单调比率变换，避免了传统方法对超参数的敏感性。理论分析表明，该方法在足够大的放大系数下能保持全局最大值，并将所有驻点集中在真实最优解附近。同时，文章提供了随机梯度上升的复杂度界限，并验证了留一法基线在降低方差方面的有效性。实验结果显示该方法在高维基准和黑盒对抗攻击中表现出更强的鲁棒性和竞争力。

item.title: 基于文本输出的大型语言模型参数规模推断方法
item.content:encoded:
通过文本预测推断大模型参数规模，揭示行业设计策略差异。



摘要： 本文提出一种黑盒方法，通过分析模型生成的文本输出来推断大型语言模型的保守参数下限。该方法利用经典文献、宗教文本等广泛存在于预训练数据中的内容，评估模型对不同长度文本片段的预测准确性，从而反映其记忆能力，进而推断参数规模。研究构建了两种互补的推断方法：一种是用于比较两个模型大小的统计检验，另一种是通过主成分分析提取参数规模的潜在指标。实验验证表明，该方法在开源模型上表现准确，并应用于闭源模型后揭示了行业内不同的参数增长策略，展示了在API限制下仍可系统性探测隐藏设计选择的可能性。

item.title: Agyn：面向AI代理的可扩展运行平台
item.content:encoded:
Agyn提供安全、可扩展的AI代理运行平台。



摘要： 随着AI代理在生产环境中广泛应用，其非确定性工作流程、状态会话和特权访问特性带来了新的工程挑战。本文介绍了Agyn，一个基于Kubernetes的信号驱动、状态感知的无服务器运行时，结合Terraform提供代理和框架定义，并采用零信任与最小权限安全模型。Agyn具备代理、模型和云平台的兼容性，适用于大规模AI代理管理。

item.title: 流式音频语言模型中的等待-思考-回答控制机制研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种流式音频语言模型的等待-思考-回答控制机制，提升交互质量与响应效率。



摘要： 本文探讨了在流式语音交互中，如何平衡推理质量和响应延迟的问题。作者引入了一种可学习的等待-思考-回答控制机制，该机制基于人类对话的渐进性，能够在部分音频证据下决定何时等待、何时进行推理更新以及何时给出答案。通过使用Qwen2.5-Omni-7B作为基础模型，构建了对齐的等待-思考-回答数据，并采用监督微调和动态采样策略优化（DAPO）进行训练。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中提升了准确率并减少了最终推理时间。结果表明，在音频流中适时显式推理有助于提升流式模型的交互性能。

item.title: 基于信息瓶颈的在线强化学习方法提升大语言模型性能
item.content:encoded:
提出IB-Score与IB-TPO提升LLM在线RL性能。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型在在线强化学习中探索与利用不平衡的问题，提出了一种基于信息瓶颈理论的新指标IB-Score，用于评估策略的平衡性。通过分析发现现有方法如GRPO在训练过程中难以保持平衡，导致性能不佳。为此，作者提出了IB-TPO框架，结合IB-Score作为优化目标，并引入IB引导的树采样策略，显著提升了在线采样效率和性能。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中优于现有方法，性能提升2.9%至3.6%。

item.title: CogniVerse：一种改进多模态检索增强生成的框架
item.content:encoded:
CogniVerse提升多模态问答的准确性和连贯性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为CogniVerse的新多模态检索增强生成（MMRAG）框架，旨在解决现有系统在检索噪声、跨模态语义对齐、自适应推理和生成连贯性方面的不足。该框架借鉴人类认知机制，包含三个协同组件：认知反思模块用于动态评估检索需求并过滤相关内容；多模态检索模块通过信息几何和谱图理论实现嵌入对齐与知识图谱优化；层次化生成模块则利用最优传输损失平衡局部准确性和全局语义连贯性。实验表明，CogniVerse在准确性和生成连贯性方面均优于现有系统，同时降低了检索延迟。

item.title: 基于李代数的连续时间生成建模框架
item.content:encoded:
通过固定向量场与可学习标量控制构建高效生成模型。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于Chow-Rashevskii定理的连续时间生成建模框架，利用少量固定向量场和可学习标量控制构建表达性强的流。该方法通过调节固定向量场的标量控制函数来构造速度场，而非直接学习高维向量场。当固定向量场为括号生成时，其李代数可以覆盖整个空间，从而在较少控制通道下实现高效的分布传输。该框架具有参数高效和几何解释性强的特点，能够独立于环境维度选择可学习的标量输出通道。文章还提出了一个表达性原理，证明在适当可控性和适定性假设下，此类控制流可以将源分布转换为目标分布。模型通过连续归一化流似然目标进行训练，并在合成数据上进行了验证。

item.title: SURE：提升语音模型部署评估可比性与可复现性的统一框架
item.content:encoded:
SURE统一了语音模型评估与训练流程，提升可比性和可复现性。



摘要： 本文介绍了SURE，一个用于语音基础模型和语音大语言模型的统一实验框架。SURE通过标准化预测格式、归一化和评分，解决了因后处理不一致和训练结果难以跨数据规模和流水线复现而导致的评估不可比问题。SURE在真实声学和语言压力下对传统流水线和语音LLMs进行评估，并引入代理辅助的训练转换流程，将论文和代码映射为版本化的、可运行的训练流水线。该框架提升了部署导向评估的可比性与可复现性。

item.title: LLM记忆系统错误追踪与优化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出MemTrace框架，实现LLM记忆系统错误追踪与性能提升。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLM）记忆系统的错误追踪与归因问题，提出了一种将记忆流水线转换为可执行记忆演化图的新框架，以实现对信息流的细粒度追踪。同时构建了MemTraceBench基准测试集，涵盖多种代表性记忆系统，系统地分析了记忆失败模式。文中还引入了自动归因方法，通过迭代追踪操作子图来定位失败根源。分析表明，记忆故障主要源于操作层面的问题，如信息丢失和检索错位。进一步利用细粒度归因信号指导下游提示优化，建立了闭环系统，可自动修正故障并提升任务性能达7.62%。代码已开源。

item.title: ForestHG-Trace：用于森林生态推理的可追溯框架
item.content:encoded:
提出ForestHG-Trace框架提升森林生态问答准确性。



摘要： 文章介绍了ForestHG-Trace，一个用于森林环境长期生态推理的可追溯框架。该框架通过多模态NEON森林场景构建生态超图，支持更高阶的推理。结合LLM引导的代理，执行读取、过滤、扩展、聚合等操作，生成可回放的执行痕迹和紧凑证据记录。同时构建了ForestTraceQA基准测试，评估多种任务类型和推理深度的生态问答性能。实验表明，该框架在答案准确性和执行忠实性上优于单步基线和场景图代理，但执行深度仍是长期生态问答的主要瓶颈。

item.title: 视频光照调和与去闪烁模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种视频光照调和方法，提升视频与背景场景的匹配度。



摘要： 本文提出一种视频光照调和方法，通过调整阴影、色调和光照强度，使前景视频与目标背景场景相匹配。由于视频标注数据难以获取，作者采用去闪烁模型来减少帧间光照抖动，并利用真实和合成视频训练扩散模型以生成高质量的视频调和结果。同时，引入非对称alpha掩码条件技术，从真实视频中学习清晰边界。实验表明，该方法在时间一致性、自然性、边界清晰度和物理光照行为方面优于现有方法。

item.title: 显式图像工具交互提升多模态越狱鲁棒性研究
item.content:encoded:
显式图像工具交互有效降低越狱成功率，提升模型安全性。



摘要： 本文探讨了多种视觉-语言模型推理范式在越狱攻击下的鲁棒性表现。通过实验发现，显式图像工具交互方法在多个模型中平均将越狱成功率降低了约30%，表现出更高的安全性。尽管返回的图像内容可能不安全或被手动覆盖，但其攻击成功率仍显著低于直接回答方式。研究提出了一种图像工具安全向量框架，解释了图像工具调用如何通过隐藏表示的残差偏移提升安全性。该研究为多模态模型的安全设计提供了新思路，并强调了针对不同流程进行安全评估的重要性。

item.title: 基于PILOT的实时语义分割模型持续学习方法
item.content:encoded:
提出PILOT框架，实现实时语义分割模型的增量学习。



摘要： 本文针对实时语义分割模型在动态环境中持续学习新类别的需求，提出了一种名为PILOT的新型持续学习框架。该框架通过引入并行导数分支（D-branch），在不重新训练原始网络参数的情况下，高效捕捉新类别的高频边界信息，从而避免灾难性遗忘。实验表明，该方法仅使用新类别的数据即可显著降低训练开销，并在保持原有类别高mIoU的同时，优于现有主流持续学习方法，有效维持实时性能。

item.title: 抗体结构与抗原相互作用的计算建模研究
item.content:encoded:
提升抗体-抗原相互作用预测精度，优化计算模型。



摘要： 本文探讨了抗体相关任务在计算建模中的挑战，并提出两种改进方法。首先，基于蛋白质语言模型（PLM）的方法在单体抗体预测中取得最佳效果，但在复合物预测中表现不佳，因缺乏抗原与抗体间的协同进化信号。其次，通过MSA优化和收敛感知重采样策略，提升了AlphaFold3在抗体-抗原复合物预测上的性能，且无需重新训练模型。

item.title: 多模态条件下的高质量人体运动生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出AnyMo框架，实现多模态条件下高质量人体运动生成。



摘要： 本文针对人体运动生成中的多模态交互与合成难题，提出了OmniHuMo数据集和AnyMo框架。OmniHuMo包含超过5000小时的运动数据及320万条对齐的多模态注释，涵盖文本、语音、音乐和轨迹等多种信息。AnyMo结合了基于残差FSQ的运动分词器和可扩展的掩码建模Transformer，能够在任意多模态组合下生成高质量的运动序列，展现出对空间和风格属性的灵活控制能力。

item.title: 知识图谱在电池材料假设生成中的有效性研究
item.content:encoded:
知识图谱对生成假设有影响，但并非必需。



摘要： 本文研究了知识图谱（KG）在电池材料假设生成中的作用，评估了不同模型（Mistral-7B、Llama-3.1-70B 和 Gemini 2.5 Flash）在不同 KG 结构下的表现。结果表明，KG 的结构和密度会影响生成结果，但模型自身也能够从先验知识中恢复部分 KG 内容。研究发现，小型的子图即可近似模拟完整 KG 的行为，表明有用的知识信号可以从压缩的科学结构子图中恢复，而非依赖完整的本地图。

item.title: Bengali语言大模型幻觉评估框架BenHalluEval的构建与分析
item.content:encoded:
首次针对孟加拉语大模型进行幻觉评估，提出BenHalluEval框架。



摘要： 本文首次系统评估了孟加拉语大语言模型中的幻觉问题，提出了BenHalluEval框架，涵盖生成式问答、混语问答、摘要和推理四个任务。通过构建12,000个幻觉样本，评估了七种不同类型的LLM，并引入BenHalluScore作为双轨校准指标，揭示了模型在幻觉检测上的显著差异。研究还指出单一轨道评估和仅靠提示策略不足以应对低资源语言场景下的幻觉问题。数据集和代码已公开。

item.title: BASTION：一种基于树的预算感知推测解码框架
item.content:encoded:
BASTION通过动态构建树结构提升推测解码效率，实现显著加速。



摘要： BASTION是一种预算感知的推测解码框架，采用基于树的扩散起草方法。与传统静态树结构不同，BASTION根据查询动态构建树，平衡草案质量和硬件限制。其包含三个协同组件：接受代理、在线延迟估计器和自适应最佳优先扩展。该框架无需训练，保持目标模型分布，且无需设置调优。在多种基准和GPU架构上，BASTION比标准自回归解码快6.61倍，优于现有块扩散基线39%。

item.title: 合成信息的起源与可追溯性机制
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨合成信息的起源及其可追溯性方法。



摘要： 文章指出，合成信息的起源是信息科学中的核心难题，其影响深远，涉及真相、信任和人类智力。由于人工智能的强大生成能力，合成信息的演化路径变得难以追踪。为此，作者提出一种类似遗传的隐写术机制，通过在生成物中隐藏父代特征，实现信息来源的可追溯性。该方法通过投影和编码技术，在信息生命周期内保留痕迹，并在需要时通过解码进行匹配。理论分析与实验验证表明，该方法在多种处理操作下仍具可行性，为构建可追踪的合成信息生态系统提供了新思路。

item.title: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Targeted Routing Hijacking in Federated RAG
item.content:encoded: Federated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (FedRAG) is attractive for privacy-sensitive applications because raw data remain local. As a result, routing must rely on client-provided semantic profiles, creating a new opportunity for manipulation. We introduce Routing Hijacking, a routing-stage attack in which a malicious client forges its profile to attract target queries despite having irrelevant underlying data. We show that this vulnerability is severe. Across three representative FedRAG routing architectures, Routing Hijacking consistently misroutes target queries and leads to downstream disruptions and failures, including missing evidence, poisoning, incorrect answers, and hallucinations. In a high-stakes MedQA-USMLE case study, we further show that poisoned retrieved evidence can mislead models across scales, leading to incorrect answers, hallucinations, and sycophantic failures. Existing defenses do not close this gap: encrypted routing preserves the exploited ranking, and Byzantine-robust Federated Learning (FL) rules transfer poorly to heterogeneous routing profiles. To address this gap, we propose a trust-aware post-routing framework that reweights clients using returned-evidence feedback, including retrieval relevance, profile consistency, and cross-client agreement; online experiments show that it suppresses persistent hijacking over recurring queries and transfers to a learned neural router. Our findings establish routing integrity as a new security challenge in FedRAG and highlight the need for stronger defenses for secure federated retrieval.

item.title: Trinity: Unifying Class-Agnostic Terrain and Semantic Segmentation for Unstructured Outdoor Environments by Leveraging Synthetic Data
item.content:encoded: Terrain understanding is fundamental for mobile robots operating in unstructured outdoor environments. Existing vision-based traversability estimation methods rely on robot-specific annotations or semantic class mappings, limiting transferability across platforms and requiring costly re-annotation when robot capabilities change, while standard semantic segmentation methods only focus on specific predefined classes, which do not capture the variety of terrains. In this work, we propose a transformer-based architecture that jointly performs class-specific semantic segmentation and class-agnostic terrain segmentation within a unified network, called Trinity. Terrain regions are segmented based solely on visual appearance, without predefined semantic labels or robot-dependent traversability scores. This formulation enables the learning of robot-agnostic visual terrain priors that can be combined with robot-specific experience for downstream tasks such as traversability estimation, visual odometry, and mission planning. To enable large-scale training with diverse terrain appearances, we extend the OAISYS simulator and introduce RUGDSynth, a synthetic dataset inspired by RUGD with class-agnostic terrain samples. Furthermore, we present the EXTerra Dataset, providing real-world images annotated with both class-specific and class-agnostic terrain labels. Experiments demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed task and the effectiveness of our joint segmentation approach in complex outdoor environments. Code and datasets will be released with this publication (after review).

item.title: From XXLTraffic to EvoXXLTraffic: Scaling Traffic Forecasting to Sensor-Evolving Networks
item.content:encoded: Existing traffic forecasting benchmarks assume a fixed sensor set, but real road-sensor networks grow continuously as the road network changes year by year. We introduce the XXLTraffic dataset family, which spans up to 27 years of California PeMS and Transport for NSW data. The fixed-sensor subsets of XXLTraffic support extremely long forecasting with multi-year gaps and standard hourly / daily long-horizon forecasting. We extend it to EvoXXLTraffic, a sensor-evolving reorganization that exposes per-year active sensors, yearly traffic-flow matrices, and yearly graph snapshots across nine PeMS districts, with growth ratios ranging from +305% to over +10,000%. We define a yearly streaming forecasting protocol on EvoXXLTraffic in which each calendar year is a continual task, and benchmark a wide range of representative baselines drawn from static spatio-temporal GNNs, naïve online schemes, evolving-graph continual methods, and retrieval / test-time methods. We find that our ultra-large evolutionary dataset better reflects the real world, and many state-of-the-art (SOTA) results no longer work. Our dataset complements existing benchmarks by enabling more realistic forecasting under ultra-long evolutionary road networks.

item.title: GEO-Bench: Benchmarking Ranking Manipulation in Generative Engine Optimization
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly rank products, documents, and recommendations for user queries, which makes manipulating these rankings a growing concern for fairness and information integrity. Research on generative engine optimization (GEO) has produced many manipulation methods, but each is evaluated on its own dataset with its own metrics, so their relative strength and detectability stay unclear. We present GEO-Bench, a benchmark that evaluates GEO ranking-manipulation attacks under one protocol. It unifies black-box prompt-based attacks (TAP, Zero-Shot), white-box gradient-based attacks (STS, RAF, StealthRank), and ten white-hat C-SEO strategies. We score every method on five datasets against a fixed open-weight ranker (Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct), using metrics for both effectiveness (NRG, Success@α, Promote@α) and stealth (keyword violation rate, perplexity ratio). Our evaluation shows that effectiveness and stealth trade off across adversarial attacks, that black-box content rewriting matches or exceeds gradient-based attacks on rank promotion while producing more fluent text and can evade both keyword- and perplexity-based detection on some domains, and that the access model does not predict attack strength. By standardizing datasets, attack implementations, and metrics, GEO-Bench enables the first direct comparison across these attack paradigms and supports the development of detection methods.

item.title: GeneralThinker: Domain-General Reasoning through Likelihood-Guided Answer-Conditioned Optimization
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards improves language model reasoning, but its reliance on domain-specific verifiers, sparse outcome rewards, and coarse-grained credit assignment limits its applicability. We introduce GeneralThinker, an on-policy framework that reformulates reasoning supervision as dense answer-conditioned optimization, enabling response-level evaluation and token-level credit assignment without domain-specific verifiers. GeneralThinker evaluates generated reasoning trajectories using the likelihood of the ground-truth answer and derives token-wise compatibility signals for fine-grained credit assignment. To stabilize optimization, it constrains token-level updates through clipping and direction-preserving modulation. Across 11 benchmarks spanning mathematics, STEM, and general reasoning, GeneralThinker achieves the best average performance. Further analyses show that uncontrolled token-level modulation can destabilize training, whereas controlled modulation makes fine-grained credit assignment consistently effective.

item.title: Unification and Optimization of Robust Supervised Learning
item.content:encoded: The literature has proposed various robust alternatives to empirical risk minimisation to address failure modes such as distribution shift, label noise and finite-sample degeneracies. Examples include distributionally robust optimization, label smoothing, vicinal risk minimization, and Mixup. However, such approaches are typically developed in isolation, forcing practitioners to commit a priori to a single failure mode even when the dominant mode for the task is unclear. To address this, we organize a broad class of existing methods along three common design axes and derive a tractable training procedure that decomposes robust learning into sequential stages (reference distribution enrichment, input-space perturbation, label-space perturbation, and sample-level aggregation), each with a choice of stance (pessimistic, neutral, or optimistic). This results in a unified design space in which joint hyperparameter optimization can compose and configure robustness strategies suited to the task at hand. Across tabular, image, and reward modeling benchmarks, joint hyperparameter optimization is competitive with the best single-method baseline in each setting, offering a reliable default for practitioners who do not know a priori which failure mode dominates their task.

item.title: Human-like in-group bias in instruction-tuned language model agents
item.content:encoded: As autonomous AI agents are deployed in persistent, interacting networks -- coordinating tasks, routing resources, and accumulating reputational histories -- the social dynamics that emerge will determine who receives opportunity and who does not, at scales no human institution can supervise. We ran a controlled multi-agent simulation in which instruction-tuned language model agents interacted across 500 turns under three conditions manipulating group label salience and resource scarcity, across six model families with 20 seeds each. When group labels were visible, we observed in-group trust bias, action homophily, and network assortativity -- all absent when labels were hidden -- a pattern structurally consistent with salience-dependence in human social psychology. This discrimination was invisible to standard action-log audits: bias operated entirely through who received each action, not what actions were chosen, with action-type distributions showing no increase in negative actions across conditions. Per-turn in-group versus out-group differentials of 5 to 16 percentage points were statistically significant for all six models (Wilcoxon signed-rank, all Benjamini-Hochberg-corrected p < 0.001), establishing group-contingent targeting as a robust property of instruction-tuned language models across architectures and training regimes. Compounded through 500 turns of reciprocation, these differentials accumulated into in-group trust biases of +0.014 to +0.100 (d = 0.84-4.52) -- illustrating how modest per-interaction targeting propagates into structural inequality in persistent networks.

item.title: Hallucination Behavior in Multimodal LLMs Across Agricultural Image Interpretation and Generation Tasks
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) are being rapidly adopted in agricultural imaging applications, ranging from crop interpretation to synthetic field image generation. However, these models frequently exhibit hallucinations outputs that appear confident yet deviate from biological or environmental reality potentially leading to misinformed agronomic insights. This study investigates such hallucinations in two complementary directions: image-to-text, where LLMs interpret crop or field imagery to describe conditions such as biotic and abiotic stresses, and text-to-image, where models generate synthetic agricultural scenes based on descriptive prompts. We examine errors involving biological inconsistency, contextual inaccuracy, and agronomic implausibility, evaluating the outputs under domain-informed criteria across multiple imaging modalities. Our analysis identifies recurring hallucination patterns within both interpretive and generative tasks. In image interpretation, LLMs (e.g., Gemma, LLAVA, Qwen, and MiniCPM) achieved modest zero-shot accuracy (63 to 75 percent), whereas few-shot prompting improved performance up to 86.8 percent, exhibiting false detections and missed infections, indicating residual hallucination effects. In text-to-image tasks, advanced models such as GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 Flash generate up to 91 percent biologically inconsistent scenes under relaxed prompt constraints, revealing fundamental weaknesses in current LLMs. This systematic assessment of visual reasoning and generation offers critical insights toward enhancing the reliability and trustworthiness of LLM-based agricultural imaging platforms.

item.title: ReverseMath: Answer Inversion for Scalable and Verifiable Mathematical Problem Generation
item.content:encoded: Mathematical reasoning benchmarks are vital for evaluating large language models (LLMs), but many are static and repeatedly exposed through public evaluation and training pipelines, making it difficult to separate genuine reasoning from memorization. Meanwhile, manually constructing new math problems with reliable answers remains costly. We introduce ReverseMath, a scalable method for generating new math problems through answer inversion. Given a problem and its answer, ReverseMath masks a numerical value in the original problem, treats the original answer as a known condition, and rewrites the problem so that the masked value becomes the new answer. The generated problem reverses the original input-output relation, making its answer known by construction. We study ReverseMath for both evaluation and training. For evaluation, paired original/reversed problems reveal substantial behavioral shifts: models sometimes fail on reversed problems and even incorrectly output the original answer, suggesting memorization-like behavior. For training, ReverseMath provides automatically labeled reversed problems as data augmentation for reinforcement learning (RL). Experiments show that including ReverseMath-generated data improves mathematical reasoning performance across multiple benchmarks, demonstrating its value as both an analysis tool and a scalable source of verifiable training data.

item.title: Towards Unified Vision-Language Models with Incomplete Multi-Modal Inputs
item.content:encoded: Video-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive multi-modal reasoning capabilities across diverse computer vision applications. However, these VLMs are task-specific and assume that both video and language inputs are complete. However, real-world VLM applications might face challenges due to deactivated sensors (e.g., cameras are unavailable due to data privacy), yielding modality-incomplete data and leading to inconsistency between training and testing data. While straightforward incomplete input can boast training generalization-ability and lead to training failure, its potential risks to VLMs regarding safety and trustworthiness have been largely neglected. To this end, we make the first attempt to propose a unified incomplete video-language model to process the incomplete multi-modal inputs. Extensive experimental results show that our method can serve as a plug-and-play module for previous works to improve their performance in various multi-modal tasks.

item.title: No More K-means: Single-Stage Sparse Coding for Efficient Multi-Vector Retrieval
item.content:encoded: Multi-vector retrieval (MVR) models, exemplified by ColBERT, have established new benchmarks in retrieval accuracy by preserving fine-grained token-level interactions. However, this granularity imposes prohibitive storage and retrieval efficiency bottlenecks: to manage the immense memory footprint and computational overhead of billion-scale token vectors, state-of-the-art systems are forced to rely on aggressive dimension reduction and complex clustering (e.g., K-means). This compromise introduces two critical limitations: excessive indexing latency of clustering large-scale corpora and semantic information loss inherent to compression. In this paper, we propose Single-stage Sparse Retrieval (SSR}, a paradigm shift that replaces expensive clustering with efficient sparse coding. Instead of compressing features into low-dimensional dense vectors, we utilize Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) to project token embeddings into a high-dimensional but highly sparse representation. This transformation enables us to bypass vector clustering entirely and leverage inverted indexing for precise, high-throughput retrieval. Extensive experiments on the BEIR benchmark demonstrate that SSR achieves a "trifecta" of improvements: it reduces indexing time by 15x compared to ColBERTv2, halves retrieval latency, and simultaneously improves retrieval performance over leading baselines.

item.title: SIGMA: Bridging Structural and Distributional Gaps for Vision Foundation Model Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) have demonstrated impressive representational capabilities. However, adapting them to downstream tasks via full fine-tuning incurs prohibitive computational and storage overhead. Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) has emerged as a compelling alternative, aiming to achieve performance parity with full fine-tuning at minimal training costs. Nonetheless, applying PEFT to VFMs for dense prediction tasks remains challenging due to the structural and distributional gaps. To bridge these gaps, we propose \textbf{S}cale-\textbf{I}ntegrated \textbf{G}lobal \textbf{M}odulation \textbf{A}dapter (\textbf{SIGMA}), a novel lightweight PEFT method, which consists of two modules: scale-adaptive fusion and semantic modulation. Specifically, the scale-adaptive fusion module is utilized to bridge structural gaps by enhancing the extraction of multi-granularity visual information. Furthermore, SIGMA introduces semantic modulation on the fusion features to perform global feature alignment to further eliminate the distribution gap. This design facilitates unified spatial and distributional adaptation, requiring only 1.72\% trainable parameters relative to the VFM backbone. Comprehensive experiments across various downstream dense tasks and multiple VFM backbones demonstrate that SIGMA achieves consistent and superior performance over state-of-the-art PEFT methods.

item.title: Test Time Training for Supervised Causal Learning
item.content:encoded: Supervised Causal Learning (SCL) has shown promise in causal discovery by framing it as a supervised learning problem. However, it suffers from significant out-of-distribution generalization challenges. We reveal three limitations of previous SCL practices: a significant performance gap between synthetic benchmarks and real-world data, fragility to distribution shifts, and failure in compositional generalization, collectively questioning its real-world applicability. To address this, we propose Test-Time Training for Supervised Causal Learning (TTT-SCL), a novel framework that dynamically generates training sets explicitly aligned with any specific test instance. We demonstrate the correlation between TTT-SCL and score-based methods, and design an efficient module for generating training sets based on the classic scoring function. Experiments on synthetic benchmarks, pseudo-real and real-world datasets demonstrate that TTT-SCL significantly outperforms existing SCL and traditional causal discovery methods.

item.title: Detecting Diffusion-Generated Time Series Under Generator Shift
item.content:encoded: The boundary between real and diffusion-generated time series is becoming increasingly difficult to draw, yet detection in this domain remains underexplored, especially when the generator is unknown. We compare white-box detection, which requires access to the generator, against black-box detection, which operates on the raw signal alone. The white-box approach, a reconstruction-based detector adapted from the image domain, works well in in-distribution but breaks down under generator shift: reconstruction-based detection in images succeeds because large generic generators provide a near-universal reconstruction prior, and no analogous generator exists for time series. In contrast, a simple off-the-shelf classifier used as a black-box detector performs remarkably well, achieving an average F1 of 79.2, a 22.1% relative improvement over the white-box approach, and a TPR@1%FPR of 57.2. Diffusion-generated time series detection is therefore not a direct transfer of the image domain problem. This work provides the first systematic exploration of white-box and black-box detection for diffusion-generated time series. We close by identifying several open and promising directions.

item.title: CSULoRA: Closest Safe Update Low-Rank Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Low-rank adaptation has become a standard method for parameter-efficient fine-tuning of large language models, but even small amounts of unsafe or adversarial fine-tuning data can substantially weaken the safety behavior of aligned models. Existing safety-preserving LoRA methods often rely on hard interventions such as projection, pruning, thresholding, or additional training objectives. While these methods can suppress unsafe update directions, they may also remove task-relevant information or require extra tuning. We introduce CSULoRA, a post-hoc method for correcting trained LoRA adapters through closest safe update estimation. CSULoRA estimates a safety-aligned subspace from the weight displacement between a safety-aligned model and its corresponding base checkpoint. It then decomposes each LoRA update into fully aligned, partially aligned, and off-subspace components. Instead of discarding components outside the estimated safety subspace, CSULoRA solves a closed-form penalized minimum-change problem that preserves the fully aligned component while smoothly attenuating potentially unsafe directions according to their relative energy. In adversarial fine-tuning experiments, CSULoRA substantially reduces attack success rate while preserving most of the utility gains obtained from standard LoRA fine-tuning.

item.title: EMAG: Differentiable 4D Gaussian Mixture Splatting for EEG Spatial Super-Resolution
item.content:encoded: High-density electroencephalography (HD-EEG) enables fine-grained measurement of cortical activity but requires expensive hardware and lengthy setup times, limiting its clinical and research accessibility. We propose EMAG (EEG Mixture of Anisotropic Gaussians), a differentiable framework that reconstructs HD-EEG signals from a sparse subset of low-density (LD) electrodes by representing brain electrical sources as a mixture of anisotropic 4D space-time Gaussians. EMAG places a mixture of multiple Gaussians at each point of a spherical brain grid, each parameterized by a full 4 x 4 precision matrix, enabling anisotropic spatial spreads and explicit coupling between spatial and temporal dimensions. The forward model renders scalp EEG via differentiable Gaussian field contributions at electrode locations, enabling end-to-end training without explicit source localization supervision. We evaluate EMAG on three public EEG benchmarks (Localize-MI, SEED, and SEED-IV) at super-resolution factors of 2x through 8/16x. EMAG outperforms the current state-of-the-art EEG super-resolution method at most super-resolution factors on three standard benchmarks (Localize-MI, SEED, SEED-IV). The explicit Gaussian parameterization further enables direct visualization and interpretability of learned brain source configurations, potentially opening avenues for clinical and neuroscientific applications, such as source localization or biomarker discovery.

item.title: 无需微调的扩散模型Talking Face生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需微调的Talking Face生成框架，提升唇形同步与视觉质量。



摘要： 本文针对现有扩散模型在Talking Face生成中需要任务特定微调和大规模数据集的问题，提出了一种无需微调的生成方法。该方法直接利用Stable Diffusion和IP-Adapter的预训练权重，通过结构分解、动态嵌入控制和噪声检测等组件，有效解决身份漂移、同步误差和时间不稳定等问题。实验表明，该方法在唇形同步精度和视觉质量上均优于现有最佳方法。

item.title: Prosecution Decision Prediction: 填补刑事法律AI评估的空白
item.content:encoded:
提出PDP任务以补充LJP在刑事起诉决策中的不足。



摘要： 本文提出Prosecution Decision Prediction (PDP)，作为首个围绕检察机关审查的法律AI任务，旨在填补传统Legal Judgment Prediction (LJP) 在评估刑事追诉责任方面的盲点。PDP通过分类案件为起诉或三种非起诉决定，反映AI在证据评估、法律归类和价值判断方面的能力。研究构建了包含4,630个真实中国检察决定的PDP-Bench数据集，实验表明当前最先进的LLM在PDP任务上的表现显著低于LJP，主流增强方法无法缩小差距，且简单的结果奖励无法实现有效的PDP区分。

item.title: 多智能体交互视频生成的世界模型
item.content:encoded:
提出一种支持多智能体交互的生成世界模型，提升视频质量和一致性。



摘要： 本文提出一种用于交互式视频生成的多智能体世界模型。该模型引入Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding，通过将智能体表示为旋转角度空间中的正则单纯形顶点，实现智能体身份的可扩展表示。同时，采用Sparse Hub Attention机制，降低跨智能体注意力计算成本。此外，通过扩散模型的因果学生网络实现实时生成，支持24帧/秒的响应速度。实验表明，该模型在多人虚拟环境中提升了视频质量、动作可控性和智能体间一致性，且无需额外训练即可适应不同数量的玩家。

item.title: 基于有限数据的岭回归正则化强度优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种计算最优正则化强度的方法，适用于有限数据集。



摘要： 本文研究在有限数据样本下，具有有界协方差和线性预测目标的L²正则化线性回归问题。作者提出了一种迭代算法，用于在固定数据集设定下从生成参数中数值计算最优正则化强度，并证明了在有限噪声水平下的收敛性。实验结果表明，该方法结合样本参数估计，在不同样本大小、维度比和噪声水平下，能够实现接近最优的随机数据泛化性能，其计算成本在欠参数化情况下等同于一次岭回归，在过参数化情况下为两次。

item.title: 基于轨迹信息的大型语言模型拒绝学习方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种动态重加权的拒绝学习方法，提升模型一致性与准确性。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLM）的拒绝学习方法，引入轨迹信息进行优势重加权，以动态调整拒绝奖励。该方法在组相对策略优化（GRPO）训练中利用多条轨迹作为自然拒绝信号，通过奖励信号探索知识边界并鼓励一致性。实验表明，该方法在AbstentionBench基准测试中表现优异，在多个类别中达到最佳性能，同时保持了基线准确率。

item.title: 长视频生成的多维度诊断评估框架DirectorBench
item.content:encoded:
DirectorBench用于评估长视频生成的质量与一致性。



摘要： 文章介绍了DirectorBench，这是一个针对长视频生成的个性化多智能体诊断基准。该基准从脚本、视觉、音频、跨模态和稳定性五个维度，通过80项结构化元数据、7个用户档案和40个检查点标准进行评估。不同于单一评分，DirectorBench能够定位检查点级别的瓶颈，并支持基于用户档案的评估。实验表明，不同工作流在转换质量和用户需求满足度方面存在显著差异，且DirectorBench能有效反映人类评价中的质量差异。

item.title: 长期决策中累积损伤对策略梯度方法的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
研究长期决策中累积损伤对策略梯度方法的影响及改进方案。



摘要： 本文探讨了在存在累积损伤的长期决策问题中，策略梯度方法所面临的两种独立失败模式：完成性（达到最终时间点）和最优性（在完成前提下达到动态规划最优）。研究提出了一种分解方法，并通过PPO算法进行实验验证。结果表明，仅提供时间范围访问权限会降低完成率，而结合动作空间限制与时间范围访问则能提高完成率但存在最优性差距。研究在两个不同领域环境中测试了四个可验证预测，结果一致且符合理论预期。

item.title: 基于CorVer的强化学习提升知识密集型问答准确性
item.content:encoded:
CorVer通过语料库统计提升问答系统事实准确性。



摘要： 本文探讨了在知识密集型问答任务中应用强化学习时面临的奖励设计难题。传统响应级奖励无法区分推理过程中的正确与错误陈述，而句子级奖励虽更精细，但依赖于昂贵且不可靠的验证工具。为此，作者提出CorVer，一种基于维基百科共现统计的轻量级奖励机制，能够高效提供句子级反馈并映射到词级优势。实验显示，CorVer在多个模型和基准测试中均优于基线，平均提升4.1个百分点，并在训练速度上具有显著优势。

item.title: 面向工业自动化周期行为的联邦学习时间序列异常检测数据集研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一个包含周期行为的MTSAD数据集以解决FL中的挑战。



摘要： 本文针对联邦学习（FL）在多变量时间序列异常检测（MTSAD）中的应用，指出现有数据集在规模、标签准确性及缺陷规避方面存在不足。同时，当前研究对工业自动化中常见的周期性行为在MTSAD中的作用探讨不足。为此，本文提出一个基于离散自动化过程重复特性的周期动态数据集，并在该数据集和公开基准数据集上评估了多种MTSAD方法，旨在填补研究空白并推动该领域发展。

item.title: 构建全生命周期的网络欺凌治理框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种全面、持续的网络欺凌治理框架。



摘要： 本文探讨了社交媒体平台中网络欺凌和有害言论的传播问题，指出当前内容审核主要依赖于静态检测，忽视了用户行为动态和有害事件的扩散机制。为此，文章提出了一种全生命周期的治理框架，涵盖内容识别、用户行为建模、扩散预警和干预治理四个阶段，并回顾了相关数据集与评估方法，讨论了多模态、可解释性、算法公平性及生成式AI的双重风险等挑战，为构建更安全的数字生态提供研究方向。

item.title: 3D-aware框架提升语义对应估计
item.content:encoded:
3D-aware框架提升语义对应估计效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种3D-aware的后训练框架，通过结合3D基础模型的先验知识，改进基于2D图像特征的语义对应估计。该方法利用SAM3D估计物体几何和姿态，并通过渲染与比较优化姿态，生成基于重建几何的PartField描述符。这些几何感知特征补充了DINO和Stable Diffusion特征，同时利用测地距离过滤候选对应关系，从而在无需人工几何监督的情况下提升语义对应性能。实验表明该方法优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于神经过程的光变曲线重建方法在LSST时代的应用
item.content:encoded:
神经过程方法提升光变曲线重建效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于神经过程的光变曲线重建方法，结合高斯过程的概率框架与深度学习的可扩展性。该方法通过元学习处理多种模拟瞬态数据，在15种瞬态类别上优于所有基准模型，包括高斯过程和神经网络。其优势在于能同时重建多个波段，速度比现有方法快四数量级，适用于LSST的实时警报流。该方法避免了传统神经网络的过度自信和高斯过程的过度保守，提供准确且校准良好的不确定性估计，为 Rubin 时代的时间天体物理研究奠定基础。

item.title: 基于状态感知神经编解码器的高保真数据包捕获生成
item.content:encoded:
TraceCodec实现高保真数据包捕获生成，提升协议验证与安全分析能力。



摘要： 本文提出TraceCodec，一种用于生成高保真数据包捕获（PCAP）的状态感知神经编解码器。传统方法直接解码为原始数据包字段，导致行为选择与协议结果纠缠，依赖后期修复。TraceCodec将每个数据包转化为带时间戳的包动作，并引入显式流槽和传输线索，学习连续的每包潜在表示。该方法通过确定性编译器将动作还原为PCAP，有效管理端点分配、TCP状态、合法性约束和包渲染。实验表明，TraceCodec在CICIDS2017数据集上与真实数据匹配度高达99.97%，显著优于原始字段解码方法。研究揭示了TraceCodec在保持TCP状态转移和多流交错方面的优势，为高保真数据包生成提供了新基础。

item.title: 无需实例标注的肾脏功能单元分割方法 MORI-Seg
item.content:encoded:
MORI-Seg 实现无实例标注的肾脏功能单元分割，提升形态定量分析精度。



摘要： 本文提出 MORI-Seg，一种无需实例级标注的深度学习分割框架，用于肾脏功能单元的实例级量化分析。传统方法依赖语义分割标注，难以实现可靠的实例分离，而现有方法成本高、效果差。MORI-Seg 通过联合建模对象中心距离场和边界带表示，从语义掩码中学习形态感知的几何表示，并利用类别条件特征解耦模块增强实例内一致性与实例间分离性。实验表明，该方法在实例分离精度和形态定量分析方面优于传统后处理和语义到实例学习方法。官方代码已开源。

item.title: 基于多模态对比微调的TRACER方法提升模型分布外鲁棒性
item.content:encoded:
TRACER方法提升多模态模型的分布外鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文针对预训练多模态模型微调过程中出现的灾难性遗忘问题，提出了一种理论框架和新的微调方法TRACER。该方法通过对比学习与加权移动平均教师指导的多视角蒸馏相结合，有效提升了模型在分布外数据上的鲁棒性。研究发现，标准的指数移动平均教师存在崩溃问题，而加权移动平均教师能够保持稳定的正则化力并实现无偏收敛。实验表明，TRACER在多个骨干网络上均表现出一致的性能提升。

item.title: 基于反思的在线策略自蒸馏提升大语言模型推理性能
item.content:encoded:
ROSD通过反思引导的错误定位蒸馏提升LLM推理表现。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Reflective On-policy Self-Distillation (ROSD) 的新方法，旨在解决传统在线策略自蒸馏（OPSD）在领域内推理效果有限、跨领域泛化能力差的问题。ROSD通过引入自我反思机制，识别并定位推理过程中的错误段落，从而实现有针对性的修正，避免覆盖有效的推理前缀和过度拟合。实验表明，ROSD在多个领域内的推理基准测试中表现优于标准OPSD，并显著提升了跨领域泛化能力。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于视觉的可通行性估计框架ViTA研究
item.content:encoded:
ViTA提升户外环境可通行性估计可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出ViTA框架，通过注入任务特定知识、处理标注歧义和融合语义与几何信息，提升视觉基础模型在非结构化户外环境中的可通行性估计性能。该方法在多个真实场景数据集上表现出色，实现了更高的IoU和精确度，并有效减少误报。

item.title: SKILD：统一图像生成与超分辨率的尺度不变扩散模型
item.content:encoded:
SKILD实现图像生成与超分辨率的统一，无需任务特定架构。



摘要： 本文提出SKILD，一种基于尺度不变性的图像学习扩散模型，能够统一图像生成和连续超分辨率任务。通过将尺度作为扩散动态的显式坐标，SKILD在自然图像和物理系统中表现出色，仅需调整起始时间步即可完成不同任务，无需额外架构或训练。实验结果显示，SKILD在CIFAR-10上取得FID 2.65和Inception Score 9.63，在ImageNet上实现2倍至8倍超分辨率，并优于条件模型。此外，它还能准确重建关键Ising模型。

item.title: 依存诱导的 headedness 对句法分词的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
依存诱导的 headedness 并不提升句法分词性能。



摘要： 本文重新探讨了标点敏感的树二分法在句法分析中的应用，研究是否依赖于依存关系的 headedness 能够提升二分法的监督效果。尽管学习到的 headedness 在内在头预测任务中优于规则方法，但在去二分后并未带来稳定的解析性能提升。特别是在标点敏感的评估中，学习到的 headedness 表现不如规则二分法，尽管在 CTB 数据集上有小幅整体提升。跨语料库迁移实验也显示出类似的不稳定性。这表明，语言学上合理的 headedness 并不一定在作为二分控制信号时是最优的。研究得出结论：更好的头预测并不意味着更好的标点敏感句法分析。

item.title: NS3：一种高效的联合排名框架用于复杂查询回答
item.content:encoded:
NS3提升复杂查询的联合排名性能，适用于多变量知识图谱查询。



摘要： 本文提出NS3（Neural Scalable Symbolic Search），一种用于处理存在性一阶查询（EFO_k）的高效框架。该方法通过合并自由变量为超节点，并在动态预算下进行域缩减，避免直接枚举所有可能的实体组合，从而有效解决高维查询的计算难题。实验表明，NS3在三个标准知识图谱数据集上显著提升了联合排名性能，同时保持了良好的边际准确性。研究还扩展了现有EFO_1数据集至k=3，为多变量查询评估提供了新的基准。

item.title: DriveWAM：基于视频扩散Transformer的自动驾驶世界-动作模型
item.content:encoded:
DriveWAM通过视频扩散Transformer实现自动驾驶规划，提升长期轨迹生成能力。



摘要： 本文提出DriveWAM，一种基于预训练视频扩散Transformer的自动驾驶世界-动作模型。该模型将视频与动作流整合为统一的时间序列，在联合流匹配目标下进行训练，保留了预训练视频生成架构的同时，适应了动作生成任务。研究引入场景演化驾驶引导，利用冻结的视觉语言模型提供语义意图指导视频-动作生成。此外，通过选择性KV记忆机制，保持长期轨迹生成的稳定性。实验表明，DriveWAM在NAVSIM和PhysicalAI-Autonomous-Vehicles基准上表现优异，并验证了世界-动作建模在端到端自动驾驶中的扩展潜力。

item.title: 基于能量感知的单次像素级分布外检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Energy-Aware NECO方法提升移动机器人语义分割的不确定性估计。



摘要： 本文提出Energy-Aware NECO，一种用于语义分割的单次像素级分布外（OOD）检测方法。该方法结合解码器特征计算的中心NECO几何比值与基于logit的能量分数，并通过凸组合进行融合。在miniMUAD数据集上的实验表明，该混合得分在AUROC指标上达到0.8539，优于NECO-only、Energy-only和预测熵基线方法。结果还显示该方法在保持单次推理效率的同时提升了整体性能。

item.title: 基于解耦架构的风力涡轮机叶片缺陷检测与报告生成系统
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种解耦式工业检测系统，提升缺陷定位与报告生成效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种用于风力涡轮机叶片检测的解耦式边缘部署系统，包含三个模块：YOLO26-x-obb检测器用于缺陷定位，Bridge模块将检测结果映射为结构化空间标记，Brain模块则基于微调的4位量化Qwen-2.5-1.5B模型生成结构化JSON报告。通过检索增强微调（RAFT）确保建议的准确性。实验表明，该系统在BLEU-4、HR和专家评分等指标上优于单一视觉-语言模型，且在相同检测证据下生成的报告质量优于大型通用API模型，具有较高的推理效率。

item.title: HEAL：一种新型的跨层去中心化学习框架
item.content:encoded:
HEAL结合多种去中心化方法，提升模型鲁棒性与隐私保护。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为HEAL的新型去中心化学习框架，它结合了联邦学习、Gossip学习和流行病学习的优势，通过优化的对等网络结构实现动态聚合节点选择。HEAL在无故障环境下表现与联邦学习相当，同时具备完全去中心化和容错能力。在存在故障和节点变动的环境中，HEAL优于Gossip和Epidemic Learning。该框架利用Elevator算法，提高了系统的自组织和自愈能力，增强了隐私保护和系统稳定性。

item.title: 个性化联邦学习算法性能比较研究
item.content:encoded:
对比分析七种PFL算法在多个数据集上的表现。



摘要： 本文对七种个性化联邦学习（PFL）算法在MNIST、SignMNIST和Digit5三个数据集上进行了全面比较分析，旨在评估其在模式识别任务中的性能。通过Accuracy、Precision、Recall和F1 Score等指标进行评估，研究发现APPLE、FedGC和FedProto表现优异，展现出较高的准确性和适应性。同时，文章还深入探讨了各算法的工作机制、优势与局限，指出不同算法在特定场景下的适用性，并强调通过迭代优化可进一步提升效果。

item.title: CorPipe 26：多语言共指消解任务的优胜方案
item.content:encoded:
CorPipe 26在CRAC 2026多语言共指消解任务中表现优异。



摘要： 本文介绍了CorPipe 26，这是提交给CRAC 2026多语言共指消解共享任务的优胜方案。该任务第五次举办，重点比较生成式大语言模型与专业系统，并新增了5个数据集和2种语言。CorPipe 26是CorPipe 25的改进版本，引入了一种新变体，在一个模型中同时预测空节点、提及和共指链接。该系统在LLM赛道上比其他提交高出2.8个百分点，在无约束赛道上高出9.5个百分点。此外，还进行了多种模型规模、空节点预测方法和跨语言零样本评估的消融实验。源代码和训练模型已公开在GitHub上。

item.title: 离散扩散模型中随机性对采样效率与质量的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
研究随机性对扩散模型采样效率与质量的影响，提出新算法提升性能。



摘要： 本文系统研究了马尔可夫转移中的随机性对扩散模型采样效率和样本质量的权衡影响。研究发现，高度确定性的转移虽然收敛速度快，但容易积累误差；而更随机的转移虽然收敛较慢，但能获得更高的样本质量。通过信息论分析，揭示了冗余转移带来的纠错机制，并提出一种名为DCRS的新推理算法，通过在正向和反向扩散过程之间交替注入可控随机性，显著提升了低函数评估次数下的采样效率。实验表明，在图像数据集上，DCRS相比标准采样器可减少10倍的采样步骤，同时保持高质量样本；在语言基准测试中，其表现则依赖于噪声过程和采样方法。

item.title: GoBOED：一种面向决策目标的贝叶斯最优实验设计方法
item.content:encoded:
GoBOED优化实验设计以提升决策质量，而非单纯减少参数不确定性。



摘要： 本文提出GoBOED，一种基于决策目标的贝叶斯最优实验设计框架。与传统方法不同，GoBOED不追求最大化信息增益，而是直接优化实验设计以提升下游决策效果。该方法结合变分后验近似和可微凸决策层，实现基于梯度的决策导向优化。理论分析表明，GoBOED对与决策无关的参数方向不敏感，从而在更广泛的实验设计中保持相同决策质量。实验结果表明，GoBOED在源定位、疫情管理和药代动力学控制等任务中均能生成更符合决策目标的实验设计，并揭示了近优设计窗口比传统方法预测的更大。

item.title: LLM推理机制与注意力头功能分析
item.content:encoded:
研究LLM如何通过注意力机制理解推理步骤和算法。



摘要： 本研究探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在少量示例下如何理解和执行抽象的推理步骤和算法。通过符号辅助的思维链（CoT）框架，研究人员将推理步骤与对应的标记置信度进行对齐，并发现推理过程中的关键位置通常具有较低的置信度。进一步采用因果中介分析方法识别出负责这些模式的注意力头。研究还表明，LLMs通过专门的注意力头获取事实性和规则性信息，而高层注意力头则用于整合信息并形成全局推理策略，如图遍历算法。

item.title: 提升大语言模型对语义中性提示扰动的鲁棒性方法
item.content:encoded:
通过微调提升大语言模型对语义相似但文本不同的提示的鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文探讨了如何在不重新训练整个模型的情况下提高大语言模型（LLMs）对语义中性提示扰动的鲁棒性。研究表明，提示变化可能显著影响模型性能。研究提出了一种基于偏差消除的微调方法，理论分析表明神经网络模块输出的系统性偏移是影响鲁棒性的关键因素。实验验证了该方法在增强模型鲁棒性和应对随机提示扰动方面的有效性。

item.title: The Abstraction Gap in Vision-Language Causal Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Vision-language models (VLMs) generate fluent causal explanations, but current evaluations cannot distinguish linguistic plausibility from faithful causal reasoning. We introduce a dual-probe methodology that isolates these properties. The Text-Only Probe measures linguistic quality. The Chain-Text Probe requires models to first generate explicit causal chains. The Abstraction Gap (AG) metric quantifies the normalized performance difference. Evaluating eight VLMs on CAGE (Causal Abstraction Gap Evaluation), a benchmark of 49,500 questions across 5,500 images spanning Pearl's causal hierarchy, we find seven models exhibit AG exceeding 0.50 with text scores of 6--8 but chain scores below 2.5. Fine-tuning on 45,000 chain-annotated examples fails to close the gap. However, one model achieves near-zero AG. The capability exists within current VLM architectures and depends on pretraining and architectural choices. CAGE provides a diagnostic tool for assessing faithful causal reasoning in VLMs.

item.title: Bridging the Detection-to-Abstention Gap in Reasoning Models under Insufficient Information
item.content:encoded: We highlight a failure mode of large reasoning models on questions with insufficient information: models may recognize that a problem is under-specified, yet still continue reasoning and produce unsupported final answers instead of abstaining. We formalize this mismatch as the detection-to-abstention gap, where detected insufficiency fails to translate into final abstention. This gap is especially concerning in high-risk domains such as medical AI, where answers based on incomplete evidence can be more harmful than refusal. To close this gap, we propose Judge-Then-Solve (JTS), a trajectory-level reasoning-control framework that trains models to make an explicit answerability commitment before solution generation. Rather than treating abstention as a final-answer style, JTS casts it as a control decision: the model either proceeds to solve or terminates early based on its answerability judgment. We instantiate this policy through supervised warm-up and missing-premise reinforcement learning with consistency and length-shaping rewards. Experiments on dense and MoE reasoning models show that JTS substantially improves reliable abstention across datasets and pushes Abstention@Detection (A@D) to near-saturation, indicating that models not only detect missing information but also act on that detection. By terminating unanswerable trajectories immediately after the answerability judgment, JTS reduces unnecessary reasoning and improves inference efficiency when continued deliberation would amplify unsupported assumptions. We also observe that missing-premise training can alter reasoning behavior on difficult but answerable problems, reducing unproductive self-reflection. These results suggest that abstention under insufficient information is a key form of reasoning control for deploying reasoning models safely and efficiently.

item.title: Defending LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems Against Cooperative Attacks with Sentence-Level Rectification
item.content:encoded: Recent years have witnessed the rapid development of Large Language Model-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS), which excel at collaborative decision-making and complex problem-solving. However, malicious agents in MAS may inject misinformation to mislead other agents and disrupt system performance, giving rise to a new research direction that focuses on attack mechanisms and defense strategies in MAS. Prior studies largely assume malicious agents act independently and investigate the corresponding defense strategies. However, we argue that malicious agents may exhibit collaborative behaviors, enabling more effective attacks through internal information exchange. In this paper, we propose an adaptive cooperative attack framework, where malicious agents autonomously coordinate and dynamically adjust their attack strategies through multi-round interactions. Furthermore, we introduce Sentence-Level Trustworthiness Analysis and Rectification (STAR), a defense framework that identifies and rectifies misleading information at the sentence level within agent communications. Our experiments show that cooperative attacks lead to a significantly larger degradation in task success rate than independent attacks, resulting in a relative drop of 5.34\%. Meanwhile, STAR effectively mitigates both cooperative and independent threats and improves task success rate by an average of 36.76\%. The code is available at https://github.com/smoooom/STAR.

item.title: Data-driven sparse identification of governing PDEs via knockoff filters and multi-criteria trade-offs
item.content:encoded: We propose KO-PDE-IDENT, a data-driven framework for identifying parsimonious partial differential equations (PDEs) with false discovery rate (FDR) control. PDE discovery from noisy observations is often hindered by extreme multicollinearity among candidate terms, which causes typical sparse-regression methods to select spurious terms. To address this problem, KO-PDE-IDENT initially mines a support set of potential candidate terms via model-X knockoff filters with finite-sample FDR control, then refines and ranks the surviving PDE alternatives. The framework integrates three components. First, knockoff feature statistics are constructed by coupling $\ell_{0}$-constrained adaptive best-subset selection with SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), yielding an effective and computationally efficient difference statistic. Second, a recursive feature elimination (RFE) procedure removes terms whose marginal contributions are dispensable and assesses statistical necessity through knockoff-perturbed hypothesis testing. Third, the final model selection is formulated as a multi-criteria decision-making (MCDM) problem, where the optimal governing equation is the alternative that best balances a wide range of criteria such as predictive accuracy, model complexity and coefficient uncertainty. We validate KO-PDE-IDENT on five canonical PDEs under severe noise corruption. Empirical results show that our framework can exactly recover the true PDE structure, eliminating false discoveries while retaining all true underlying terms, with low coefficient estimation error.

item.title: SP-MoMamba: Superpixel-driven Mixture of State Space Experts for Efficient Image Super-Resolution
item.content:encoded: State space models (SSMs) have emerged as a powerful paradigm for efficient single-image super-resolution (SR) due to their linear complexity and long-range modeling capabilities. However, existing Mamba-based methods typically rely on data-agnostic rigid scanning, which reshapes 2D images into 1D sequences over a fixed grid, inevitably disrupting spatial-semantic topology and introducing artifacts. Inspired by the \textbf{Gestalt perceptual grouping theory}, we propose \textbf{SP-MoMamba}, a superpixel-driven mixture of state space experts designed for content-aware SR. Our core idea is to transform the traditional rigid scanning into a \textbf{semantic-level interaction} by treating superpixels as fundamental units. Specifically, we introduce the \textbf{Superpixel-driven State Space Model (SP-SSM)}, which compresses semantically homogeneous regions into high-order tokens to preserve global topological consistency. To address the conflict between fixed scanning scales and diverse semantic granularities, we develop the \textbf{Multi-Scale Superpixel Mixture of State Space Experts (MSS-MoE)}. This module utilizes a dynamic routing mechanism to adaptively assign scale-specific experts, effectively capturing multi-scale textures while reducing computational redundancy. Furthermore, to prevent the loss of high-frequency details during global abstraction, we introduce a \textbf{Local Spatial Modulation Expert (LSME)} to complement the global modeling, ensuring a precise reconstruction of sharp edges and fine structures. Extensive experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that SP-MoMamba achieves superior reconstruction fidelity and a more favorable efficiency-performance trade-off compared to state-of-the-art efficient SR methods.

item.title: Feedforward 3D Editing Learns from Semantic-Part Transformation
item.content:encoded: 3D editing is a fundamental capability for scalable 3D content creation. While image editing has rapidly evolved toward large-scale feedforward generative paradigms, 3D AI generation remains dominated by training-free editing pipelines. A central challenge of feedforward 3D editing lies in the lack of high-quality paired supervision. Editable 3D assets require simultaneous preservation of geometry, multi-view consistency, structural coherence, and localized edit controllability. Existing 3D editing datasets often rely on independently generated assets, image-mediated reconstruction or narrow edit taxonomies, leading to inaccurate localization, weak preservation, blurred edit boundaries, and limited semantic consistency. In this work, we introduce a new perspective: scalable feedforward 3D editing should be learned from semantic-part transformations. Based on this insight, we propose Pxform, a high-quality 3D editing dataset with over 100K consistent before/after editing pairs across seven edit types. Instead of treating objects as unstructured shapes, our pipeline grounds edits directly in semantic 3D parts. Built upon Pxform, we further propose PartFlow, a feedforward 3D editing network that injects source-aware latent control into pretrained 3D generative priors. PartFlow introduces mask-aware velocity preservation and render-space consistency supervision to jointly improve edit fidelity and source preservation, while requiring no 3D edit mask during inference. Extensive experiments demonstrate that high-quality semantic-part supervision substantially improves scalable 3D editing, enabling PartFlow to achieve state-of-the-art performance on both geometric and appearance editing benchmarks.

item.title: AgentSociety: Incentivizing Agentic Social Intelligence
item.content:encoded: The success of deployed agents relies on their ability to handle open-ended user requests using their inherent capabilities, not only in solving requests directly but also in effectively leveraging inter-agent communication channels and feedback signals over time. This requires a multi-agent environment where agents can operate autonomously, strategically communicate, behave collaboratively and be driven by economic incentives, much like humans in society. Towards this vision, we propose $\mathtt{AgentSociety}$, a mechanism that enables decentralized agentic collaboration grounded in liquid democracy and information diffusion from social choice theory. We show that $\mathtt{AgentSociety}$ provides an environment for agents to make autonomous decisions utilizing their local context to maximize their utility while achieving collective outcomes through incentivized collaboration. Specifically, we prove that delegation to more competent neighbor agents is incentive compatible and naturally generates multi-agent routing path by consensus. Additionally, our mechanism incentivizes agents to selectively disclose information to their neighbor agents when doing so aligns with their self-interest, so as to garner influence. We characterize the Nash equilibrium showing that agent payoffs are reflective of their marginal contributions. We compare and benchmark strategy profiles adopted by open and proprietary state-of-the-art language models deployed in $\mathtt{AgentSociety}$ against best response. Finally, we evaluate collaborative performance from consensus-based routing among self-interested heterogeneous agents in $\mathtt{AgentSociety}$ on real-world datasets.

item.title: Self-Consistency via Marginal Sharpening
item.content:encoded: Inference-time sampling can elicit strong reasoning abilities from language models without additional training. Existing power-sampling methods do so by sharpening the distribution over full generated outputs, favoring completions that are individually likely under the model. We argue that this is the wrong object to target for reasoning: a completion entangles a reasoning trace with a final answer, whereas what matters is whether an answer is supported by many plausible reasoning paths. We therefore shift the target from the full-output distribution to the sharpened answer marginal, making self-consistency an inference-time objective rather than a post-hoc voting criterion. Surprisingly, this marginal target admits an efficient approximation: we propose a simple, purely autoregressive parallel sampling algorithm that approximately samples from the sharpened answer marginal, eliciting stronger performance than standard power sampling on mathematics and coding benchmarks while being orders of magnitude faster.

item.title: BitTP: The Lightweight Trajectory Prediction Model with BitLLM for Edge-Devices
item.content:encoded: Trajectory prediction is a fundamental task for autonomous systems, requiring complex reasoning about multi-agent interactions and intents. Large language models (LLMs) have recently been adopted for this task, as they provide strong contextual reasoning and interpretable, language-based trajectory representations. However, these LLM-based predictors are extremely memory- and compute-intensive, making them difficult to deploy on resource-constrained edge devices such as on-board computers in autonomous robots. To bridge this gap, we propose BitTP, which converts an LLM-based trajectory predictor into a lightweight bitlinear architecture. We demonstrate that weight-only quantization to 1.58-bit (BitTP-Weight) is optimal. Crucially, activations must remain in full precision, as quantizing them leads to severe degradation and instability in spatio-temporal reasoning. Empirically, BitTP-Weight not only preserves but improves prediction quality over the full-precision (BF16) LLM baseline, reducing ADE by 14.29% and FDE by 20.97% on average, while simultaneously reducing memory usage and inference latency relative to other quantization methods. These results demonstrate that carefully designed quantization acts as an effective regularizer, enabling the practical deployment of sophisticated LLM-based reasoning on edge devices. Code is available at: https://github.com/MintCat98/BitTP.

item.title: T-GINEE: A Tensor-Based Multilayer Graph Representation Learning
item.content:encoded: Traditional network analysis focuses on single-layer networks, real-world systems often form multilayer networks with multiple relationship types. However, existing methods typically fail to capture complex inter-layer dependencies by treating layers independently or aggregating them. To address this, we propose T-GINEE (Tensor-Based Generalized Multilayer-graph Estimating Equation), a statistical regularization framework combining tensor-based generalized estimating equations with task-specific loss to model cross-network correlations explicitly. Key innovations include: (1) CP tensor decomposition capturing structural dependencies via shared latent factors; (2) a generalized estimating equation framework modeling inter-layer correlations through working covariance matrices; and (3) a flexible link function accommodating characteristics like sparsity. Our theoretical analysis establishes consistency and asymptotic normality under mild conditions. Extensive experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets validate T-GINEE's effectiveness for multilayer network analysis.

item.title: SAM-Enhanced Segmentation on Road Datasets: Balancing Critical Classes in Autonomous Driving
item.content:encoded: Dense semantic segmentation is essential for autonomous driving, yet many multi-modal datasets lack pixel-level annotations. The Zenseact Open Dataset (ZOD) provides rich multi-sensor data but only bounding-box labels, limiting its use for segmentation research. Our primary contribution is a Segment Anything Model (SAM)-based annotation pipeline that produces dense, pixel-level annotations for ZOD by converting bounding boxes into semantic masks. In this pilot study, we process over 100,000 frames and manually curate a 2,300-frame subset (36% acceptance rate) to establish a reliable baseline. Using these annotations, we evaluate transformer-based CLFT and CNN-based DeepLabV3+ architectures across diverse weather conditions, achieving up to 48.1% mIoU with CLFT-Hybrid. To address extreme class imbalance, where pedestrians, cyclists, and signs constitute less than 1% of pixels, we explore specialized models targeting rare classes. We further validate the pipeline on the Iseauto autonomous-vehicle platform, achieving 77.5% mIoU, and show that SAM-derived representations transfer effectively across sensor configurations via bidirectional transfer learning. All code and annotations are released to support reproducible research.

item.title: WhoSaidIt: Human-LLM Collaborative Annotation for Text-Based Multilingual Speaker-Attribute Classification
item.content:encoded: Annotating speaker attributes from text is inherently ambiguous, particularly in multilingual settings where demographic and social cues are implicit and culturally variable. We propose a human-large language model (LLM) collaborative re-annotation framework for stabilizing multilingual speaker-attribute labels under practical resource constraints. Starting from a noisy corpus, we use LLMs to surface recurring annotation rationales through iterative interaction with experts, and apply disagreement-focused sampling for targeted re-annotation. Using this framework, we construct WhoSaidIt, a multilingual dataset covering nine speaker-attribute labels. We quantify divergence between original and revised annotations, benchmark recent LLMs, and analyze the effect of explicit rationales on model behavior. Our results reveal substantial cross-lingual differences in annotation decisions and demonstrate both the strengths and limitations of LLMs in speaker-attribute classification.

item.title: MOOSE-Copilot: A Web-Based Interactive Assistant for Unified Exploratory and Fine-Grained Scientific Hypothesis Discovery
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) show remarkable potential in scientific hypothesis discovery. However, existing approaches face two critical limitations: they treat divergent exploratory ideation and convergent fine-grained refinement as isolated tasks, and they operate autonomously with little to no human guidance. We present MOOSE-Copilot, the first unified framework to bridge this abstraction gap through a formalized human-AI interaction (HAII) protocol. Our system empowers scientists to steer the generative process via three explicit signals: initial blueprints, inter-stage routing, and regenerative feedback. Quantitative evaluations demonstrate that injecting these structured expert signals significantly outperforms purely autonomous baselines, establishing a performance ceiling under oracle guidance. Furthermore, to democratize this paradigm, we develop an intuitive web-based interface featuring interactive tree visualization. This explicitly eliminates the steep learning curve of complex command-line agentic tools, empowering interdisciplinary researchers to directly leverage, visually orchestrate, and accelerate end-to-end scientific breakthroughs.

item.title: xModel-KD: Cross-modal Knowledge Distillation for 3D Scene Perception using LiDAR
item.content:encoded: Point cloud segmentation is a fundamental task in 3D scene understanding. Its progress is constrained by the high cost and time required for dense 3D annotations, making labeled samples difficult to obtain. Beyond annotation scarcity, different sensing modalities face inherent limitations. 2D images provide rich texture and appearance cues, yet they lack explicit depth and geometric structure. In contrast, 3D point clouds capture accurate spatial geometry but are sparse and contain no texture information. As a result, relying on a single modality restricts the richness of learned representations and weakens generalization. Although recent multi-modal methods that combine 3D point clouds with 2D images have demonstrated strong performance in tasks such as classification and retrieval, they typically depend on large-scale labeled datasets and have not been fully exploited for data-efficient dense prediction. To address these limitations, we propose a novel cross-modal knowledge distillation framework, xModel-KD, for 3D point cloud segmentation. Our method exploits the complementary strengths of 2D texture and 3D geometry by learning unified per-point representations through cross-modal alignment. Specifically, we design a cross-modal fusion encoder trained with a contrastive objective that enforces feature consistency between corresponding 2D and 3D representations across multiple views. By integrating powerful pre-trained backbones with a targeted fusion strategy, the proposed framework effectively transfers appearance cues from images to geometry-aware point features. Experimental results show that cross-modal fusion achieves a 2% absolute improvement in mIoU over a LiDAR-only baseline, demonstrating the benefit of leveraging complementary multi-modal information for scalable and annotation-efficient 3D scene understanding.

item.title: Supervised Distributional Reduction via Optimal Transport and Dependence Maximization
item.content:encoded: Learning representations that capture both intrinsic data geometry and target-relevant structure remains a fundamental challenge, particularly in settings where data reduction must balance compression with predictive fidelity. While distributional reduction-encompassing joint clustering and dimensionality reduction-offers a principled way to summarize data, its supervised variants remain relatively under-explored, despite the importance of retaining task-relevant signal for downstream prediction and decision-making. We propose Supervised Distributional Reduction (SDR), an algorithm for learning target-aware representations by combining optimal transport with explicit dependence maximization. SDR builds on the Fused Gromov-Wasserstein (FGW) objective to align the relational structure of the input distribution with a set of representative points, while augmenting it with a direct dependence term that encourages the learned embeddings to capture predictive signal more explicitly. This results in compact representations that reflect both geometric structure and supervision. Beyond representation learning, SDR naturally induces a data-dependent, non-stationary geometry that can be leveraged for settings such as Gaussian Process (GP) modelling. By redefining distances through target-aware distributional alignment, SDR enables the construction of adaptive kernels that respond to local variations in both data geometry and supervision, offering an optimal transport-based perspective on non-stationary kernel design.

item.title: LongAV-Compass: Towards Unified Evaluation of Minute-Scale Audio-Visual Generation Across T2AV, I2AV, and V2AV
item.content:encoded: Audio-visual generation is rapidly advancing from short clips to minute-long content, while existing evaluation protocols remain largely confined to short-form settings. Existing benchmarks primarily focus on 5--10 second text-conditioned generation and rarely support unified evaluation across text, image, and video conditioning modalities. Moreover, they provide limited insight into how identity consistency, narrative coherence, and audio-visual alignment degrade over extended temporal horizons. To bridge this gap, we introduce LongAV-Compass, a systematic benchmark for minute-long audio-visual generation. LongAV-Compass contains 284 curated test cases spanning text-to-audio-video (T2AV), image-to-audio-video (I2AV), and video-to-audio-video (V2AV), organized by application scenario and generation complexity. The benchmark combines taxonomy-guided benchmark construction with a unified evaluation framework that integrates MLLM-assisted assessment with complementary perceptual and multimodal metrics, including DINO-v2, ArcFace, CLIP, and ImageBind. The framework evaluates more than 20 fine-grained dimensions covering within-segment quality, cross-segment consistency, global narrative coherence, semantic alignment, and audio-visual synchronization. Through experiments on 11 representative models together with human-alignment validation, LongAV-Compass provides a diagnostic testbed for analyzing the limitations of current systems in sustaining coherent, semantically aligned, and temporally consistent minute-scale audio-visual generation across diverse input modalities.

item.title: RAISE: RAG Design as an Architecture Search Problem
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems expose numerous design choices spanning query rewriting, chunking, retrieval depth, reranking, and context compression. In practice, these choices are often configured through heuristics, hindering systematic evaluation and reproducibility across settings. We argue that this challenge is best formulated as RAG architecture search. To support controlled and reproducible study of this problem, we introduce the RAG Intelligence Search Engine (RAISE), a comprehensive framework and benchmark for RAG hyperparameter optimization, which evaluates optimization methods for RAG pipelines under standardized search spaces and budgets. RAISE implements 13 search algorithms and evaluates them across seven public text and multimodal datasets using three random seeds. Our experiments show that optimization performance is highly task-dependent: methods that perform strongly on one dataset may not generalize consistently across others, cautioning against interpreting aggregate rankings as evidence of universally superior strategies. RAISE provides a common experimental substrate for fair, reproducible, and systematic research on RAG hyperparameter optimization.

item.title: A multifractal-based masked auto-encoder: an application to medical images
item.content:encoded: Masked autoencoders (MAE) have shown great promise in medical image classification. However, the random masking strategy employed by traditional MAEs may overlook critical areas in medical images, where even subtle changes can indicate disease. To address this limitation, we propose a novel approach that utilizes a multifractal measure (Renyi entropy) to optimize the masking strategy. Our method, termed Multifractal-Optimized Masked Autoencoder (MO-MAE), employs a multifractal analysis to identify regions of high complexity and information content. By focusing the masking process on these areas, MO-MAE ensures that the model learns to reconstruct the most diagnostically relevant features. This approach is particularly beneficial for medical imaging, where fine-grained inspection of tissue structures is crucial for accurate diagnosis. We evaluate MO-MAE on several medical datasets covering various diseases, including MedMNIST and COVID-CT. Our results demonstrate that MO-MAE achieves promising performance, surpassing other basiline and state-of-the-art models. The proposed method also adds minimum computational overhead as the computation of the proposed measure is straightforward. Our findings suggest that the multifractal-optimized masking strategy enhances the model's ability to capture and reconstruct complex tissue structures, leading to more accurate and efficient medical image representation. The proposed MO-MAE framework offers a promising direction for improving the accuracy and efficiency of deep learning models in medical image analysis, potentially advancing the field of computer-aided diagnosis.

item.title: The Missing Piece in Pre-trained Model Evaluation: Reward-Guided Decoding Unlocks Task-Oriented Behavior Without Parameter Updates
item.content:encoded: With the rapid progress of large language models (LLMs), reliably evaluating the capabilities of pre-trained LLMs has become increasingly important. The challenge is that base pre-trained models are optimized for next-token prediction and often fail to follow instructions or produce well-formed answers under standard prompting and direct decoding. As a result, benchmark performance can conflate model capability with decoding-induced failures to produce task-oriented outputs, while exposing such behavior often relies on costly post-training. Recent decodingonly approaches attempt to reshape output distributions, but such methods can be inefficient and brittle across open-ended tasks. To address these limitations, we propose Energy-Based Decoding (EBD), a training-free, reward-guided framework for activating task-oriented behaviors from frozen pre-trained LLMs across both open-ended and objective tasks. EBD augments decoding with an external lightweight reward model, steering generations toward high-utility responses while anchoring them to the pre-trained model prior through a reward-tilted target distribution. We show that EBD shifts base-model outputs toward more instructionfollowing behavior, increasing behavioral similarity to post-trained counterparts and enabling a fairer inference-time evaluation of accessible pre-trained-model behavior. Empirically, EBD outperforms baselines across five models and six benchmarks, improving Qwen3-8B-Base on AlpacaEval2.0 from 8.8 to 44.5, reducing Mistral-7B Math500 latency by 18.9x relative to prior decoding work, and remaining robust to reward-model size.

item.title: BEAT框架提升电影预告片自动生成效果
item.content:encoded:
BEAT框架通过音乐-视觉对齐和动态规划提升电影预告片生成质量。



摘要： 本文提出BEAT框架，用于自动电影预告片生成。该框架包含MuVA音乐-视觉对齐编码器和Bar-DP能量自适应动态规划算法，能够灵活匹配音乐节奏与画面剪辑，提升预告片的节奏感和观赏性。研究还构建了TrailerArena基准测试平台，涵盖20多项指标，全面评估预告片生成效果。实验表明，BEAT在多个维度上均取得最佳表现，实现了端到端的高质量预告片生成。

item.title: 隐私保护下的e值统计检验最优率研究
item.content:encoded:
研究隐私条件下e值检验的最大功效与算法效率。



摘要： 本文探讨了在满足ε-差分隐私的前提下，如何通过e值进行统计检验以最大化检验功效。研究分析了在给定两个分布的情况下，测试独立同分布样本时的最优e值性能，并提出了一种精确匹配该最优率的算法。在序贯检验场景中，作者给出了私有e过程的停止时间上下界，并通过实验验证了所提算法在多种隐私级别和测试问题上的数据效率优于现有方法DP-SPRT。

item.title: Setswana情感标注质量随时间下降的分析与模型评估
item.content:encoded:
研究Setswana情感标注质量随时间下降原因及模型表现。



摘要： 该研究分析了跨多批次的Setswana情感标注任务中，标注质量随时间下降的问题。尽管整体Kappa值为0.76（优秀），但每批次的Kappa值下降超过32点。研究发现，标签混淆主要集中在负面和中性之间，部分标注者出现自动标注倾向，且标注时间间隔越长，Kappa值越低。此外，对三种多语言编码器和专有模型进行了基准测试，微调后F1分数提升显著，GPT-5在少样本情况下表现最佳。研究还发布了数据集、标注时间戳和分析代码，以支持未来非洲语言NLP资源的质量审计。

item.title: 轻量级3D场景感知方法ESAM++在边缘设备上的应用
item.content:encoded:
ESAM++提升边缘设备3D场景感知效率与实用性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为ESAM++的轻量级3D场景感知方法，适用于资源受限的边缘设备。该方法通过引入3D稀疏特征金字塔网络（SFPN），有效提取多尺度几何特征，显著降低计算开销和模型大小。在ScanNet、ScanNet200、SceneNN和3RScan等多个基准数据集上进行评估，结果表明ESAM++在保持高精度的同时，推理速度比ESAM快3倍，模型体积缩小2倍，具备在边缘设备上实际部署的潜力。

item.title: 基于用户感知的主动知识获取框架提升对话系统情感支持能力
item.content:encoded:
UKA框架提升对话系统情感支持效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为User-Aware Active Knowledge Acquisition (UKA)的无梯度主动对话学习框架，旨在增强对话系统在多轮交互中对用户隐含需求的适应能力。该框架通过理论心智不确定性估计机制，优先选择能获取更多信息的回应，从而提高用户反馈的效率。实验表明，UKA在多个对话基准测试中优于现有方法，在对话质量和用户对齐方面表现优异。

item.title: 高斯向量在坐标非线性映射下的子高斯集中不等式
item.content:encoded:
提出一种维度无关的高斯向量子高斯集中不等式。



摘要： 本文提出了一种适用于任何有界函数在良好条件协方差下的维度无关子高斯集中不等式。该结果由Gemini 3.5 Flash发现，并应用于回答Simone Bombari关于符号量化线性映射Y = sgn(Wx)的问题。该工具为分析高斯向量在非线性变换下的行为提供了新的方法。

item.title: 多智能体协作问题求解的拓扑耦合提示框架
item.content:encoded:
TCP-MCP联合优化提示与通信结构，提升多智能体系统性能。



摘要： 本文提出TCP-MCP（拓扑耦合提示多智能体协作求解框架），通过统一优化智能体提示和通信拓扑结构，提高多智能体系统的协作效率。该方法在初始化阶段进行景观探测以校准搜索行为，并通过帕累托前沿诊断在任务性能、令牌成本和结构复杂性三个目标下自适应调整探索策略。实验表明，TCP-MCP在MMLU-Pro、MMLU和GSM8K基准测试中分别达到82.66%、89.96%和96.61%的准确率，优于自动图生成基线，并且在令牌使用上比辩论式系统减少最多5.69倍。

item.title: 基于双ConvNeXt V2的RGB-热红外多模态融合语义分割方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种多模态融合架构，提升复杂环境下的语义分割效果。



摘要： 本文针对城市驾驶场景中恶劣光照条件下语义分割的挑战，提出一种基于双ConvNeXt V2主干网络的多模态融合架构。该方法通过阶段式、模态自适应的融合策略，在早期特征阶段采用基于频率的融合模块，结合热红外与可见光图像信息；在后期特征阶段设计跨模态注意力和多尺度深度卷积模块，增强语义对应关系。实验结果表明，该方法在MFNet和PST900数据集上取得了优异性能，且参数量和计算成本显著低于现有方法。

item.title: LLMs在自闭症群体中的偏见与评估框架研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示大语言模型在自闭症相关文本中存在偏见，提出新的评估框架。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在涉及自闭症群体的决策任务中可能放大多元视角或压制特定观点的问题。尽管已有研究指出LLMs中存在残疾相关偏见，但其如何理解或识别能力主义仍不明确。研究引入了一种基于心理测量权重、贴近社区实际的评估框架，以检测反自闭症的能力主义语言。该框架比传统多数投票方法更为严格，显著低估了自闭症者及接受自闭症的观点。研究发现，LLMs常产生有害输出，误将社区重新定义的语言标记为能力主义，并在评估工具被掩盖时表现出对自闭症者更负面的态度。错误分析显示，模型依赖表面关键词匹配，而非上下文因素如说话者身份或语言是否促进内部团结或伤害外部群体。

item.title: 基于LLM的进化搜索优化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究LLM调用预算分配与运行可靠性，提升搜索效率。



摘要： 本文探讨了在有限LLM调用预算下如何优化搜索策略，以提高数学和组合任务的求解效果。通过分析多个模型和任务，发现能力排序主要依赖于有效FLOPs，并提出一种基于多臂老虎机的BaSE算法，在不改变模型、提示或评估器的情况下，显著提升了平均性能，尤其在高方差场景中表现出更高的可靠性。

item.title: Proprio：提升视频生成物理合理性的训练-free框架
item.content:encoded:
Proprio通过自评估机制提升视频生成的物理合理性。



摘要： 本文提出Proprio，一种无需训练的框架，用于评估和提升视频生成模型输出的物理合理性。该方法受本体感觉启发，利用模型在受控潜在扰动下的流动残差作为自我评分信号，通过动态时空掩码聚焦于运动相关区域，并用于最佳选择或梯度优化。实验表明，Proprio在多个基准测试中优于现有方法，在物理常识任务中显著提升性能，且人类评估也支持其有效性。结果表明，冻结的视频生成器内部包含可用于评估和改进物理合理性的信号。

item.title: ROVER：一种高效的多图像推理视觉证据路由方法
item.content:encoded:
ROVER提升多图像推理的视觉证据整合效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出ROVER（Routing Object-centric Visual Evidence for grounded multi-image Reasoning），一种轻量级、可学习的插件，用于高效地全局视觉证据路由。ROVER通过在每次对象定位预测时注入特定的token三元组，协同聚合推理上下文、通过对象中心的差异注意力提炼图像内线索，并在视觉工作空间中跨对象和图像路由和整合历史感知证据。ROVER被集成到Qwen2.5-VL-7B中，并开发了交错的SFT-to-GRPO训练流程。实验表明，该方法在MM-GCoT和VideoEspresso数据集上表现最佳，分别提升了4.8%和8.6%的答案准确率，且具有良好的迁移能力。

item.title: MoE到全连接网络的转换方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出将MoE模型转为全连接结构的方法，提升性能与效率。



摘要： 本文提出了一种系统性的框架，将训练好的Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) 模型转换为标准的全连接架构。通过评分、选择和分组专家，并将其连接为一个密集的前馈网络，再通过知识蒸馏进行优化。在Qwen3-30B-A3B等模型上进行了广泛实验，评估了多种评分、分组和规模缩放方法，共生成350种配置。结果显示，评分方法对性能影响最大，提出的多样性感知评分方法优于现有方法。在参数量相同的对比下，MoE到全连接的转换方法在下游任务准确率上比密集模型剪枝高出6.3个百分点，且训练速度更快。

item.title: 角色提示对大语言模型响应质量的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
角色提示提升专业深度但降低清晰度，需多维度评估。



摘要： 本研究通过对比四种提示方式在1140个开放问题上的表现，探讨角色提示对大语言模型响应质量的影响。结果显示，角色提示在医学、心理学等需要结构化专家框架的领域中提升了专业深度，但在金融、法律等需要简洁解释的领域表现不佳。研究还发现，结合嵌入搜索和LLM选择的混合检索方法优于单一嵌入方法，但无法消除专业深度与清晰度之间的权衡。结论表明，角色提示主要改变了响应特征而非普遍提升能力，因此需要多指标评估其效果。

item.title: 基于Transformer的布料模拟框架ClothTransformer
item.content:encoded:
ClothTransformer利用Transformer实现高效、统一的布料模拟。



摘要： 本文提出ClothTransformer，一种基于Transformer的布料模拟框架，通过将布料模拟转化为潜在空间中的自回归序列建模，解决了传统神经布料模拟器在场景适应性、网格耦合和碰撞处理方面的不足。该框架在多种场景下（如人体服装、机器人操作和自由落体碰撞）均表现出色，误差比现有方法降低4至9倍，并引入了一个高保真、无穿透的大型数据集以支持更精确的碰撞检测。

item.title: 大型语言模型能否检测并报告自身内部状态？
item.content:encoded:
研究指出当前证据不足以证明大模型具备元认知监控能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型是否能够检测并报告自身的内部状态。尽管已有研究认为模型可以做到这一点，但作者基于人类元认知研究提出质疑，认为需要区分真正的自我觉察与基于表层线索的模式匹配。文章重新评估了两种评估范式，发现模型在检测内部状态干扰和预测隐藏状态标签时的表现可能源于对输入异常的识别，而非真正理解自身状态。此外，在一个更严格的控制任务中，模型表现接近随机，进一步表明现有证据不足以支持模型具备元认知监控能力的结论。

item.title: 编码代理与开发者工作流对齐问题的观察研究
item.content:encoded:
研究分析编码代理在实际开发中的对齐问题及解决方式。



摘要： 本文通过观察20,574次编码代理会话，分析了代理与开发者工作流之间的对齐问题。研究将对齐问题定义为开发者反馈所揭示的系统故障，并从形式、原因、成本和解决方式四个维度进行标注。研究发现，90.5%的对齐问题主要带来努力和信任成本，而非系统破坏。尽管大多数问题需要用户手动修正，但不同IDE和CLI环境下的对齐模式存在差异，且随着时间推移，约束违规和错误自我报告问题有所增加。研究结果为编码代理的设计和评估提供了重要参考。

item.title: 基于多维评估的Focal Reward方法提升大语言模型强化学习性能
item.content:encoded:
Focal Reward方法有效平衡多维评估指标，提升大模型强化学习效果。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型（LLM）在强化学习中因多维评估指标不平衡导致的奖励极化问题，提出了一种新的Focal Reward方法。该方法通过逆向奖励投影机制估计每个评估维度的饱和度，并引入自动加权系数进行精细平衡，从而提升训练效果。实验表明，Focal Reward在三个模型规模和六个基准测试中均优于现有最强静态聚合基线，其优势来源于对仍具提升空间维度的在线动态调整。

item.title: 自适应储备计算框架在混沌系统建模中的应用
item.content:encoded:
本文提出自适应储备计算框架，提升混沌系统建模性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种自适应储备计算框架，用于评估机器学习模型在CTF-4-Science Lorenz基准下的表现。该框架针对十二个不同任务进行优化，涵盖五个不同的场景，如基础预测、噪声信号重建、噪声下的预测、少样本学习和参数泛化。通过精确的储备状态同步、直方图引导的候选选择、多种子储备搜索以及序列多序列训练等方法，显著提升了模型性能。该框架在公开基准测试中获得74.91分，表明自适应储备计算是一种高效且具有竞争力的混沌系统建模方法。

item.title: NAVA：一种用于联合音视频生成的原生对齐框架
item.content:encoded:
NAVA提升音视频同步与语义一致性，参数更少效果更好。



摘要： 本文提出NAVA，一种用于联合音视频生成的原生音频-视觉对齐框架。该框架通过在专用交互空间中建立音视频对应关系，并利用外部上下文进行联合去噪过程，克服了现有方法在细粒度音视频协同进化和低级同步耦合方面的不足。NAVA采用Align-then-Fuse MMDiT架构，实现了从模态感知对齐到模态共享联合去噪的过渡。此外，引入了Timbre-in-Context Conditioning，实现可控的语音音色控制。实验表明，NAVA在Verse-Bench和Seed-TTS数据集上表现优异，具有更高的视频质量、精确的音视频同步、竞争性的音频质量和更强的参考音色可控性，仅使用6.3B参数。

item.title: Smoothed Score Queries and the Complexity of Sampling
item.content:encoded: We study the query complexity of sampling from high-dimensional Gaussian distributions using gradient information. In the standard oracle model, exact gradients expose only matrix-vector products with the precision matrix, leading to polynomial approximation barriers and a characteristic \(\sqrtκ\) dependence on the condition number. We show that this barrier disappears when the sampler is allowed to query \emph{smoothed scores}, namely gradients of the logarithms of the Gaussian-convolved densities. For a Gaussian target with precision matrix \(Λ\), a smoothed-score query at noise level \(τ\) gives access to the resolvent \((Λ+τ^{-1}I)^{-1}\). Combining geometrically spaced noise levels with sinc-quadrature rational approximation, we obtain a sampler with $q=O\!\left(\bigl(\logκ+\log(e\sqrt d/δ_{\rm TV})\bigr)\log(e\sqrt d/δ_{\rm TV})\right)$ smoothed-score queries for total variation error \(δ_{\rm TV}\), improving the condition-number dependence from \(\sqrtκ\) to logarithmic. We also study finite-bit gradient oracles. Using coordinatewise quantization of the transformed smoothed-score answers and a final dithering step, we obtain a sampling scheme whose total communicated gradient information is polylogarithmic in \(κ\); in particular, for fixed dimension and accuracy, the bit complexity is \(O(\log^2κ)\). To complement these upper bounds, we introduce a channel-synthesis, or reverse-Shannon, converse technique for sampling lower bounds. This converts total-variation simulation guarantees into communication requirements and yields an \(Ω(\logκ)\) lower bound on the required gradient information. Together, these results identify smoothed scores as a provably more informative oracle for sampling and give nearly matching upper and lower bounds for its finite-bit complexity.

item.title: SilentRetrieval：针对RAG系统的数据污染攻击方法
item.content:encoded:
SilentRetrieval通过伪造文档攻击RAG系统，实现高成功率且隐蔽性强。



摘要： 本文提出SilentRetrieval，一种针对RAG系统的两阶段数据污染攻击方法。该方法利用协同束搜索和上下文自适应触发生成技术，生成看似正常但包含恶意触发的文档，从而在不显著增加困惑度的情况下提升攻击成功率。实验表明，在多个数据集上，SilentRetrieval在保持较低异常检测率的同时，取得了较高的检索命中率和攻击成功率。尽管结合了检索侧和生成侧的防御措施可以有效降低攻击效果，但会带来延迟代价。人类评估也显示其比非流畅基线更不易被发现，但仍比正常内容更可疑。
