item.title: 基于简短自然语言的担忧文本对生理数据心理解释的补充研究
item.content:encoded:
简短担忧文本可增强生理数据的心理解释性。



摘要： 本文研究了通过超短自然语言描述的担忧内容，作为被动生理数据补充的可行性。在一项为期一年、涉及458名大学生的研究中，参与者定期提供简短的担忧描述。研究比较了多种NLP方法在睡眠和身体活动等九个指标上的表现，发现情感维度与生理结果有显著关联，而具体主题无明显影响。这表明情感状态比具体内容更能反映生理变化，为数字健康应用提供了设计指导。

item.title: Agent基准测试安全审计与对抗性漏洞挖掘
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示Agent基准测试中的奖励欺骗问题并提出安全审计方案。



摘要： 本文探讨了当前Agent基准测试中出现的奖励欺骗现象，即模型在不完成实际任务的情况下获得高分。作者通过分析历史案例，总结出八种常见漏洞模式，并提出Agent-Eval Checklist作为设计基准的参考。同时，开发了BenchJack系统，用于自动化检测和挖掘潜在的奖励欺骗漏洞。进一步扩展为生成对抗性管道，持续发现并修复漏洞，提升基准测试的安全性。实验表明，BenchJack成功识别出多个基准中的219个漏洞，并显著降低可攻击任务比例，证明评估流程缺乏对抗性思维，需加强主动审计。

item.title: 基于LLM的多智能体系统优化框架CANTANTE
item.content:encoded:
CANTANTE提升多智能体系统性能并优化参数配置。



摘要： 本文提出CANTANTE，一种用于优化基于大语言模型的多智能体系统的框架。该框架通过对比不同联合配置在相同查询上的表现，将系统级奖励分解为每个智能体的更新信号，从而解决信用分配问题。研究将代理提示视为可学习的系统参数，并在编程、数学推理和多跳问答任务中验证了其有效性。实验结果显示，CANTANTE在多个基准测试中表现最佳，显著优于现有方法，同时降低了推理成本。此外，信用相关性分析表明，该框架能够生成有意义的个体信号，而非仅反映全局系统评分。

item.title: Executable Agentic Memory for GUI Agent
item.content:encoded: Modern GUI agents typically rely on a model-centric and step-wise interaction paradigm, where LLMs must re-interpret the UI and re-decide actions at every screen, which is fragile in long-horizon tasks. In this paper, we propose Executable Agentic Memory (EAM), a structured Knowledge Graph (KG) that shifts GUI planning from free-form generation to a robust retrieval-and-execution process. Our approach includes a sample-efficient memory construction pipeline using state-aware DFS and action-group mining to compress multi-step routines. To ensure efficient planning, we introduce a value-guided graph search where a lightweight Q-function model steers Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) over the KG. We theoretically establish bias-consistency for the Q-model and derive sample complexity bounds for path recovery. Empirically, EAM outperforms state-of-the-art baselines like UI-TARS-7B by up to $19.6\%$ on AndroidWorld, while reducing token costs $6\times$ relative to GPT-4o. With a $2.8$s average latency, EAM enables reliable, quick, and long-horizon GUI automation.

item.title: ATD-Trans: A Geographically Grounded Japanese-English Travelogue Translation Dataset
item.content:encoded: Geographic text, or textual data rich in geographic (geo-) information is a valuable source for various geographic applications, e.g., tourism management. Making such information accessible to speakers of other languages further enhances its utility; thus, accurate machine translation (MT) is essential for equity in multilingual geo-information access. To facilitate in-depth analysis for geographic text, we introduce ATD-Trans, a geographically grounded Japanese--English travelogue translation dataset, which enables evaluation of MT quality at both the overall and geo-entity levels across domestic (within Japan) and overseas regions. Our experiments on existing language models examine two factors: model language focus and geographic regions. The results highlight advantages of Japanese-enhanced models and greater difficulty in translating domestic-region geo-entities mentioned in travel blogs.

item.title: Reconnecting Fragmented Citation Networks with Semantic Augmentation
item.content:encoded: Citation graphs are fundamental tools for modeling scientific structure, but are often fragmented due to missing citations of scientifically connected articles. To address this issue, we propose a computationally efficient hybrid framework integrating citation topology with large language model (LLM)-based text similarity. Using 662,369 Web of Science publications in Mathematics and Operations Research & Management Science, we augment the original graph by adding semantic edges from small, disconnected components and weighting existing citations according to textual similarity. Semantic augmentation substantially reduces fragmentation while preserving disciplinary homogeneity. Compared to embedding-only clustering, cluster detection on augmented graphs using the Leiden algorithm retains structural interpretability while offering multi-scale organization. The method scales efficiently to large datasets and offers a practical strategy for strengthening citation-based indicators without collapsing disciplinary boundaries.

item.title: AGOP-IxG: A Gradient Covariance Filter for Local Feature Attribution on Tabular Data, with a Controlled Benchmark
item.content:encoded: Automated machine learning pipelines increasingly produce models whose predictions must be explained to end users, auditors, and downstream decision systems. The most widely used feature attribution methods (SHAP, Integrated Gradients, LIME) are typically chosen by convention rather than measured fidelity, because rigorous evaluation is impeded by the absence of ground-truth attribution on real data. We propose AGOP-IxG, a fast per-sample attribution method for tabular classifiers that pre-multiplies the per-sample gradient by a top-$K$ rank-truncated Average Gradient Outer Product matrix, and evaluate it against four widely-used baselines on a controlled tabular benchmark designed for AutoML practitioners. In Part 1, we construct three synthetic multi-class tabular tasks (linear, sparse nonlinear, interaction-based) where ground-truth attribution per sample is analytically or numerically derivable, and compare five methods: AGOP-IxG, SHAP (DeepExplainer), Integrated Gradients, InputXGradient, and LIME. AGOP-IxG leads on Spearman rank correlation and noise feature mass on all three synthetic datasets, and on top-$k$ precision on the interaction dataset. Across all settings, AGOP-IxG is approximately $350\times$ to $1{,}650\times$ faster than SHAP. In Part 2, we evaluate global faithfulness on Adult Income and Credit Card Default using the ROAR protocol; the methods cluster within $\sim 1.7\%$ relative AUC, consistent with AGOP-IxG being optimized for per-sample local attribution rather than global feature ranking.

item.title: Holistic Evaluation and Failure Diagnosis of AI Agents
item.content:encoded: AI agents execute complex multi-step processes, but current evaluation falls short: outcome metrics report success or failure without explaining why, and process-level approaches struggle to connect failure types to their precise locations within long, structured traces. We present a holistic agent evaluation framework that pairs top-down agent-level diagnosis with bottom-up span-level evaluation, decomposing analysis into independent per-span assessments. This decomposition scales to traces of arbitrary length and produces span-level rationales for each verdict. On the TRAIL benchmark, our framework achieves state-of-the-art results across all metrics on both GAIA and SWE-Bench, with relative gains over the strongest prior baselines of up to 38% on category F1, up to 3.5x on localization accuracy, and up to 12.5x on joint localization-categorization accuracy. Per-category analysis shows our framework leading in more error categories than any other evaluator. Notably, the same frontier model achieves several times higher localization accuracy when used inside our framework than as a monolithic judge over the full trace, showing that evaluation methodology, not model capability, is the bottleneck.

item.title: Agentifying Patient Dynamics within LLMs through Interacting with Clinical World Model
item.content:encoded: Sepsis management in the ICU requires sequential treatment decisions under rapidly evolving patient physiology. Although large language models (LLMs) encode broad clinical knowledge and can reason over guidelines, they are not inherently grounded in action-conditioned patient dynamics. We introduce SepsisAgent, a world model-augmented LLM agent for sepsis treatment recommendation. SepsisAgent uses a learned Clinical World Model to simulate patient responses under candidate fluid--vasopressor interventions, and follows a propose--simulate--refine workflow before committing to a prescription. We first show that world-model access alone yields inconsistent LLM decision performance, motivating agent-specific training. We then train SepsisAgent through a three-stage curriculum: patient-dynamics supervised fine-tuning, propose--simulate--refine behavior cloning, and world-model-based agentic reinforcement learning. On MIMIC-IV sepsis trajectories, SepsisAgent outperforms all traditional RL and LLM-based baselines in off-policy value while achieving the best safety profile under guideline adherence and unsafe-action metrics. Further analysis shows that repeated interaction with the Clinical World Model enables the agent to learn regularities in patient evolution, which remain useful even when simulator access is removed.

item.title: To Whom Do Language Models Align? Measuring Principal Hierarchies Under High-Stakes Competing Demands
item.content:encoded: Language models deployed in high-stakes professional settings face conflicting demands from users, institutional authorities, and professional norms. How models act when these demands conflict reveals a principal hierarchy -- an implicit ordering over competing stakeholders that determines, for instance, whether a medical AI receiving a cost-reduction directive from a hospital administrator complies at the expense of evidence-based care, or refuses because professional standards require it. Across 7,136 scenarios in legal and medical domains, we test ten frontier models and find that models frequently fail to adhere to professional standards during task execution, such as drafting, when user instructions conflict with those standards -- despite adequately upholding them when users seek advisory guidance. We further find that the hierarchies between user, authority, and professional standards exhibited by these models are unstable across medical and legal contexts and inconsistent across model families. When failing to follow professional standards, the primary failure mechanism is knowledge omission: models that demonstrably possess relevant knowledge produce harmful outputs without surfacing conflicting knowledge. In a particularly troubling instance, we find that a reasoning model recognizes the relevant knowledge in its reasoning trace -- e.g., that a drug has been withdrawn -- yet suppresses this in the user-facing answer and proceeds to recommend the drug under authority pressure anyway. Inconsistent alignment across task framing, domain, and model families suggests that current alignment methods, including published alignment hierarchies, are unlikely to be robust when models are deployed in high-stakes professional settings.

item.title: Quantifying Potential Observation Missingness in Inverse Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL), which infers reward functions from demonstrations, is a valuable tool for modeling and understanding decision-making behavior. Many variants of IRL have been developed to capture complexities of human decision-making, such as subjective beliefs, imperfect planning, and dynamic goals. However, an often-overlooked issue in real-world behavioral datasets is that the recorded data may be missing observations that were available to the original decision-maker. In use-inspired settings such as healthcare, this can make expert actions appear suboptimal, even when they were near-optimal given the information available at the time. As a result, the rewards learned by standard IRL may be misleading. In this paper, we identify the minimal perturbations to the recorded observations needed for the expert's actions to appear optimal. We develop a practical algorithm for this problem and demonstrate its utility for quantifying the possible extent of missing observations in behavioral datasets through extensive experiments on synthetic navigation tasks, a cancer treatment simulator, and ICU treatment data.

item.title: LRCP: Low-Rank Compressibility Guided Visual Token Pruning for Efficient LVLMs
item.content:encoded: Large vision-language models (LVLMs) achieve strong multimodal understanding, but their inference cost grows rapidly with the number of visual tokens, especially for high-resolution images and long videos. Existing attention-based methods estimate token importance from attention scores, which may introduce positional bias, while representation-based methods reduce visual redundancy based on feature relations or reconstruction errors, overlooking the global structure of the visual token set. In this paper, we revisit visual token compression from the perspective of low-rank compressibility. Across models and datasets, we observe that visual token representations exhibit a pronounced low-rank structure, with a dominant subspace that remains stable even after a large fraction of tokens is randomly removed. Motivated by this finding, we propose LRCP, a training-free compression framework that first estimates the dominant low-rank subspace of visual tokens via PCA, and then scores each token by its projection residual onto this subspace, retaining tokens that are poorly explained by the low-rank background. Extensive experiments show that LRCP achieves superior results, preserving 94.7% of the original image-understanding performance with an 88.9% token reduction and 97.8% of the average video-understanding accuracy with an 87.5% token reduction.

item.title: Linked Multi-Model Data on Russian Domestic and Foreign Policy Speeches
item.content:encoded: This paper introduces a dataset of interlinked multimodal political communications from the Russian government, addressing persistent deficiencies in the availability of social text- and image-based data for authoritarian politics contexts. The dataset comprises two large corpora of official speeches delivered by senior actors within the Kremlin and the Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs over multiple decades. For each speech, we provide Russian- and English-language texts, associated images and captions where available, and harmonized metadata including (e.g.) dates, speakers, (geo)locations, and official government content tags. Unique identifiers link images to speeches and align Russian and English versions of the same communication texts. We further augment these linked datasets with validated topical annotations for both speech texts and speech images, which are generated via transformer-based multimodal topic modeling and refined by a Russian politics expert. The resulting data resources support multimodal, multilingual, temporal, and/or spatial analyses of (authoritarian) political communication and offer a valuable testbed for social science research and large language model (LLM) applications in political domains.

item.title: Scale-Sensitive Shattering: Learnability and Evaluability at Optimal Scale
item.content:encoded: We study the optimal scale at which real-valued function classes exhibit uniform convergence and learnability. Our main result establishes a scale-sensitive generalization of the fundamental theorem of PAC learning: for every bounded real-valued class and every $γ>0$, uniform convergence at scale $γ$, agnostic learnability at scale $γ/2$, and finiteness of the fat-shattering dimension at every scale $γ'>γ$ are equivalent. This resolves a question by Anthony and Bartlett (Cambridge Univ. Press 1999) on the precise scales governing learnability, refuting a conjecture attributed there to Phil Long that a multiplicative 2-factor gap is unavoidable, and improves the upper bounds of Bartlett and Long (JCSS 1998), which incur such a loss. The key technical ingredient is a direct bound on empirical $\ell_\infty$ covering numbers, avoiding the standard detour through packing numbers. As a consequence, we obtain sharp asymptotic metric-entropy bounds in terms of the fat-shattering scale $γ$: an $O(\log^2 n)$ bound holds already at scale $γ/2$, while an $O(\log n)$ bound holds at scale $2γ$. We further show that the $O(\log^2 n)$ bound is sometimes tight. These results resolve open questions by Alon et al. (JACM 1997) and Rudelson and Vershynin (Ann. of Math. 2006). As an application, we establish a sharp dichotomy for bounded integral probability metrics: every such IPM is either estimable or cannot be weakly evaluated within any multiplicative factor $c<3$, while $3$-weak evaluability always holds, resolving an open question from Aiyer et al. (ICML 2026). We also highlight several open questions on quantitative sample complexity and evaluability.

item.title: DT-Transformer: A Foundation Model for Disease Trajectory Prediction on a Real-world Health System
item.content:encoded: Accurate disease trajectory prediction is critical for early intervention, resource allocation, and improving long-term outcomes. While electronic health records (EHRs) provide a rich longitudinal view of patient health in clinical environments, models trained on curated research cohorts may not reflect routine deployment settings, and those trained on single-hospital datasets capture only fragments of each patient's trajectory. This highlights the importance of leveraging large, multi-hospital health systems for training and validation to better reflect real-world clinical complexity. In this work, we develop DT-Transformer, a foundation model trained on 57.1M structured EHR entries over 1.7M patients from Mass General Brigham (MGB), spanning 11 hospitals and a broad network of outpatient clinics. DT-Transformer achieves strong discrimination in both held-out and prospective validation settings. Next-event prediction achieves a median age- and sex-stratified AUC of 0.871 across 896 disease categories, with all categories exceeding AUC 0.5. These results support health system-scale training as a path toward foundation models suited to real-world clinical forecasting.

item.title: A Unified Non-Parametric and Interpretable Point Cloud Analysis via t-FCW Graph Representation
item.content:encoded: We introduce an empowered transposed Fully Connected Weighted (t-FCW) graph representation to embed point clouds into a metric space. While original t-FCW has shown promising results for point cloud classification, the reasons behind its effectiveness and its broader applicability remained unclear. In this work, we analyze the properties that make the empowered and original t-FCW effective and design a network that uses the empowered t-FCW exclusively as feature extractors. From an interpretability perspective, we build memory banks for classification, part segmentation, and semantic segmentation using the empowered t-FCW. Our analysis reveals that the empowered t-FCW inherits robustness from surface descriptors, provides interpretability through dimension-wise relations. These properties enable a highly efficient and interpretable network, which processes the ModelNet40 classification problem in approximately 7 seconds on an NVIDIA RTX A5000 GPU. Importantly, empowered t-FCW can function both as a lightweight standalone baseline and as a complementary plug-in to existing deep models.

item.title: HIR-ALIGN: Enhancing Hyperspectral Image Restoration via Diffusion-Based Data Generation
item.content:encoded: Hyperspectral image (HSI) restoration is crucial for reliable analysis, as real HSIs suffer from degradations like noise, blur, and resolution loss. However, existing models trained on source data often fail on target domains lacking clean references, a common occurrence in practice. To address this issue, we present HIR-ALIGN, a plug-and-play target-adaptive augmentation framework that enhances hyperspectral image restoration by augmenting limited training images with synthetic data that closely matches the target distribution using no extra data. It consists of three stages: (i) proxy generation, where off-the-shelf restoration models restore degraded target observations to produce semantics-preserving proxy HSIs that approximate target-domain clean images; (ii) distribution-adaptive synthesis, where a blur-robust unCLIP diffusion model generates target-aligned RGBs from proxy RGBs, with prompt conditioning and embedding-space noise initialization. Then, a warp-based spectral transfer module synthesizes HSIs by aligning each generated RGB with the proxy RGB, estimating soft patch-wise transport weights, and applying these weights and learnable local interpolation kernels to the proxy HSI; and (iii) aligned supervised finetuning, where restoration networks pretrained on the source distribution are finetuned using both the proxy HSIs and synthesized target-aligned HSIs, and are then deployed on degraded target images. We further provide theoretical analysis showing that augmentation-based finetuning can achieve lower target-domain restoration risk by jointly improving target distribution coverage and controlling spectral bias. Extensive experiments on simulated and real datasets across denoising and super-resolution tasks demonstrate that HIR-ALIGN consistently improves source-only supervised baselines, outperforming both source-only counterparts and representative unsupervised methods.

item.title: RTLC -- Research, Teach-to-Learn, Critique: A three-stage prompting paradigm inspired by the Feynman Learning Technique that lifts LLM-as-judge accuracy on JudgeBench with no fine-tuning
item.content:encoded: LLM-as-a-judge is now the default measurement instrument for open-ended generation, but on the public JudgeBench benchmark even strong instruction-tuned judges barely scrape past random on objective-correctness pairwise items. We introduce RTLC, a three-stage prompting recipe -- Research, Teach-to-Learn, Critique -- that promotes a single black-box LLM into an ensemble-of-thought judge with no fine-tuning, retrieval, or external tools. Stage 1 wraps the input in a fixed pedagogical scaffold porting the Feynman Learning Technique (study $\to$ teach $\to$ find gaps $\to$ simplify) into LLM prompting. Stage 2 draws N=10 independent candidate verdicts at temperature 0.4. Stage 3 acts as its own critic, cross-comparing the candidate set against the original question to emit one critiqued verdict at temperature 0. On JudgeBench-GPT (350 hard pairwise items), Claude 3.7 Sonnet's pairwise accuracy climbs from 64.6% (single-shot vanilla prompt) to 78.6% (RTLC critique-of-10) -- an absolute 14.0-percentage-point gain. RTLC also beats N=10 self-consistency majority voting (77.7%) and a zero-shot first candidate (74.0%). A clean three-step ablation attributes +9.4 pp to the Teach-to-Learn scaffold, +3.7 pp to N=10 marginalisation, and +0.9 pp to explicit critique. We discuss the cost-accuracy frontier (RTLC sits above self-consistency at every working point), the error-budget breakdown across the four JudgeBench categories (knowledge, reasoning, math, coding), and how RTLC composes orthogonally with post-hoc judge-score calibration, with the two interventions compounding multiplicatively in practice.

item.title: WARDEN: Endangered Indigenous Language Transcription and Translation with 6 Hours of Training Data
item.content:encoded: This paper introduces WARDEN, an early language model system capable of transcribing and translating Wardaman, an endangered Australian indigenous language into English. The significant challenge we face is the lack of large-scale training data: in fact, we only have 6 hours of annotated audio. Therefore, while it is common practice to train a single model for transcription and translation using large datasets (like English to French), this practice is no longer viable in the Wardaman to English context. To tackle the low-resource challenge, we design WARDEN to have separate transcription and translation models: WARDEN first turns a Wardaman audio input into phonemic transcription, and then the transcription into English translation. Further, we propose two useful techniques to enhance performance. For transcription, we initialize the Wardaman token from Sundanese, a language that shares similar phonemes with Wardaman, to accelerate fine-tuning of the transcription model. For translation, we compile a Wardaman-English dictionary from expert annotations, and provide this domain-specific knowledge to a large language model (LLM) to reason and decide the final output. We empirically demonstrate that this two-stage design works better than data-hungry unified approaches in extremely low data settings. Using a mere 6 hours of annotated data, WARDEN outperforms larger open-source and proprietary models and establishes a strong baseline. Data and code are available.

item.title: 基于多智能体框架的多媒体验证系统
item.content:encoded:
提出一种可争议的多智能体框架用于多媒体验证。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于多智能体框架的多媒体验证方法，结合了多模态大语言模型、外部验证工具和基于竞技场的定量双极论证（A-QBAF），以应对ICMR 2026多媒体验证挑战。该方法将每个案例分解为以主张为中心的部分，检索针对性证据，并将证据转化为带有来源和强度评分的结构化支持与攻击性论点。通过局部论证图解决冲突并处理不确定性，最终生成透明、可编辑且计算实用的验证报告。该系统的实现已公开在GitHub上。

item.title: 大语言模型对HMSC语义理解的实证研究
item.content:encoded:
大语言模型对HMSC语义理解有限，准确率仅52%。



摘要： 本文研究了大语言模型（LLMs）在处理高阶消息序列图（HMSCs）时的语义理解能力。通过129项任务测试，发现LLMs整体准确率为52%，其中对基本语义概念如事件和顺序的理解较好（约88%），但在抽象、组合、轨迹计算等复杂任务中表现较差（约36%-42%）。所有测试模型均未能正确处理共区域和显式因果依赖等概念，表明其对HMSC形式化语义的理解仍存在显著不足。

item.title: PRISM：实时防御多智能体LLM中的敏感信息传播
item.content:encoded:
PRISM有效防止多智能体系统中敏感信息泄露。



摘要： 本文提出PRISM，一种实时防御机制，用于解决多智能体大语言模型（LLM）中因共享上下文导致的敏感信息传播问题。该方法将凭证泄露视为生成过程中的序列风险累积问题，在每个解码步骤中结合16种特征信号计算风险评分，通过绿色、黄色和红色风险区域实现逐标记干预。实验表明，PRISM在2000个任务的对抗基准测试中表现出色，F1值达0.832，且无任何任务级泄露，显著优于现有方法。

item.title: EverAnimate：高效长时动画生成方法
item.content:encoded:
EverAnimate提升长时动画生成质量与角色一致性。



摘要： EverAnimate是一种高效的后训练方法，用于长时动画视频生成，旨在保持视觉质量和角色身份。文章指出，长时动画面临挑战，因为高度动态的人体运动需要在相对静态的环境中合成，导致分块生成容易出现累积漂移问题，包括低层质量漂移和高层语义漂移。为解决这一问题，EverAnimate通过锚定到持久的潜在上下文记忆来恢复漂移的流动轨迹，包含两个互补机制：持久潜在传播和恢复性流动匹配。实验表明，仅使用轻量级LoRA微调，EverAnimate在短时和长时动画生成任务中均优于现有方法。

item.title: 提升模型在微调后保持能力的训练方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究如何通过训练策略增强模型在微调后的能力保持。



摘要： 本文探讨了如何通过上游训练策略提升模型在后续微调过程中对已习得能力的保留。研究发现，直接的后训练表现不能可靠预测微调后的保留效果，而早期数据混合（将后训练数据融入预训练阶段）能有效提升能力的鲁棒性。实验表明，在计算资源匹配的情况下，最优策略并非极端分配数据，而是平衡预训练与后训练。此外，回放和丢弃等技术在后训练阶段可与早期暴露互补，进一步提升模型性能。研究强调应将抗遗忘能力作为上游训练的首要目标。

item.title: FRAME：多路径证据融合的图像篡改检测方法
item.content:encoded:
FRAME通过多路径证据融合提升图像篡改检测性能。



摘要： 随着图像编辑工具和生成式AI的发展，数字图像的真实性验证变得愈发困难。尽管已有多种检测算法，但它们在鲁棒性、证据完整性及泛化能力方面存在不足。为此，本文提出FRAME，一种基于多路径分析和自适应证据融合的图像篡改检测方法。FRAME将多种取证算法整合到多路径分析空间中，根据输入图像动态选择有效路径，并融合互补证据以提高检测与定位效果。该方法超越了单一分析和固定融合策略，提升了图像取证的灵活性与可靠性。实验结果表明FRAME在多种篡改场景中均表现出色，相关代码已开源。

item.title: TokAlign++提升大语言模型词汇适配性能
item.content:encoded:
TokAlign++优化词汇对齐，提升多语言模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出TokAlign++方法，通过学习更好的词元对齐词典来改进词汇适配性能。将源和目标词汇视为两种不同语言，从单语词元表示中学习双语词元对齐词典，并根据该词典重新排列模型参数以适应新词汇。实验结果显示，该方法在15种语言上提升了多语言文本压缩率并保留了原始模型的多语言能力，仅需1000步即可恢复原始模型性能。统一词汇后，词元级蒸馏显著提升了基础模型，仅使用235M个词元。

item.title: 变分线性注意力提升长序列记忆性能
item.content:encoded:
变分线性注意力降低内存开销并提升长序列记忆性能。



摘要： 本文提出变分线性注意力（VLA），通过在线正则化最小二乘问题优化记忆更新，有效控制内存状态增长。理论分析表明，VLA在所有序列长度和头维度下保持递归雅可比谱范数为1，并在有限输入下实现状态自限。实验结果显示，VLA在1000个token时将Frobenius范数降低109倍，在多查询关联回忆任务中达到近完美准确率，且在高内存负载下表现优于DeltaNet和标准线性注意力。此外，基于Triton的优化内核实现了14倍速度提升，使VLA在约43,000个token时超越softmax注意力延迟。

item.title: 基于临床对齐的医学图像生成模型微调方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CAS和CRAFT提升医学图像生成的临床对齐性。



摘要： 本文针对医学图像生成中因标注数据有限导致的幻觉和临床不一致性问题，提出了一种新的评估指标Clinical Alignment Score (CAS) 和微调框架Clinical Reward-Aligned Finetuning (CRAFT)。CRAFT通过结合多模态大语言模型和视觉-语言模型的知识，利用标签条件提示、临床清单和可微奖励优化来提升生成图像的临床合理性。实验结果显示，CRAFT在多个医学影像模态上显著提升了CAS分数和下游分类性能，并有效减少了低对齐率的生成结果。

item.title: 基于环境自适应的极端事件预测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出EAPO框架提升极端事件预测性能。



摘要： 本文针对从气象数据中预测罕见极端事件（如野火）的挑战，提出了一种环境自适应偏好优化（EAPO）框架。该框架通过k近邻检索构建与目标分布对齐的数据集，并结合监督学习和偏好优化进行微调，以增强对少数但高影响事件的识别能力。EAPO在面对环境变化时仍能保持稳定性能，在真实野火预测任务中取得ROC-AUC 0.7310的优异表现，显著提升了极端情况下的检测效果。

item.title: 基于机器学习的妊娠相关血栓性微血管病风险预测研究
item.content:encoded:
机器学习模型可有效预测妊娠相关血栓性微血管病风险。



摘要： 本研究通过回顾性分析300例妊娠病例，包括142例P-TMA患者和158例对照组，利用146个纵向实验室指标构建机器学习模型。研究采用五种算法评估，最终选择梯度提升模型，在测试集中获得AUROC为0.872，AUPRC为0.883，具有良好的敏感性和特异性。研究发现，孕6周的胱抑素C水平可能作为早期监测指标，提示临床中可通过常规实验室数据识别P-TMA风险。

item.title: 联邦Actor-Critic框架下的协同策略训练研究
item.content:encoded:
提出联邦Actor-Critic框架，实现个性化与共享策略的平衡。



摘要： 本文提出一种联邦Actor-Critic框架，允许智能体在共享线性子空间的同时保持个性化的本地策略。通过迭代估计共同子空间、局部Critic头和本地策略，实现了在马尔可夫采样下的有限时间收敛。理论分析表明，Critic误差和策略梯度范数分别以特定速率收敛，展示了代理数量K的线性加速效果。为应对异构轨迹和耦合学习动态，引入新的扰动分析和QR分解方法，并细化了马尔可夫采样与冻结策略之间的差异。实验在联邦Hopper-v5上验证了该框架的有效性，优于单一PPO和FedAvg PPO。

item.title: 基于Mamba的降水实时预报模型MambaRain
item.content:encoded:
MambaRain提升0-3小时降水预报精度与稳定性。



摘要： 文章提出MambaRain，一种结合Mamba长时序建模与自注意力机制的多尺度编码解码架构，用于提升0-3小时降水实时预报性能。该模型通过融合全局时序动态与空间相关性，有效克服传统方法在90分钟以上预测中精度下降的问题，并引入频谱损失函数减少混沌降水系统的模糊伪影，显著提高预报准确性，尤其在2-3小时预测范围内表现突出。

item.title: 多粒度开放世界目标计数方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出多粒度计数方法以提升开放世界目标计数准确性。



摘要： 本文指出，开放世界目标计数仍存在挑战，主要原因是计数粒度未被明确。作者将开放世界计数重新定义为多粒度计数，通过视觉示例和细粒度文本指定目标外观和语义粒度。为解决数据瓶颈，提出首个全自动数据扩展流程，构建了KubriCount数据集。实验表明，现有模型在细粒度区分上表现不佳，因此训练了HieraCount模型，显著提升了多粒度计数的准确性和泛化能力。

item.title: 科学贡献图谱与技术路线预测研究
item.content:encoded:
构建科学贡献图谱，提升科技发展预测能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种自动化技术路线规划方法，通过从学术论文中提取科学贡献并建立其前提条件的关联。研究构建了包含200万条科学贡献和1250万条前提关系的科学贡献图谱，用于预测未来科技发展的前提技术。实验表明，现代模型在该任务上的表现持续提升，达到0.48的MAP指标。该资源有望支持科学影响力评估和自动化科学发现。

item.title: 生成式AI在教学中的应用与师生感知研究
item.content:encoded:
生成式AI可有效辅助教学，学生难以区分AI与人工生成的课件。



摘要： 本文探讨了生成式AI工具在教学中的应用，特别是通过分析教育者对AI生成课件的评价，以及学生对AI与人工生成课件的感知差异。研究对比了NotebookLM、Claude、M365 Copilot、Cursor和Claude Code等工具生成的课件质量，并发现编码助手类工具生成的课件在准确性、完整性和教学有效性方面表现最佳。此外，学生普遍认为AI生成的课件质量与教师制作的相当，且无法可靠识别哪些是AI生成的。研究还发现高质量的课件往往被低估为AI生成，表明学生可能将低质量与AI来源联系起来。这些结果展示了将生成式AI整合到教学设计中的潜力，并呼吁进一步研究如何负责任地利用此类工具。

item.title: 基于离线推理蒸馏的模型优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种改进的离线推理蒸馏框架，提升小模型推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种离线推理蒸馏框架，旨在解决传统方法中因教师与学生分布不一致导致的推理误差累积问题。该方法在保持离线数据高效性和高质量监督的同时，通过自适应调整教师指导以匹配学生的推理分布，显著提升了数学推理任务中的准确性与稳定性。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中优于现有离线蒸馏算法，同时保留了良好的指令遵循能力。

item.title: 基于条件记忆的生成推荐框架ComeIR
item.content:encoded:
提出ComeIR框架解决生成推荐中的表示构建瓶颈。



摘要： 生成推荐（GR）通过自回归生成语义标识符（SID）来预测目标物品。现有方法在构建物品级表示时面临两个挑战：直接合并SID嵌入可能导致信息丢失，而依赖外部输入的方法无法有效保留SID结构信息。为此，研究提出ComeIR框架，通过条件记忆增强物品表示，重建SID嵌入为物品感知输入，并在SID解码中恢复令牌粒度。该框架包括MM引导的令牌评分、双层Engram记忆和记忆恢复预测头，实验表明其有效且可扩展。

item.title: 基于自然语言的运行时状态构建框架SDP
item.content:encoded:
SDP通过自然语言谓词构建状态空间，提升任务执行效果。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为State-Centric Decision Process (SDP)的运行时框架，用于在缺乏明确状态空间的语言环境中构建必要的决策要素。该框架让智能体通过自然语言谓词逐步构建状态，每一步都验证观察结果是否符合谓词描述，并将通过验证的谓词作为已认证状态。这种方法生成了任务诱导的状态空间、观测到状态的映射、已认证的转移和终止条件，适用于规划、科学探索、网页推理和多跳问答等任务。实验表明SDP在五个基准测试中表现最佳，尤其在长序列任务中优势更明显。此外，SDP生成的轨迹支持多种分析，如谓词信用分配、故障定位、部分进度测量和模块化操作替换。

item.title: 基于结构因果模型的因果图推断研究
item.content:encoded:
两组辅助环境可推断任意非线性机制的因果图。



摘要： 本文研究了因果发现问题，即从数据中推断因果方向。通过结构因果模型（SCM）的框架，作者证明在假设因果关系无环且跨多个环境保持不变的前提下，仅需两个辅助环境即可推断出任意非线性机制的因果图。此外，该结论进一步表明，这种设定可以保证SCM功能机制的可识别性，并确保正确的反事实推断。研究通过合成数据验证了理论结果的正确性。

item.title: Qwen-Image-2.0：统一高保真生成与精准编辑的图像生成模型
item.content:encoded:
Qwen-Image-2.0提升图像生成与编辑能力，支持多语言和复杂指令。



摘要： Qwen-Image-2.0是一款集成高保真图像生成与精准编辑能力的通用图像生成基础模型。针对现有模型在超长文本渲染、多语言排版、高分辨率写实、指令遵循及高效部署等方面的不足，该模型通过结合Qwen3-VL作为条件编码器，并采用多模态扩散Transformer进行联合建模，实现了强大的多模态理解与灵活生成能力。它支持长达1000个token的指令，适用于幻灯片、海报、信息图和漫画等文本丰富的场景，显著提升了多语言文本质量和排版效果，同时增强了写实图像的细节、纹理和光照一致性，能够更可靠地响应多样化的复杂提示。

item.title: DBS-Adam优化器在事故伤害严重性预测中的应用
item.content:encoded:
DBS-Adam优化器提升不平衡序列数据的模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为动态批次敏感Adam（DBS-Adam）的优化器，通过动态调整学习率来提高模型在不平衡和序列数据上的训练稳定性和收敛速度。该方法基于梯度范数和批次损失的指数移动平均计算批次难度得分。实验中，DBS-Adam与双向LSTM结合用于事故伤害严重性预测，并通过SMOTE-ENN和焦点损失处理类别不平衡问题。对比实验显示，DBS-Adam在多个指标上优于现有优化器，表现出更高的准确率、精确率和F1分数，验证了其在实时事故分类中的有效性。

item.title: 生物医学多跳问答任务与MedHopQA挑战分析
item.content:encoded:
MedHopQA挑战评估了多跳问答系统性能，RAG策略表现优异。



摘要： 本文介绍了BioCreative IX MedHopQA共享任务，旨在评估大语言模型在生物医学领域的多跳推理能力。该任务构建了一个包含1000个复杂问答对的新型数据集，特别关注罕见疾病。每个问题需要整合两个独立维基页面的信息进行两跳推理。共有13个团队提交了48份方案，系统通过表面字符串比较和概念准确性（MedCPT）进行评估。结果显示，增强系统性能显著优于基线模型，最高F1得分为89.30%，EM得分为87.30%。研究发现，检索增强生成（RAG）等策略对提升性能至关重要，同时概念级评估能更准确地衡量答案质量。MedHopQA数据集已公开，以支持后续研究。

item.title: 从ZPD到LLM：人类互动与认知工具的再思考
item.content:encoded:
LLM通过交互实现类似人类的思维，而非分布式表示。



摘要： 文章回顾了维果茨基关于意识和儿童发展的研究，提出儿童通过与文化成人互动在最近发展区中发展科学思维。文章进一步指出，大语言模型并非通过分布式表示进行思考，而是以“原始思维”方式进行实践性处理。作者认为，这些模型不产生幻觉，而是在“做梦”，关键在于探索支持常识行为的认知工具。文章强调，互动是人类沟通的核心，而非理解的附加部分。

item.title: 基于ViT的深度伪造检测漏洞分析与对抗攻击研究
item.content:encoded:
ViT检测系统存在可被利用的脆弱性，SIAA攻击效果接近白盒。



摘要： 随着AI生成图像越来越逼真，视觉Transformer（ViT）成为深度伪造检测的核心技术。然而，依赖预训练模型的检测系统存在潜在漏洞。本文提出一种灰盒攻击方法——代理迭代对抗攻击（SIAA），仅利用检测器的ViT主干知识，在特征空间内生成高效对抗样本。实验表明，该攻击在多种ViT检测器和灰盒场景中均表现出高成功率，接近白盒攻击效果，揭示了仅掌握主干知识即可破坏检测可靠性，凸显了对抗多媒体取证中防御机制的重要性。

item.title: LLM自主代理行为越狱风险评估与LITMUS基准测试
item.content:encoded:
LLM代理存在行为越狱风险，LITMUS提供安全评估基准。



摘要： 文章指出，基于大语言模型的自主代理在真实操作系统环境中可能引发不可逆的安全风险，即行为越狱。现有评估基准仅关注语义层安全，忽视了物理层危害。为此，研究者提出LITMUS基准，通过语义-物理双重验证机制和系统状态回滚，全面评估代理行为安全性。该基准包含819个高风险测试用例，涵盖三种攻击方式，并配备自动化多代理评估框架。实验发现，当前代理缺乏有效安全意识，且存在执行幻觉现象，即口头拒绝但实际执行危险操作。研究揭示了代理在技能注入和实体包装攻击下的脆弱性，LITMUS为真实操作系统中LLM代理的行为安全评估提供了首个标准化平台。

item.title: 基于经验框架的长时序图像编辑方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合规划与奖励驱动执行的图像编辑框架，提升多步骤指令的编辑效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种用于长时序图像编辑的经验框架，通过规划器生成结构化的原子分解步骤，协调器选择工具和区域执行每一步操作。视觉语言评判器提供基于结果的奖励，以确保指令遵循性和视觉质量。协调器通过最大化这些奖励进行训练，并利用成功轨迹优化规划器。该方法通过将规划与奖励驱动执行紧密结合，相较于单步或基于规则的多步基线方法，能够生成更连贯和可靠的编辑结果。

item.title: 基于自监督学习的多导联心电图分类方法ECG-NAT
item.content:encoded:
ECG-NAT通过多尺度特征提取实现高效准确的心电图分类。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为ECG-NAT的新型自监督学习方法，用于多导联心电图（ECG）分类。该方法通过两阶段训练流程，首先进行生成式预训练，利用掩码自编码器从无标签数据中学习鲁棒的特征表示；随后通过双损失函数进行判别性微调，提升分类性能。其分层注意力机制能高效捕捉从局部心跳形态到整体节律模式的多尺度时间特征，在仅使用1%标注数据的情况下，达到88.1%的准确率，展现出在低资源环境下的强大性能。该框架兼顾分类效果与计算效率，适用于实时心电诊断。

item.title: 无需初始模型的主动学习方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出一种无需初始模型的主动学习方法，提升效率与灵活性。



摘要： 本文探讨了在不依赖初始候选模型的情况下，使用随机初始化的CNN和Transformer进行主动学习的可行性。通过评估三种基于置信度的采样策略（高置信度、低置信度以及高低置信度组合），实验表明低置信度策略表现最佳，证明其在减少标注负担的同时仍能获得良好效果。研究验证了该方法在不同数据集和领域的有效性，为传统主动学习框架提供了更高效、灵活的替代方案。

item.title: 基于最小描述长度的粒球分类器研究
item.content:encoded:
MDL-GBC提升粒球分类的透明度与边界敏感性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于最小描述长度（MDL）的粒球分类器（MDL-GBC），旨在提高粒球分类的透明度和边界敏感区域的建模能力。该方法将类条件粒球构建视为一个局部模型选择问题，通过正类证据和负类边界证据进行比较，采用统一的描述长度准则评估三种候选解释模型。实验表明，MDL-GBC在18个基准数据集上表现出色，具有较高的准确率和F1值，是一种有效且可解释的分类方法。

item.title: Generating HDR Video from SDR Video
item.content:encoded: The high dynamic range (HDR) video ecosystem is approaching maturity, but the problem of upconverting legacy standard dynamic range (SDR) videos persists without a convincing solution. We propose a framework for HDR video synthesis from casual SDR footage by leveraging large-scale generative video models. We introduce a Multi-Exposure Video Model (MEVM) that can predict exposure-bracketed linear SDR video sequences from a single nonlinear SDR video input. We further propose a learnable Video Merging Model (VMM) that merges the predicted exposure-bracketed video into a high-quality HDR sequence while preserving detail in both shadows and highlights. Extensive experiments, quantitative and qualitative evaluation, and a user study demonstrate that our approach enables robust HDR conversion for in-the-wild examples from casual consumer videos and even iconic films. Finally, our model can support HDR synthesis pipelines built upon existing SDR generative video models. Output HDR videos can be viewed on our supplementary webpage: sdr2hdrvideo.github.io

item.title: MAP: A Map-then-Act Paradigm for Long-Horizon Interactive Agent Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Current interactive LLM agents rely on goal-conditioned stepwise planning, where environmental understanding is acquired reactively during execution rather than established beforehand. This temporal inversion leads to Delayed Environmental Perception: agents must infer environmental constraints through trial-and-error, resulting in an Epistemic Bottleneck that traps them in inefficient failure cycles. Inspired by human affordance perception and cognitive map theory, we propose the Map-then-Act Paradigm (MAP), a plug-and-play framework that shifts environment understanding before execution. MAP consists of three stages: (1) Global Exploration, acquiring environment-general priors; (2) Task-Specific Mapping, constructing a structured cognitive map; and (3) Knowledge-Augmented Execution, solving tasks grounded on the map. Experiments show consistent gains across benchmarks and LLMs. On ARC-AGI-3, MAP enables frontier models to surpass near-zero baseline performance in 22 of 25 game environments. We further introduce MAP-2K, a dataset of map-then-act trajectories, and show that training on it outperforms expert execution traces, suggesting that understanding environments is more fundamental than imitation.

item.title: SemaTune: Semantic-Aware Online OS Tuning with Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: Online OS tuning can improve long-running services, but existing controllers are poorly matched to live hosts. They treat scheduler, power, memory, and I/O controls as black-box variables and optimize a scalar reward. This view ignores cross-knob policy structure, breaks down when application metrics are unavailable, and can send a running service into degraded regions that persist after the bad setting is removed. We present SemaTune, a host-side framework for steady-state OS tuning with bounded language-model guidance. SemaTune turns knob schemas, telemetry, current configuration, recent action--response history, and retrieved prior runs into a compact decision context. A fast loop proposes low-latency updates, a slower loop periodically revises the search strategy, and every proposed change passes through typed validation before reaching kernel or sysctl interfaces. This lets the controller reason about OS-control meaning and indirect performance signals while keeping model cost, latency, and authority constrained. We evaluate SemaTune on 13 live workloads from five benchmark suites while tuning up to 41 Linux parameters. Across the suite, SemaTune improves stable-phase performance by 72.5\% over default settings and by 153.3\% relative to the strongest non-LLM baseline. A 30-window session costs about \$0.20 in model calls. With only host-level metrics, SemaTune still outperforms baselines given direct application objectives by 93.7 percentage points, while avoiding severe degraded regions reached by structure-blind exploration.

item.title: When Absolute State Fails: Evaluating Proprioceptive Encodings for Robust Manipulation
item.content:encoded: As end-to-end robotic policies are progressively deployed in the real world to solve real tasks, they face a gap between the training and inference conditions. Scaling the amount and diversity of the training data has shown some success in improving zero-shot generalization, yet robots still fail when faced with new, unseen test conditions. For instance, while robots with fixed frames of reference are common, those with moving frames pose a greater challenge for deployment. To address this specific instance of the issue, we present a study of strategies for encoding the robot's proprioceptive state to improve both in- and out-of-distribution performance at test time. Through a systematic study of joint representations, we find that a simple episode-wise relative frame provides the best trade-off between task performance and robustness, outperforming the baselines in extensive real-robot experiments conducted in a realistic test environment. The results suggest a practical path to leveraging data collected by robots with varying frames of reference and deployment to unseen test configurations.

item.title: DeepTokenEEG Enhancing Mild Cognitive Impairment and Alzheimers Classification via Tokenized EEG Features
item.content:encoded: The detection of Alzheimers disease (AD) is considered crucial, as timely intervention can improve patient outcomes. Electroencephalogram (EEG)-based diagnosis has been recognized as a non-invasive, accessible, and cost-effective approach for AD detection; however, it faces challenges related to data availability, accuracy of modern deep learning methods, and the time-consuming nature of expert-based interpretation. In this study, a novel lightweight and high-performance model, DeepTokenEEG, was designed for the diagnosis of AD and the classification of EEG signals from AD patients, individuals with other neurological conditions, and healthy subjects. Unlike traditional heavy-weight models, DeepTokenEEG ultilizes spatial and temporal tokenizer that effectively captures AD-related biomarkers in both temporal and frequency domain with only 0.29 million paramaters. Trained in a combined dataset of 274 subjects, including 180 AD cases, and 94 healthy controls, the proposed method achieves a maximum recorded accuracy of 100% on specific frequency bands, representing an improvement of 1.41-15.35% over state-of-the-art methods on the same dataset. These results indicate the potential of DeepTokenEEG for early detection and screening of AD, with promising applicability for deployment due to its compact size.

item.title: Does RAG Know When Retrieval Is Wrong? Diagnosing Context Compliance under Knowledge Conflict
item.content:encoded: The Context-Compliance Regime in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) occurs when retrieved context dominates the final answer even when it conflicts with the model's parametric knowledge. Accuracy alone does not reveal how retrieved context causally shapes answers under such conflict. We introduce Context-Driven Decomposition (CDD), a belief-decomposition probe that operates at inference time and serves as an intervention mechanism for controlled retrieval conflict. Across Epi-Scale stress tests, TruthfulQA misconception injection, and cross- model reruns, CDD exposes three patterns. P1: context compliance is measurable in an upper-bound adversarial setting, where Standard RAG reaches 15.0% accuracy on TruthfulQA misconception injection (N=500). P2: adversarial accuracy gains transfer across model families: CDD improves accuracy on Gemini-2.5-Flash and on Claude Haiku/Sonnet/Opus, but rationale-answer causal coupling does not transfer. CDD reaches 64.1% mistake- injection causal sensitivity on Gemini-2.5-Flash, while sensitivities for all three Claude variants fall in the [-3%, +7%] range, suggesting that the Claude-side accuracy gains operate through a mechanism distinct from the explicit conflict-resolution trace. P3: explicit conflict decomposition improves robustness under temporal drift and noisy distractors, with CDD reaching 71.3% on temporal shifts and 69.9% on distractor evidence on the full Epi-Scale adversarial benchmark. These three patterns identify context-compliance as a structural axis along which standard RAG can be probed and intervened on, distinct from retrieval-quality or single-method robustness questions, and motivate releasing Epi-Scale for systematic study across model families and retrieval pipelines.

item.title: Effective Context in Transformers: An Analysis of Fragmentation and Tokenization
item.content:encoded: Transformers predict over a representation of a sequence. The same data can be written as bytes, characters, or subword tokens, and these representations may be lossless. Yet, under a fixed context window, they need not expose the same information to the model. This raises a basic question: how does the choice of representation change what a finite-context predictor can achieve? We study this question on Markov sources and uncover two complementary phenomena. First, we observe that moving to smaller representation units can hurt prediction even when the context window is enlarged to cover the relevant source history. To explain this, we introduce fragmentation: a lossless recoding that replaces each source symbol by several smaller units. We prove that fragmentation can strictly increase the optimal finite-context log-loss, showing that the gap is not merely an optimization or capacity issue, but can be intrinsic to the representation. This gives a theoretical account of the finite-context gap observed in byte- and character-level models such as ByT5 and CANINE relative to subword-tokenized models. Second, we study the opposite direction: greedy tokenization -- BPE, WordPiece, and related methods -- which groups source symbols into larger units. We show that tokenization can make a short token window behave like a longer source-context window, and we give a loss guarantee describing when this is achievable. The guarantee depends on how reliably token windows span the needed source history, together with the compression rate of the tokenizer. This also yields a simple diagnostic for real tokenizers: measuring how much source context a fixed token window reliably contains. Together, the two directions establish a finite-context information-theoretic framework for reasoning about representation choices in Transformers.

item.title: Synthesizing POMDP Policies: Sampling Meets Model-checking via Learning
item.content:encoded: Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes (POMDPs) are the standard framework for decision-making under uncertainty. While sampling-based methods scale well, they lack formal correctness guarantees, making them unsuitable for safety-critical applications. Conversely, formal synthesis techniques provide correctness-by-construction but often struggle with scalability, as general POMDP synthesis is undecidable. To bridge this gap, we propose a synthesis framework that integrates sampling, automata learning, and model-checking. Inspired by Angluin's $L^*$ algorithm, our approach utilizes sampling as a membership oracle and model-checking as an equivalence oracle. This enables the synthesis of finite-state controllers with formal guarantees, provided the sampling-induced policy is regular. We establish a relative completeness result for this framework. Experimental results from our prototypical implementation demonstrate that this method successfully solves threshold-safety problems that remain challenging for existing formal synthesis tools. We believe our algorithm serves as a valuable component in a portfolio approach to tackling the inherent difficulty of POMDP synthesis problems.

item.title: H-OmniStereo: Zero-Shot Omnidirectional Stereo Matching with Heading-Aligned Normal Priors
item.content:encoded: Stereo matching on top-bottom equirectangular images provides an effective framework for full-surround perception, as vertically aligned epipolar lines enable the use of advanced perspective stereo architectures that are largely driven by large-scale datasets and monocular priors. However, the performance of such adaptations is severely limited by the scarcity of omnidirectional stereo datasets and the degradation of perspective monocular priors under spherical distortions.To address these challenges, we propose H-OmniStereo, a zero-shot omnidirectional stereo matching framework. First, we construct high-quality synthetic dataset comprising over 2.8 million top-bottom equirectangular stereo pairs to scale up training. Second, we introduce an equirectangular monocular normal estimator, specifically operating in a heading-aligned coordinate system. Beyond providing distortion-robust and cross-view-consistent geometric priors for establishing reliable correspondences in stereo matching, this design boosts training efficiency and accommodates train-test FoV mismatches.Extensive experiments show that our approach achieves higher accuracy than existing methods on out-of-domain datasets and successfully generalizes to real-world consumer camera setups using a single model. Both the model and the dataset will be open-sourced.

item.title: MetaMoE: Diversity-Aware Proxy Selection for Privacy-Preserving Mixture-of-Experts Unification
item.content:encoded: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models scale capacity by combining specialized experts, but most existing approaches assume centralized access to training data. In practice, data are distributed across clients and cannot be shared due to privacy constraints, making unified MoE training challenging. We propose MetaMoE, a privacy-preserving framework that unifies independently trained, domain-specialized experts into a single MoE using public proxy data as surrogates for inaccessible private data. Central to MetaMoE is diversity-aware proxy selection, which selects client-domain-relevant and diverse samples from public data to effectively approximate private data distributions and supervise router learning. These proxies are further used to align expert training, improving expert coordination at unification time, while a context-aware router enhances expert selection across heterogeneous inputs. Experiments on computer vision and natural language processing benchmarks demonstrate that MetaMoE consistently outperforms recent privacy-preserving MoE unification methods. Code is available at https://github.com/ws-jiang/MetaMoE.

item.title: Does language matter for spoken word classification? A multilingual generative meta-learning approach
item.content:encoded: Meta-learning has been shown to have better performance than supervised learning for few-shot monolingual spoken word classification. However, the meta-learning approach remains under-explored in multilingual spoken word classification. In this paper, we apply the Generative Meta-Continual Learning algorithm to spoken word classification. The generative nature of this algorithm makes it viable for use in application, and the meta-learning aspect promotes generalisation, which is crucial in a multilingual setting. We train monolingual models on English, German, French, and Catalan, a bilingual model on English and German, and a multilingual model on all four languages. We find that although the multilingual model performs best, the differences between model performance is unexpectedly low. We also find that the hours of unique data seen during training seems to be a stronger performance indicator than the number of languages included in the training data.

item.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in Large Language Model (LLM) agents have enabled complex agentic workflows where models autonomously retrieve information, call tools, and reason over large corpora to complete tasks on behalf of users. Despite the growing adoption of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) in agentic search systems, existing literature lacks a systematic comparison of how retrieval strategy choice interacts with agent architecture and tool-calling paradigm. Important practical dimensions, including how tool outputs are presented to the model and how performance changes when searches must cope with more irrelevant surrounding text, remain under-explored in agent loops. This paper reports an empirical study organized into two experiments. Experiment 1 compares grep and vector retrieval on a 116-question sample from LongMemEval, using a custom agent harness (Chronos) and provider-native CLI harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI), for both inline tool results and file-based tool results that the model reads separately. Experiment 2 compares grep-only and vector-only retrieval while progressively mixing in additional unrelated conversation history, so that each query is embedded in more distracting material alongside the passages that matter. Across Chronos and the provider CLIs, grep generally yields higher accuracy than vector retrieval in our comparisons in experiment 1; at the same time, overall scores still depend strongly on which harness and tool-calling style is used, even when the underlying conversation data are the same.

item.title: DocAtlas: Multilingual Document Understanding Across 80+ Languages
item.content:encoded: Multilingual document understanding remains limited for low-resource languages due to scarce training data and model-based annotation pipelines that perpetuate existing biases. We introduce DocAtlas, a framework that constructs high-fidelity OCR datasets and benchmarks covering 82 languages and 9 evaluation tasks. Our dual pipelines, differential rendering of native DOCX documents and synthetic LaTeX-based generation for right-to-left scripts produce precise structural annotations in a unified DocTag format encoding layout, text, and component types, without learned models for core annotation. Evaluating 16 state-of-the-art models reveals persistent gaps in low-resource scripts. We show that Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) using rendering-derived ground truth as positive signal achieves stable multilingual adaptation, improving both in-domain (+1.9%) and out-of-domain (+1.8%) accuracy without measurable base-language degradation, where supervised fine-tuning degrades out-of-domain performance by up to 21%. Our best variant, DocAtlas-DeepSeek, improves +1.7% over the strongest baseline.

item.title: Learning with Semantic Priors: Stabilizing Point-Supervised Infrared Small Target Detection via Hierarchical Knowledge Distillation
item.content:encoded: Single-frame Infrared Small Target Detection (ISTD) aims to localize weak targets under heavy background clutter, yet dense pixel-wise annotations are expensive. Point supervision with online label evolution reduces annotation cost; however, lightweight CNN detectors often lack sufficient semantics, leading to noisy pseudo-masks and unstable optimization. To address this, we propose a hierarchical VFM-driven knowledge distillation framework that uses a frozen Vision Foundation Model (VFM) during training. We formulate point-supervised learning as a bilevel optimization process: the inner loop adapts a VFM-embedded teacher on reweighted training samples, while the outer loop transfers validation-guided knowledge to a lightweight student to mitigate pseudo-label noise and training-set bias. We further introduce Semantic-Conditioned Affine Modulation (SCAM) to inject VFM semantics into CNN features at multiple layers. In addition, a dynamic collaborative learning strategy with cluster-level sample reweighting enhances robustness to imperfect pseudo-masks. Experiments on diverse challenging cases across multiple ISTD backbones demonstrate consistent improvements in detection accuracy and training stability. Our code is available at https://github.com/yuanhang-yao/semantic-prior.

item.title: AIMing for Standardised Explainability Evaluation in GNNs: A Framework and Case Study on Graph Kernel Networks
item.content:encoded: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have advanced significantly in handling graph-structured data, but a comprehensive framework for evaluating explainability remains lacking. Existing evaluation frameworks primarily involve post-hoc explanations, and operate in the setting where multiple methods generate a suite of explanations for a single model. This makes comparison of explanations across models difficult. Evaluation of inherently interpretable models often targets a specific aspect of interpretability relevant to the model, but remains underdeveloped in terms of generating insight across a suite of measures. We introduce AIM, a comprehensive framework that addresses these limitations by measuring Accuracy, Instance-level explanations, and Model-level explanations. AIM is formulated with minimal constraints to enhance flexibility and facilitate broad applicability. Here, we use AIM in a pipeline, extracting explanations from inherently interpretable GNNs such as graph kernel networks (GKNs) and prototype networks (PNs), evaluating these explanations with AIM, identifying their limitations and obtaining insights to their characteristics. Taking GKNs as a case study, we show how the insights obtained from AIM can be used to develop an updated model, xGKN, that maintains high accuracy while demonstrating improved explainability. Our approach aims to advance the field of Explainable AI (XAI) for GNNs, providing more robust and practical solutions for understanding and improving complex models.

item.title: Causal Forcing++：帧级自回归视频生成的高效方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Causal Forcing++，提升帧级自回归视频生成效率与质量。



摘要： 本文研究了帧级自回归视频生成方法，针对现有方法在初始化阶段的瓶颈，提出了Causal Forcing++。该方法通过因果一致性蒸馏（causal CD）实现高效的少步数初始化，避免预计算和存储完整轨迹的需求，从而提升生成效率和优化难度。实验表明，在帧级两步设置下，该方法在多个指标上优于当前最佳方法，并显著降低了首帧延迟和训练成本。此外，该方法还被扩展用于动作条件下的世界模型生成。

item.title: 基于蒙特卡洛启发的分析先验提升胶囊内镜分类性能
item.content:encoded:
蒙特卡洛模型提升RGB图像中血红蛋白检测精度。



摘要： 本文研究了基于蒙特卡洛启发的分析先验模型在胶囊内镜图像分类中的应用。通过在Kvasir-Capsule数据集上测试两种软件配置，发现该模型在多个种子下均能提升宏观AUC值，特别是在淋巴管扩张类别的检测中效果显著。此外，蒸馏变体方法在纯RGB图像上运行，并提供可解释的热图。尽管部分罕见血管病变类别表现波动较大，但整体结果表明该方法具有一定的改进潜力。

item.title: 基于时间策略组合的最优决策方法研究
item.content:encoded:
通过时间组合策略提升马尔可夫决策过程的最优解性能。



摘要： 本文研究了在奖励函数随时间变化的马尔可夫决策过程中，子策略的时间拼接问题。作者引入了通用迪杰斯特拉搜索（GDS），证明了通过组合中间最优子策略可以恢复全局最优目标策略。受GDS“搜索、选择、更新”原理启发，提出了动态潜在路由（DLR）方法，该方法在单次训练阶段中联合学习离散潜在代码、路由策略和模型参数。实验表明，在低数据微调设置下，DLR在四个数据集和六个模型上表现优于监督微调，平均提升6.6个百分点，而先前的离散潜在基线方法则持续表现不佳。机制分析和代码消融实验显示，DLR学习到了具有明确因果作用的结构化路由行为。

item.title: MIRAI：一种统一评估表格模型综合责任与完整性的框架
item.content:encoded:
MIRAI统一评估表格模型的完整性与责任性，超越预测性能。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为MIRAI的统一评估框架，用于在高风险表格领域中评估人工智能模型的解释性、公平性、鲁棒性、隐私性和可持续性。该框架通过标准化和方向对齐的维度评分，将多个指标整合为一个综合分数，使不同架构和计算配置的模型能够直接比较。实验表明，预测性能高的模型未必在整体责任与完整性上表现更好，有时简单模型反而在多维平衡上更优。MIRAI为监管环境下的负责任模型选择提供了实用基础。

item.title: 基于正负样本的不平衡数据学习方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新方法处理高度不平衡数据中的正例与未标记数据。



摘要： 本文提出了一种新的从正例和未标记数据中学习的方法，用于处理高度不平衡的数据集。许多现实问题如疾病基因识别、目标营销、欺诈检测和推荐系统，由于标注数据有限而难以用传统机器学习方法解决。训练数据通常包含正例和未标记实例，其中未标记数据主要为负例，但也包含一些正例。尽管PU学习已有深入研究，但针对不平衡情况或难以检测的正例仍缺乏有效方法。本文方法利用聚焦的经验风险估计器，结合正例和未标记数据训练二分类器，在两种标注机制下（完全随机选择正例和随机选择正例）均表现出色。此外，该方法在财务错误报告检测等实际应用中也展现出价值。

item.title: CalibAnyView：多视角相机标定的统一方法
item.content:encoded:
CalibAnyView实现多视角相机标定，提升几何一致性与鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文提出CalibAnyView，一种支持任意数量输入视角的统一相机标定方法。该方法通过显式建模跨视角几何一致性，提高标定精度与鲁棒性。研究构建了一个大规模多视角视频数据集，涵盖多种真实场景和镜头畸变，并基于此开发了多视角Transformer模型，用于预测密集透视场，再结合几何优化框架联合估计相机内参和重力方向。实验表明，CalibAnyView在单视角和多视角设置下均优于现有方法，为野外环境中的3D重建和机器人感知提供可靠基础。

item.title: 基于预填适配器的高效大语言模型个性化方法
item.content:encoded:
PreFT提升多适配器服务吞吐量，性能损失小。



摘要： 本文提出PreFT（预填仅适配）方法，通过仅在预填阶段应用适配器，显著提高多用户个性化大语言模型的服务吞吐量。实验表明，在Llama 3.1 70B模型上，PreFT的吞吐量是传统PEFT的1.9倍。尽管在监督微调任务中PreFT的评估损失略高，但可通过增加秩来补偿，同时保持高吞吐量。在强化学习任务中，PreFT表现与传统PEFT相当。该研究验证了PreFT在个性化服务中的准确率与吞吐量之间的更优权衡。

item.title: 任务感知层剪枝对分布外数据的提升机制研究
item.content:encoded:
任务感知层剪枝提升分布外数据性能，与几何特性相关。



摘要： 本文研究任务感知层剪枝（TALE）在特定任务中的表现，发现其在分布内数据上无提升，但在分布外数据上显著提高准确率。通过分析层间范数和成对距离，发现分布外输入会引入与分布内不同的几何特征，而任务感知剪枝能识别并移除导致这种偏差的层，从而将分布外表示重新对齐到任务适应的几何结构，提升模型性能。实验验证了这一机制在不同模型规模下的有效性。

item.title: 基于多智能体递归思维的微服务系统根因定位方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出RCLAgent框架提升微服务系统根因定位准确性和效率。



摘要： 随着微服务系统复杂度增加，故障频率上升，准确的根因定位对系统可靠性至关重要。传统机器学习和深度学习方法存在可解释性差和迁移能力弱的问题，而现有大语言模型方法面临上下文爆炸和串行推理结构的限制。本文通过分析实际SRE操作，提出RCLAgent框架，采用多智能体递归思维与并行推理机制，通过分解诊断过程、递归组织智能体并生成全局证据图，显著提升了定位准确率和推理效率。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中优于现有技术。

item.title: 基于BSDE的深度学习方法优化与偏差分析
item.content:encoded:
研究BSDE方法在求解高维PDE中的偏差问题并提出改进方案。



摘要： 本文探讨了基于反向随机微分方程（BSDE）的深度学习方法在解决高维偏微分方程（PDE）中的应用。传统欧拉-玛丽亚姆（EM）时间离散化方法会引入训练损失偏差，虽然高阶方法如Heun可以消除偏差，但会重新引入二阶空间导数并增加计算开销。本文对EM引起的偏差进行了系统分析，并提出了一种无偏且无需二阶导数的训练框架，保留了BSDE方法的计算优势。相关代码已开源。

item.title: Cattle Trade：评估大语言模型在复杂经济博弈中的战略推理能力
item.content:encoded:
Cattle Trade基准测试LLMs在多智能体经济博弈中的战略推理与资源管理能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了Cattle Trade，一个用于评估大语言模型（LLMs）作为代理在不完全信息、对抗互动和资源限制环境下进行战略推理的多智能体基准。该基准结合拍卖、隐藏报价交易挑战、讨价还价、虚张声势、对手建模和资源分配，形成一个持续50至60回合的长期游戏。不同于以往仅测试单一能力的基准，Cattle Trade评估代理是否能在竞争性多智能体经济游戏中综合运用多种技能。研究对七种成本高效的LLMs和三种确定性代码代理进行了242场游戏测试，发现战略连贯性（如支出效率、资源纪律和阶段适应性出价）比支出总量或单一子技能更能预测排名。两个启发式代码代理表现优于大多数LLMs，行为轨迹揭示了LLMs常见的失败模式，如过度出价、自我出价、破产交易挑战启动及对手状态适应能力弱。

item.title: 基于局部时空卷积网络的步态识别方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出LSTCN模型提升步态识别准确率。



摘要： 本文针对步态识别中视频数据复杂性和外部干扰因素带来的挑战，提出一种结构简单但高效的局部时空卷积网络（LSTCN）。该方法通过引入全局双向空间池化机制和局部时空卷积层，有效提取步态的时空特征，并利用非对称卷积核增强特征表示能力，从而提升识别性能。

item.title: 词汇难度预测模型的比较与分析
item.content:encoded:
两种词汇难度预测模型在准确性和可解释性上表现优异。



摘要： 本文介绍了两种用于词汇难度预测的模型：一种是高精度的黑盒模型，在开放赛道中取得最佳成绩；另一种是可解释模型，其性能优于微调编码器基线。黑盒模型通过软目标损失函数微调大语言模型，在评分任务中实现了r > 0.91的相关性。可解释模型不仅保持了强相关性（r > 0.77），还提供了对词汇难度影响因素的洞察。分析表明，英国文化协会的知识型词汇列表中的词汇难度常受拼写难度或测试题构造的影响，而不仅仅是词汇本身的产出难度。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于神经网络的多路径随机旅行商问题求解方法
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种新方法解决多路径随机旅行商问题。



摘要： 本文研究了在智能城市和城市物流中常见的多路径随机旅行商问题，该问题涉及在多个路径间选择最优路线以最小化期望总成本。文章采用两阶段随机规划方法，第一阶段确定访问顺序，第二阶段根据实时交通状况选择最佳路径。为减少计算负担，引入神经网络代理模型来近似第二阶段的期望值。不同神经网络架构和训练策略被提出并评估，结果表明该方法在计算效率、解质量及泛化能力方面表现优异，适用于复杂不确定性下的车辆路径问题。

item.title: 基于流匹配的自动驾驶控制轨迹生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种直接生成控制轨迹的自动驾驶规划器，适用于多种场景。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于流匹配的自动驾驶规划器，直接输出由加速度和曲率组成的可操作控制轨迹。该模型以鸟瞰图（BEV）为输入，在少量常微分方程（ODE）积分步骤中生成控制序列，实现低延迟推理，适合实时闭环重规划。模型仅在意大利帕尔马市的真实城市场景中训练，并在包括多车道高速公路和未见过的城市场景在内的多种环境中进行评估。结果表明，模型在未见条件下仍能可靠泛化，保持稳定的闭环控制并成功完成与训练分布差异较大的场景。这得益于BEV表示的几何中心视角以及流匹配公式在分布偏移下的优雅退化特性。

item.title: GeoFuse：融合道路地图的无人机图像地理定位方法
item.content:encoded:
GeoFuse通过融合道路地图提升无人机图像在恶劣天气下的定位精度。



摘要： 本文提出GeoFuse，一种融合卫星图像与道路地图的跨模态框架，以提升无人机图像在恶劣天气条件下的地理定位性能。通过引入精确对齐的道路地图数据，GeoFuse增强了模型对天气变化的鲁棒性。该方法采用令牌级和通道级特征融合，并结合动态门控机制自适应加权不同模态信息。最终通过类别级对比学习优化跨视角对齐。实验表明，GeoFuse在University-1652和DenseUAV基准上分别提升了3.46%和23.18%的Recall@1准确率，优于现有方法。

item.title: CLVR框架提升文本到图像生成的复杂性和效率
item.content:encoded:
CLVR框架提升文本到图像生成的复杂性和效率。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为CLVR的闭环视觉推理框架，旨在解决当前文本到图像生成模型在处理复杂语义和优化稳定性方面的不足。该框架通过将视觉语言逻辑规划与像素级扩散生成深度结合，引入了自动化数据引擎和代理提示强化学习（PPRL），以提高推理轨迹的可靠性并解决长期上下文优化问题。此外，还提出了Δ-空间权重合并方法（DSWM），有效降低了迭代去噪带来的延迟问题，使每步推理成本降至4个NFE，同时保持高质量生成效果。实验表明，CLVR在多个基准测试中表现优于现有开源基线，并接近商业模型性能。

item.title: 机器人镜像触觉感知技术研究
item.content:encoded:
研究展示如何通过视觉与触觉对齐使机器人具备镜像触觉能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了通过视觉与触觉信息的对齐，使机器人能够模拟人类的镜像触觉现象。研究提出Mirror Touch Net框架，通过语义、分布和几何约束实现视觉与触觉表示的多层级对齐，从而从RGB图像中预测机器人手部1140个触点的毫米级触觉信号。实验表明，这种对齐方式重塑了视觉表征，使其与触觉流形一致，简化了跨模态映射。该方法还可扩展至对人类手部的跨域观察，实现触觉预测与反射性响应，为具身智能与人机共情交互提供新路径。

item.title: 图像超分辨率中视觉伪影的显著性评估研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出伪影显著性概念，用于评估图像超分辨率中的视觉缺陷影响。



摘要： 本文针对图像超分辨率技术中存在的视觉伪影问题，提出“伪影显著性”作为新的评估指标，定义为观众注意到特定区域存在明显伪影的比例。研究设计了众包标注协议，并构建了SR-Prominence数据集，包含多个来源的3,935个伪影掩码。实验发现，传统全参考质量评估指标如SSIM和DISTS在局部显著性检测上表现优异，而无参考方法和专用检测器则在跨数据集泛化能力上存在不足。研究还提供了一种无需进一步众包即可对新指标进行基准测试的客观评分协议，推动超分辨率伪影评估从二元缺陷检测向感知影响转变。

item.title: 基于区域证据的疾病筛查框架EviScreen
item.content:encoded:
EviScreen通过历史病例证据提升疾病筛查的准确性和可解释性。



摘要： 文章提出EviScreen，一个基于区域证据的疾病筛查框架，旨在解决现有医学图像筛查模型可解释性差和性能不足的问题。该框架通过从双知识库中检索历史病例的区域证据，实现回溯性可解释性，并利用证据感知推理模块结合当前病例与历史证据进行预测，从而提高筛查性能。此外，EviScreen通过对比检索生成的异常图增强定位可解释性，而非依赖后验显著性图。实验表明，该方法在真实世界疾病筛查基准上表现优异，具有更高的特异性和临床召回率。

item.title: PDI-Bench：一种评估生成视频几何一致性的量化框架
item.content:encoded:
PDI-Bench用于评估生成视频的物理合理性，揭示传统指标未捕捉的几何缺陷。



摘要： 本文提出PDI-Bench（视角失真指数基准），用于量化评估生成视频中的几何一致性。该框架通过分割和点跟踪获取对象中心观察数据，再利用单目重建将其提升到三维世界坐标，并计算投影几何残差，以检测尺度-深度对齐、三维运动一致性和结构刚性等三个维度的失败模式。研究构建了PDI-Dataset，涵盖多种场景以测试这些几何约束。实验表明，PDI能够揭示主流视频生成模型中未被常见感知指标捕捉的特定几何问题，为物理合理视频生成提供诊断信号。

item.title: 基于概念级的视觉-语言模型去学习方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种概念级去学习框架，实现精准知识删除与语义保留。



摘要： 本文针对视觉-语言模型（VLMs）在去学习过程中难以精确删除目标知识的问题，提出了一种基于概念级的可解释去学习框架。该方法通过多模态大语言模型从遗忘集构建任务特定的概念词表，并将视觉表示分解为稀疏、非负的语义概念组合，从而提供细粒度的知识操作接口。基于此分解，方法将去学习过程转化为概念级优化，选择性抑制目标概念，同时保留实例内非目标语义和跨模态全局知识。实验表明，该方法在域内和域外遗忘场景中均能实现更全面的目标遗忘，同时更好地保留非目标知识，并保持与现有方法相当的模型性能。

item.title: 基于光谱先验的遥感图像生成方法AnyBand-Diff
item.content:encoded:
AnyBand-Diff通过物理引导实现高精度遥感图像生成。



摘要： 现有扩散模型在生成真实图像方面取得进展，但直接应用于遥感图像时忽视了物理规律，导致光谱失真和辐射不一致。本文提出AnyBand-Diff，一种基于光谱先验的扩散框架，通过双随机掩码策略恢复完整光谱信息，并引入物理引导采样机制确保辐射一致性。同时设计多尺度物理损失函数，在像素、区域和全局层面施加约束。实验表明，AnyBand-Diff有效提升了遥感图像生成的科学实用性。

item.title: ATLAS：一种结合代理推理与潜在推理的视觉推理框架
item.content:encoded:
ATLAS结合代理操作与潜在表示，提升视觉推理性能。



摘要： 本文提出ATLAS框架，通过引入功能性标记（functional token）实现代理操作与潜在视觉推理的结合。该标记无需视觉监督，可直接通过下一个词预测生成，避免了冗长的中间视觉内容生成。同时，ATLAS兼容传统的SFT和RL训练方式，无需架构修改。为解决强化学习中功能性标记稀疏问题，作者引入LA-GRPO方法，通过静态加权辅助目标稳定训练过程。实验表明，ATLAS在多个挑战性基准上表现优异，具有良好的可解释性，为未来视觉推理研究提供新方向。

item.title: 基于知识嵌入的车辆路径规划框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种知识嵌入的车辆路径规划框架，提升求解质量。



摘要： 本文针对具有复杂约束和多目标的车辆路径规划问题（CVRP），提出了一种融合知识的强化学习框架。该框架借鉴了先路线后分簇的启发式方法，并通过动态规划解决分簇子问题，以指导强化学习求解器进行路径构造。为应对问题分解带来的部分可观测性问题，引入了统一的历史增强上下文处理模块。实验表明，该框架在多种CVRP变体中均表现出优于现有学习方法的求解质量，与经典启发式方法的差距更小，展现出良好的泛化能力。

item.title: MARS：一种基于结构化记忆的智能推荐系统
item.content:encoded:
MARS通过结构化记忆提升个性化推荐效果。



摘要： 本文提出MARS（Memory-Augmented Agentic Recommender System），一种将推荐视为部分可观测问题的框架。该系统通过三层记忆结构——事件缓冲区、偏好记忆和用户档案，逐步抽象用户的噪声行为数据，形成对用户偏好的紧凑估计。MARS采用由大语言模型驱动的规划器，动态调度六种操作（提取、强化、弱化、整合、遗忘、重合成），替代传统的固定周期策略。实验表明，MARS在四个InstructRec基准数据集上表现优异，平均HR@1提升26.4%，NDCG@10提升10.3%。

item.title: NeuroAtlas：评估预训练模型在脑电领域的表现
item.content:encoded:
研究评估预训练模型在脑电领域表现，发现其效果有限。



摘要： 本文介绍了NeuroAtlas，一个涵盖42个脑电数据集和26万小时数据的大型脑电基准。研究对比了EEG专用模型与通用时间序列模型的表现，发现EEG专用模型并不总是优于通用模型。同时指出传统机器学习指标不足以评估临床实用性，提出了更合适的评估方法，如事件决策质量、睡眠图特征和脑龄差距等。研究还发现模型性能在不同领域内差异较大，表明当前模型尚未实现统一的脑电建模能力。NeuroAtlas为下一代统一脑电模型提供了数据和评估标准。

item.title: FEST：一种基于少量示范的强化学习算法
item.content:encoded:
FEST利用少量示范提升RLVR样本效率，效果显著。



摘要： 本文提出FEST，一种基于少量示范的强化学习算法，用于提升大型语言模型在复杂任务中的表现。传统RLVR在生成正确推理链时效率低下，而FEST通过引入监督信号、在线策略信号和衰减权重机制，在仅需128个示范的情况下取得了优异结果。实验表明，FEST在多个基准测试中优于基线方法，甚至在使用完整数据集时也能达到相近性能，有效降低了对大量示范数据的依赖。

item.title: EnergyLens：面向大语言模型推理的能效优化框架
item.content:encoded:
EnergyLens提升大模型推理能效，支持多GPU配置优化。



摘要： EnergyLens是一个端到端的大语言模型（LLM）推理能效优化框架。随着LLM规模扩大，预测和减少其能耗对可持续性和数据中心运营至关重要。现有方法要么需要生产级代码和昂贵的性能分析，要么无法准确捕捉多GPU能耗行为。EnergyLens通过基于einsum的接口，结合负载不平衡感知的MoE建模和经验驱动的通信能耗模型，有效解决这一问题。在Llama3和Qwen3-MoE上验证显示，其多GPU预填充和解码能耗的平均绝对百分比误差在9.25%至13.19%之间，揭示了不同配置间的显著能耗差异，并证明了计算-通信重叠优化的复杂性。

item.title: 基于语言反馈的变分策略蒸馏方法提升强化学习性能
item.content:encoded:
提出VPD框架解决RLVR中奖励稀疏问题，提升复杂推理任务表现。



摘要： 本文针对强化学习中因奖励信号稀疏导致的探索瓶颈问题，提出一种新的变分策略蒸馏（VPD）框架。该方法将语言反馈学习建模为变分期望最大化问题，通过动态优化教师策略和学生策略的协同进化，提高从文本反馈中提取可操作信号的能力。实验表明，VPD在科学推理和代码生成任务中优于传统RLVR和现有自蒸馏方法，并揭示了反馈驱动与环境驱动强化学习的性能边界。
