item.title: Phoenix-VL 1.5 Medium：面向新加坡的多模态大模型
item.content:encoded:
Phoenix-VL 1.5 Medium是面向新加坡的多模态大模型，表现优异。



摘要： Phoenix-VL 1.5 Medium是一款拥有1230亿参数的多模态、多语言基础模型，专为适应新加坡本地语言和文化而设计。该模型通过在本地化多模态语料库上进行持续预训练，并结合新加坡文化、法律和政策相关的数据进行后训练，实现了高性能表现。它在新加坡本地的多模态、法律和政府政策基准测试中表现领先，同时在全球通用多模态、多语言和STEM基准测试中也保持竞争力。研究还提出了一个新的评估体系，涵盖本地知识基准和机构对齐的行为与安全框架。

item.title: 基于立体视觉的机器人模仿学习框架StereoPolicy
item.content:encoded:
StereoPolicy提升机器人在复杂场景中的操作能力。



摘要： 本文提出StereoPolicy，一种利用同步立体图像对增强几何推理的视觉-运动策略学习框架。该方法无需显式3D重建或相机标定，通过预训练的2D视觉编码器处理每张图像，并通过立体Transformer融合特征，隐式捕捉空间对应和视差信息。StereoPolicy与扩散模型和预训练视觉-语言-动作策略兼容，在多个仿真基准中表现优于RGB、RGB-D、点云和多视角基线。此外，该框架在真实机器人实验中也表现出色，验证了立体视觉在机器人操作中的有效性。

item.title: MAGE：一种支持冻结模型演化的多智能体图引导框架
item.content:encoded:
MAGE通过知识图谱实现冻结模型的持续演化与学习。



摘要： 本文提出MAGE（多智能体图引导演化）框架，将自我知识外部化为包含四个子图的协同演化知识图谱。该框架通过经验子图存储教师编写的失败修正和学习者自身正确的推理轨迹，并在推理时作为任务条件指导冻结执行模型。在演化过程中，图结构、任务级搜索赌徒和技能级路由赌徒从同一奖励流中更新，而学习者的主干网络保持不变。实验表明，MAGE在多个基准测试中表现优异，且自采的成功轨迹与教师修正互补，分别在不同任务中发挥主要作用。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的系统辨识框架ASIA研究
item.content:encoded:
ASIA利用大语言模型实现系统辨识自动化，提升效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文介绍了ASIA框架，该框架通过大语言模型作为自主编码代理，自动完成系统辨识中的模型选择、训练算法和超参数调优，无需人工干预。研究在两个系统辨识基准上验证了ASIA的有效性，并分析了其搜索行为、发现的架构与训练策略以及模型质量。同时讨论了该方法的潜力与局限性，包括隐式测试泄漏、方法透明度降低和可复现性问题。

item.title: GuardAD：一种基于动态逻辑推理的自动驾驶安全防护机制
item.content:encoded:
GuardAD提升自动驾驶安全性，降低事故率32.07%



摘要： 本文提出GuardAD，一种适用于多模态大语言模型的自动驾驶安全防护机制。GuardAD通过将安全问题建模为动态的马尔可夫逻辑状态，结合神经符号逻辑形式化和n阶马尔可夫逻辑归纳，实现对复杂交通场景中潜在危险的持续推理。该方法不修改底层模型，而是通过逻辑驱动的动作修正提升安全性。实验表明，GuardAD在多个基准测试中显著降低事故率，同时小幅提升任务性能。封闭环路仿真和实车测试进一步验证了其有效性。

item.title: 多层安全框架防范LLM辅助的SQL注入攻击
item.content:encoded:
提出多层安全框架防范LLM辅助的SQL注入攻击。



摘要： 随着大语言模型（LLMs）的发展，自然语言接口在结构化数据库中的应用日益广泛。然而，这种交互方式也带来了新的安全风险，尤其是通过提示到SQL的转换过程可能放大SQL注入漏洞。本文提出了一种多层安全框架，包括前端提示净化、行为和语义异常检测模型以及基于特征的控制层，以检测和缓解LLM辅助的SQL注入攻击。通过在多种真实攻击场景下的评估，实验结果表明该框架具有高检测准确率和低误报率，显著提升了LLM驱动数据库应用的安全性。

item.title: 深度伪造检测新方法：基于Alpha混合假设的BlenD模型
item.content:encoded:
提出Alpha混合假设，提升深度伪造检测跨数据集性能。



摘要： 本文提出Alpha混合假设，认为当前帧级深度伪造检测器主要通过搜索Alpha混合痕迹来识别伪造内容，而非学习语义异常或生成指纹。实验验证了该假设，并引入BlenD方法，利用真实人脸图像与自混合图像增强数据集，在无需显式生成深度伪造图像的情况下，实现了跨数据集的最佳检测效果。研究还表明，结合混合搜索器和抗混合捷径模型可进一步提升检测性能，最终达到94.0%的AUROC。相关代码和模型将公开。

item.title: NCO: A Versatile Plug-in for Handling Negative Constraints in Decoding
item.content:encoded: Controlling Large Language Models (LLMs) to prevent the generation of undesirable content, such as profanity and personally identifiable information (PII), has become increasingly critical. While earlier approaches relied on post-processing or resampling, recent research has shifted towards constrained decoding methods that control outputs during generation to mitigate high computational costs and quality degradation. However, preventing multiple forbidden hard constraints or regex constraints from appearing anywhere in the output is computationally challenging. A straightforward solution is to convert these constraints into a single automaton that tracks all forbidden patterns during decoding, but this often becomes impractically large. Standard regex engines also do not readily support the operations needed to build such a constraint, such as complement and intersection. In order to address these limitations, we propose NCO, a decoding strategy that performs online pattern matching over finite hard constraints and regex constraints, reducing computational overhead without inducing state explosion. NCO is fully compatible with standard inference strategies, including various sampling methods and beam search, while also supporting soft masking for probabilistic suppression. We empirically demonstrate its effectiveness across practical tasks, including PII and profanity suppression. Our implementation is available at https://github.com/hyundong98/NCO-Decoding.git .

item.title: An Annotation Scheme and Classifier for Personal Facts in Dialogue
item.content:encoded: The advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) has enabled their application in personalized dialogue systems. We present an extended annotation scheme for personal fact classification that addresses limitations in existing approaches, particularly PeaCoK. Our scheme introduces new categories (Demographics, Possessions) and attributes (Duration, Validity, Followup) that enable structured storage, quality filtering, and identification of facts suitable for dialogue continuation. We manually annotated 2,779 facts from Multi-Session Chat and trained a multi-head classifier based on transformer encoders. Combined with the Gemma-300M encoder, the classifier achieves $81.6 \pm 2.6$\% macro F1, outperforming all few-shot LLM baselines (best: GPT-5.4-mini, 72.92\%) by nearly 9 percentage points while requiring substantially fewer computational resources. Error analysis reveals persistent challenges in semantic boundary disambiguation, temporal aspect interpretation, and pragmatic reasoning for followup assessment. The dataset\footnotemark[1] and classifier\footnotemark[2] are publicly available.

item.title: Explainability of Recurrent Neural Networks for Enhancing P300-based Brain-Computer Interfaces
item.content:encoded: Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) based on P300 event-related potentials offer promising applications in health, education, and assistive technologies. However, challenges related to inter- and intra-subject variability and the explainability of Deep Learning (DL) models limit their practical deployment. In this work, we present the Post-Recurrent Module (PRM), an additional layer designed to improve both performance and transparency, incorporated into a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) architecture for classifying P300 signals from EEG data. Our approach enables a dual analysis of spatio-temporal signals through both global and local explainability techniques, allowing us not only to identify the most relevant brain regions and critical time intervals involved in classification, but also to interpret model decisions in terms of spatio-temporal EEG patterns consistent with well-stablished neurophysiological descriptions of the P300. Experimental results show a 9\% improvement in performance over state of the art, while also revealing the importance of inter- and intra-subject variability, in alignment with established neuroscience literature. By making model decisions transparent and efficient, we present a framework for explainable EEG-based models. This framework is not limited to more efficient P300 detection, but can be generalized to a wide range of EEG-based tasks. Its ability to identify key spatial and temporal features makes it suitable for applications such as motor imagery, steady-state visual evoked potentials, and even cognitive workload assessment.

item.title: Position: Academic Conferences are Potentially Facing Denominator Gaming Caused by Fully Automated Scientific Agents
item.content:encoded: The implicit policy of maintaining relatively stable acceptance rates at top AI conferences, despite exponentially growing submissions, introduces a critical structural vulnerability. This position paper characterizes a new systemic threat we term Agentic Denominator Gaming, in which a malicious actor deploys AI agents to generate and submit a large volume of superficially plausible but low-quality papers. Crucially, their objective is not the acceptance of low-quality papers, but rather to inflate the submission denominator and overwhelm reviewing capacity. Under a relatively stable acceptance rate, this dilution can systematically increase the publication probability of a small, targeted set of legitimate papers. We analyze the practical feasibility of this threat and its broader consequences, including intensified reviewer burnout, degraded review quality, and the emergence of industrialized automated agent mills. Finally, we propose and evaluate a range of mitigation strategies, and argue that durable protection will require system-level policy and incentive reforms, rather than relying primarily on technical detection alone.

item.title: CausalGS: Learning Physical Causality of 3D Dynamic Scenes with Gaussian Representations
item.content:encoded: Learning a physical model from video data that can comprehend physical laws and predict the future trajectories of objects is a formidable challenge in artificial intelligence. Prior approaches either leverage various Partial Differential Equations (PDEs) as soft constraints in the form of PINN losses, or integrate physics simulators into neural networks; however, they often rely on strong priors or high-quality geometry reconstruction. In this paper, we propose CausalGS, a framework that learns the causal dynamics of complex dynamic 3D scenes solely from multi-view videos, while dispensing with the reliance on explicit priors. At its core is an inverse physics inference module that decouples the complex dynamics problem from the video into the joint inference of two factors: the initial velocity field representing the scene's kinematics, and the intrinsic material properties governing its dynamics. This inferred physical information is then utilized within a differentiable physics simulator to guide the learning process in a physics-regularized manner. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CausalGS surpasses the state-of-the-art on the highly challenging task of long-term future frame extrapolation, while also exhibiting advanced performance in novel view interpolation. Crucially, our work shows that, without any human annotation, the model is able to learn the complex interactions between multiple physical properties and understand the causal relationships driving the scene's dynamic evolution, solely from visual observations.

item.title: A Recursive Decomposition Framework for Causal Structure Learning in the Presence of Latent Variables
item.content:encoded: Constraint-based causal discovery is widely used for learning causal structures, but heavy reliance on conditional independence (CI) testing makes it computationally expensive in high-dimensional settings. To mitigate this limitation, many divide-and-conquer frameworks have been proposed, but most assume causal sufficiency, i.e., no latent variables. In this paper, we show that divide-and-conquer strategies can be theoretically generalized beyond causal sufficiency to settings with latent variables. Specifically, we propose a recursive decomposition framework, termed DiCoLa, that enables divide-and-conquer causal discovery in the presence of latent variables. It recursively decomposes the global learning task into smaller subproblems and integrates their solutions through a principled reconstruction step to recover the global structure. We theoretically establish the soundness and completeness of the proposed framework. Extensive experiments on synthetic data demonstrate that our approach significantly improves computational efficiency across a range of causal discovery algorithms, while experiments on a real-world dataset further illustrate its practical effectiveness.

item.title: CapVector: Learning Transferable Capability Vectors in Parametric Space for Vision-Language-Action Models
item.content:encoded: This paper proposes a novel approach to address the challenge that pretrained VLA models often fail to effectively improve performance and reduce adaptation costs during standard supervised finetuning (SFT). Some advanced finetuning methods with auxiliary training objectives can improve performance and reduce the number of convergence steps. However, they typically incur significant computational overhead due to the additional losses from auxiliary objectives. To simultaneously achieve the enhanced capabilities of auxiliary training with the simplicity of standard SFT, we decouple the two objectives of auxiliary-objective SFT within the parameter space, namely, enhancing general capabilities and fitting task-specific action distributions. To deliver the goal, we only need to train the model to converge on a small-scale task set using two distinct training strategies, resulting in two finetuned models. The parameters' difference between the two models can then be interpreted as capability vectors provided by auxiliary objectives. These vectors are then merged with pretrained parameters to form a capability-enhanced meta model. Moreover, when standard SFT is augmented with a lightweight orthogonal regularization loss, the merged model attains performance comparable to auxiliary finetuned baselines with reduced computational overhead. Internal and external experiments demonstrate that our capability vectors (1) are effective and versatile across diverse models, (2) can generalize to novel environments and embodiments out of the box.

item.title: V4FinBench: Benchmarking Tabular Foundation Models, LLMs, and Standard Methods on Corporate Bankruptcy Prediction
item.content:encoded: Corporate bankruptcy prediction is a high-stakes financial task characterized by severe class imbalance and multi-horizon forecasting demands. Public datasets supporting it remain scarce and small: widely used free benchmarks contain between 6,000 and 80,000 company-year observations, while larger resources are behind subscription paywalls. To address this gap, we introduce V4FinBench, a benchmark of over one million company-year records from the Visegràd Group (V4) economies (2006-2021), with 131 financial and non-financial features, six prediction horizons, and a composite distress criterion jointly capturing solvency, profitability, and liquidity deterioration. V4FinBench is designed to support the evaluation of tabular and foundation-model methods under realistic class imbalance, with positive rates between 0.19% and 0.36%. We provide reference evaluations of standard tabular baselines, finetuned TabPFN, and QLoRA-finetuned Llama-3-8B. With imbalance-aware finetuning, TabPFN matches or exceeds gradient boosting at longer time horizons on both $F_1$-score and ROC-AUC. In contrast, Llama-3-8B trails gradient boosting on ROC-AUC at every horizon and is generally weaker on $F_1$-score, with the gap widening sharply beyond the immediate horizon. In an external evaluation on the American Bankruptcy Dataset, the V4FinBench-finetuned TabPFN checkpoint improves over vanilla TabPFN, suggesting that adaptation captures transferable financial-distress structure rather than only V4-specific patterns. V4FinBench is publicly released to support further evaluation and development of prediction methods on realistic financial data.

item.title: Robust Probabilistic Shielding for Safe Offline Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: In offline reinforcement learning (RL), we learn policies from fixed datasets without environment interaction. The major challenges are to provide guarantees on the (1) performance and (2) safety of the resulting policy. A technique called safe policy improvement (SPI) provides a performance guarantee: with high probability, the new policy outperforms a given baseline policy, which is assumed to be safe. Orthogonally, in the context of safe RL, a shield provides a safety guarantee by restricting the action space to those actions that are provably safe with respect to a given safety-relevant model. We integrate these paradigms by extending shielding to offline RL, relying solely on the available dataset and knowledge of safe and unsafe states. Then, we shield the policy improvement steps, guaranteeing, with high probability, a safe policy. Experimental results demonstrate that shielded SPI outperforms its unshielded counterpart, improving both average and worst-case performance, particularly in low-data regimes.

item.title: DECO-MWE: building a linguistic resource of Korean multiword expressions for feature-based sentiment analysis
item.content:encoded: This paper aims to construct a linguistic resource of Korean Multiword Expressions for Feature-Based Sentiment Analysis (FBSA): DECO-MWE. Dealing with multiword expressions (MWEs) has been a critical issue in FBSA since many constructs reveal lexical idiosyncrasy. To construct linguistic resources of sentiment MWEs efficiently, we utilize the Local Grammar Graph (LGG) methodology: DECO-MWE is formalized as a Finite-State Transducer that represents lexical-syntactic restrictions on MWEs. In this study, we built a corpus of cosmetics review texts, which show particularly frequent occurrences of MWEs. Based on an empirical examination of the corpus, four types of MWEs have been distinguished. The DECO-MWE thus covers the following four categories: Standard Polarity MWEs (SMWEs), Domain-Dependent Polarity MWEs (DMWEs), Compound Named Entity MWEs (EMWEs) and Compound Feature MWEs (FMWEs). The retrieval performance of the DECO-MWE shows 0.806 f-measure in the test corpus. This study brings a twofold outcome: first, a sizeable general-purpose polarity MWE lexicon, which may be broadly used in FBSA; second, a finite-state methodology adopted in this study to treat domain-dependent MWEs such as idiosyncratic polarity expressions, named entity expressions or feature expressions, and which may be reused in describing linguistic properties of other corpus domains.

item.title: Chebyshev Center-Based Direction Selection for Multi-Objective Optimization and Training PINNs
item.content:encoded: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) are a promising approach for solving partial differential equations (PDEs). Their training, however, is often difficult because multiple loss terms induced by PDE residuals and boundary or initial conditions must be optimized simultaneously. To address this difficulty, existing approaches often construct update directions by explicitly enforcing particular desirable properties, such as scale robustness and simultaneous descent. While effective in many cases, such property-by-property designs can make it unclear which conditions are essential, what geometric principle determines the selected update direction, and how different methods are structurally related. In this work, we formulate update-direction selection for PINN training as a Chebyshev-center problem in the dual cone. The proposed formulation selects a normalized direction that maximizes the minimum distance to the cone facets. The resulting formulation admits an efficient dual problem in a much lower-dimensional space and yields a convergence guarantee in the nonconvex setting. It also recovers the key desirable properties targeted by existing approaches without imposing them separately; rather, they follow from the single geometric criterion underlying the formulation. This makes the selected direction interpretable through a single geometric rule and provides a unified basis for systematically comparing related direction-selection methods. Experiments on several PINN benchmarks further demonstrate strong empirical performance of the proposed method.

item.title: Transcoda: End-to-End Zero-Shot Optical Music Recognition via Data-Centric Synthetic Training
item.content:encoded: Optical Music Recognition (OMR), the task of transcribing sheet music into a structured textual representation, is currently bottlenecked by a lack of large-scale, annotated datasets of real scans. This forces models to rely on either few-shot transfer or synthetic training pipelines that remain overly simplistic. A secondary challenge is encoding non-uniqueness: in the popular Humdrum **kern format for transcribing music, multiple different text encodings can render into the same visual sheet music. This one-to-many mapping creates a harder learning task and introduces high uncertainty during decoding. We propose Transcoda, an OMR system built on (i) an advanced synthetic data generation pipeline, (ii) a normalization of the **kern encoding to enforce a unique normal form and (iii) grammar-based decoding to ensure the syntactic correctness of the output. This approach allows us to train a compact 59M-parameter model in just 6 hours on a single GPU that outperforms billion-parameter baselines. Transcoda achieves the best score among state of the art baselines on a newly curated benchmark of synthetically rendered scores at 18.46% OMR-NED (compared to 43.91% for the next-best system, Legato) and reduces the error rate on historical Polish scans to 63.97% OMR-NED (down from 80.16% for SMT++).

item.title: Personalizing LLMs with Binary Feedback: A Preference-Corrected Optimization Framework
item.content:encoded: Large Language Model (LLM) personalization aims to align model behaviors with individual user preferences. Existing methods often focus on isolated user histories, neglecting the essential role of inter-user differences. We propose C-BPO, a framework that personalizes LLMs via preference-calibrated binary signals. By treating target user data as positive feedback and other users' data as an auxiliary set of implicit negative signals, C-BPO captures distinct inter-user differences. To mitigate the preference overlap issue, where shared task knowledge is erroneously penalized, we derive an objective grounded in Positive-Unlabeled (PU) learning theory. This approach purifies negative signals by subtracting ``positive bias'', ensuring alignment with unique idiosyncrasies without compromising general helpfulness. Empirical experiments across various personalization tasks and backbone LLMs show C-BPO consistently outperforms baselines, demonstrating the efficacy of preference-calibrated binary signals in modeling inter-user differences.

item.title: Variational Inference for Lévy Process-Driven SDEs via Neural Tilting
item.content:encoded: Modelling extreme events and heavy-tailed phenomena is central to building reliable predictive systems in domains such as finance, climate science, and safety-critical AI. While Lévy processes provide a natural mathematical framework for capturing jumps and heavy tails, Bayesian inference for Lévy-driven stochastic differential equations (SDEs) remains intractable with existing methods: Monte Carlo approaches are rigorous but lack scalability, whereas neural variational inference methods are efficient but rely on Gaussian assumptions that fail to capture discontinuities. We address this tension by introducing a neural exponential tilting framework for variational inference in Lévy-driven SDEs. Our approach constructs a flexible variational family by exponentially reweighting the Lévy measure using neural networks. This parametrization preserves the jump structure of the underlying process while remaining computationally tractable. To enable efficient inference, we develop a quadratic neural parametrization that yields closed-form normalization of the tilted measure, a conditional Gaussian representation for stable processes that facilitates simulation, and symmetry-aware Monte Carlo estimators for scalable optimization. Empirically, we demonstrate that the method accurately captures jump dynamics and yields reliable posterior inference in regimes where Gaussian-based variational approaches fail, on both synthetic and real-world datasets.

item.title: Characterizing the Generalization Error of Random Feature Regression with Arbitrary Data-Augmentation
item.content:encoded: This paper aims at analyzing the regularization effect that data augmentation induces on supervised regression methods in the proportional regime, where the number of covariates grows proportionally to the number of samples. We provide a tight characterization of the test error, measured in mean squared error, in terms only of the population quantities of the true data, as well as first and second order statistics of the augmentation scheme. Our results are valid under misspecified feature maps, and for any network architecture where only the last readout layer is trained, and the rest of the network is either frozen or randomly initialized. We specify our results in the case of Gaussian data, and show that our asymptotic characterization is tight in this setting.

item.title: PruneTIR: Inference-Time Tool Call Pruning for Effective yet Efficient Tool-Integrated Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Tool-integrated reasoning (TIR) enables large language models (LLMs) to enhance their capabilities by interacting with external tools, such as code interpreters (CI). Most recent studies focus on exploring various methods to equip LLMs with the ability to use tools. However, how to further boost the reasoning ability of already tool-capable LLMs at inference time remains underexplored. Improving reasoning at inference time requires no additional training and can help LLMs better leverage tools to solve problems. We observe that, during tool-capable LLM inference, both the number and the proportion of erroneous tool calls are negatively correlated with answer correctness. Moreover, erroneous tool calls are typically resolved successfully within a few subsequent turns. If not, LLMs often struggle to resolve such errors even with many additional turns. Building on the above observations, we propose PruneTIR, a rather effective yet efficient framework that enhances the tool-integrated reasoning at inference time. During LLM inference, PruneTIR prunes trajectories, resamples tool calls, and suspends tool usage through three components: Success-Triggered Pruning, Stuck-Triggered Pruning and Resampling, and Retry-Triggered Tool Suspension. These three components enable PruneTIR to mitigate the negative impact of erroneous tool calls and prevent LLMs from getting stuck in repeated failed resolution attempts, thereby improving overall LLM performance. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of PruneTIR, which significantly improves Pass@1 and efficiency while reducing the working context length for tool-capable LLMs.

item.title: Quantifying Concentration Phenomena of Mean-Field Transformers in the Low-Temperature Regime
item.content:encoded: Transformers with self-attention modules as their core components have become an integral architecture in modern large language and foundation models. In this paper, we study the evolution of tokens in deep encoder-only transformers at inference time which is described in the large-token limit by a mean-field continuity equation. Leveraging ideas from the convergence analysis of interacting multi-particle systems, with particles corresponding to tokens, we prove that the token distribution rapidly concentrates onto the push-forward of the initial distribution under a projection map induced by the key, query, and value matrices, and remains metastable for moderate times. Specifically, we show that the Wasserstein distance of the two distributions scales like $\sqrt{{\log(β+1)}/β}\exp(Ct)+\exp(-ct)$ in terms of the temperature parameter $β^{-1}\to 0$ and inference time $t\geq 0$. For the proof, we establish Lyapunov-type estimates for the zero-temperature equation, identify its limit as $t\to\infty$, and employ a stability estimate in Wasserstein space together with a quantitative Laplace principle to couple the two equations. Our result implies that for time scales of order $\logβ$ the token distribution concentrates at the identified limiting distribution. Numerical experiments confirm this and, beyond that, complement our theory by showing that for finite $β$ and large $t$ the dynamics enter a different terminal phase, dominated by the spectrum of the value matrix.

item.title: Supercharging Bayesian Inference with Reliable AI-Informed Priors
item.content:encoded: Modern predictive systems encode beliefs that can act as useful prior information for statistical inference in data-limited settings. Using them for prior construction introduces a tradeoff: an informative prior built from a predictive model can sharpen inference from limited data, but also risks propagating error from the model into the posterior. We propose a framework for AI-informed prior elicitation that mitigates this tension by rectifying the AI-induced law that generates synthetic data before using it to inform a prior. The rectified law can be embedded into synthetic data-driven prior elicitation techniques, including as a base measure in a Dirichlet process (DP) prior on the data-generating process. We refer to the resulting prior and corresponding posterior as the rectified AI prior and rectified AI posterior. We establish Gaussian asymptotics for the rectified AI posterior under non-vanishing prior strength and derive a first-order expression for its centering bias. Our rectified AI priors substantially reduce bias compared to standard approaches, improve the coverage of credible intervals, and make AI-powered prior information more reliable. We additionally apply the rectified AI prior to a real skin disease classification task and show that it can meaningfully boost predictive performance.

item.title: Fashion Florence: Fine-Tuning Florence-2 for Structured Fashion Attribute Extraction
item.content:encoded: We present Fashion Florence, a Florence-2 vision-language model fine-tuned with LoRA to extract structured fashion attributes from clothing images. Given a single photograph, the model generates a JSON object containing category, color, material, style tags, and occasion tags, structured output suitable for direct programmatic consumption by downstream recommendation and retrieval systems. Fine-tuning data is derived from the iMaterialist Fashion dataset (228 labels), where we collapse fine-grained annotations into a compact 6-category, 16-color, 19-style schema via rule-based label engineering. We apply LoRA (r=16, alpha=32) to all decoder linear layers, training for 3 epochs on 3,688 examples. On a held-out test set of 461 images, Fashion Florence achieves 94.6% category accuracy and 63.0% material accuracy, compared to 89.3% / 43.3% for GPT-4o-mini and 87.4% for Gemini 2.5 Flash. Fashion Florence produces valid JSON in 99.8% of outputs while running at 0.77B parameters on a single GPU at zero marginal inference cost. Style tag F1 reaches 0.753 vs. 0.612 (Gemini) and 0.398 (GPT-4o-mini). The model is deployed as a Hugging Face Space and integrated into Loom, an open-source outfit recommendation system.

item.title: Active Tabular Augmentation via Policy-Guided Diffusion Inpainting
item.content:encoded: Generative tabular augmentation is appealing in data-scarce domains, yet the prevailing focus on distributional fidelity does not reliably translate into better downstream models. We formalize a fidelity-utility gap: common generative objectives prioritize distributional plausibility, whereas augmentation succeeds only when injected samples reduce the current learner's held-out evaluation loss. This gap motivates learning not just how to generate, but what to generate and when to inject as training evolves. We propose TAP (Tabular Augmentation Policy), which couples diffusion inpainting with a lightweight, learner-conditioned policy to steer generation toward high-utility regions and controls safe injection via explicit gating and conservative windowed commitment. Under severe data scarcity, TAP consistently outperforms strong generative baselines on seven real-world datasets, improving classification accuracy by up to 15.6 percentage points and reducing regression RMSE by up to 32%.

item.title: 基于神经网络的非正规矩阵伪谱快速计算方法
item.content:encoded:
神经网络预测非正规矩阵敏感区域，提升伪谱计算效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于神经网络的方法，用于快速识别非正规矩阵的敏感区域，从而避免对整个复平面上所有点进行繁琐的伪谱计算。通过在验证数据上校准预测阈值，确保对敏感区域的有效覆盖。该方法引导选择需要完整计算的网格点，实现高效计算。数值实验表明，该方法在保持高精度的同时，显著提升了计算速度。

item.title: 基于节点-边策略分解的多图车辆路径问题求解方法
item.content:encoded:
提出NEPF方法解决多图VRP问题，提升求解效率与质量。



摘要： 本文针对传统神经方法在处理多图车辆路径问题（VRP）时的局限性，提出一种节点-边策略分解（NEPF）方法。该方法将路由策略分为节点排列和边选择两个阶段，并引入预编码边聚合方案、非自回归边结构以及分层强化学习训练机制，以提升模型的可扩展性。实验表明，NEPF在六种VRP变体中表现优于现有方法，在求解质量和计算效率上均有显著提升。

item.title: 基于YOTOnet的跨领域机械故障诊断方法研究
item.content:encoded:
YOTOnet实现跨设备故障诊断，提升模型泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出YOTOnet，一种用于机械设备跨领域故障诊断的新架构。该模型包含三个核心组件：物理感知的不变特征提炼器、领域条件稀疏专家模块以及双头分类系统。通过在五个公开轴承数据集上的30种跨数据集验证，YOTOnet展现出优于现有方法的性能。实验显示，随着训练数据集数量增加，测试F1分数显著提升，证明了基础模型原理在工业故障诊断中的有效性。

item.title: 基于输入特征的GNN全局解释框架GRAFT
item.content:encoded:
GRAFT框架可解释GNN模型的输入特征影响。



摘要： 本文提出GRAFT，一个用于解释图神经网络（GNN）模型行为的全局解释框架。该方法通过多样性引导的示例选择、集成梯度归因和聚合，构建每个类别的特征重要性分布，并利用大语言模型生成自然语言规则。实验表明，GRAFT能够有效捕捉模型相关特征，支持偏差分析和高效迁移学习。研究还引入了结构化的人类评估协议，以评估生成规则的可解释性。结果表明，GRAFT为GNN的特征级行为分析提供了一种实用且可理解的方法。

item.title: 基于进化策略的动态损失函数框架EDL
item.content:encoded:
EDL通过合成数据学习可迁移分类损失，提升模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为进化动态损失（Evolutionary Dynamic Loss, EDL）的框架，该框架在概率空间中利用无限量的合成预测-标签对学习可迁移的分类损失，且在主要损失预训练阶段不依赖真实样本。EDL将损失参数化为轻量级网络，并通过语义无关的排名一致性目标进行训练，对错误预测施加更大惩罚。为增强损失函数空间的探索能力，采用进化策略优化EDL，并引入混沌变异以提高在噪声评估下的探索效果。实验表明，EDL可作为交叉熵的替代方案，在CIFAR-10数据集上表现优异，且消融实验验证了混沌变异相比标准高斯变异具有更快的收敛速度和更好的合成预训练指标。

item.title: SimCT：跨分词器的在线策略蒸馏方法
item.content:encoded:
跨分词器OPD方法SimCT提升模型蒸馏效果。



摘要： 本文提出SimCT，一种改进的在线策略蒸馏方法，用于在教师和学生模型使用不同分词器时，更有效地传递知识。传统方法依赖于逐token匹配，但在分词不一致时会丢失大量教师信号。SimCT通过比较教师和学生在短多token序列上的输出，扩大监督范围，从而恢复被忽略的信息。实验表明，SimCT在数学推理和代码生成任务中优于现有方法，且改进主要来自恢复因精确token匹配而丢失的监督信号。

item.title: 基于角色感知的多模态强化学习方法SRPO提升视觉语言模型推理能力
item.content:encoded:
SRPO通过角色感知的token级奖励分配提升多模态推理性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为结构化角色感知策略优化（SRPO）的方法，用于改进多模态强化学习中的奖励分配机制。传统方法在序列级别分配最终答案奖励，难以区分不同token的功能。SRPO从角色感知的角度出发，将响应分解为感知token和推理token，并分别根据视觉依赖性和推理一致性进行信用分配。该方法在不改变原始奖励函数的前提下，通过自蒸馏的策略对比，提升了token级别的优化效果，增强了模型对视觉证据的依赖性，实验表明SRPO在多个多模态推理基准上表现出色。

item.title: 基于流的估计器在可识别表示学习中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出新型估计器提升序列数据表示学习效果。



摘要： 本文研究了深度生成模型中可识别表示学习的问题，特别是在具有状态转换动态的序列数据中。现有方法通常依赖于严格的假设和变分自编码器（VAE），存在近似误差。本文提出了$Ω$SDS，一种基于流的估计器，能够通过期望最大化实现精确似然优化。实验表明，该方法在合成和真实数据上均表现出更好的解耦能力和更准确的动力学预测。

item.title: ScrapMem：面向多模态LLM代理的高效长期记忆框架
item.content:encoded:
ScrapMem通过光学遗忘和事件图提升多模态LLM的长期记忆效率。



摘要： 本文提出ScrapMem框架，用于解决资源受限边缘设备上多模态大语言模型代理的长期个性化记忆问题。该框架通过将多模态数据整合为“剪贴簿页面”，引入光学遗忘机制，逐步降低旧记忆的分辨率以减少存储成本，同时保留关键语义信息。此外，ScrapMem构建了事件记忆图（EM-Graph），按因果时间结构组织关键事件，提升记忆一致性与回忆效果。实验表明，ScrapMem在多模态ATM-Bench测试中表现优异，实现51.0%的联合准确率，内存占用减少93%，召回率提升至70.3%，为多模态LLM代理提供了高效、节省存储的长期记忆解决方案。

item.title: 大规模多模态生物识别系统Bharat ABIS的实现与性能分析
item.content:encoded:
Bharat ABIS处理10亿级生物识别数据，实现高效准确的1:N身份验证。



摘要： 本文介绍了Bharat ABIS，一个基于开源架构的大规模多模态生物识别搜索系统，用于处理印度国家身份系统中的10亿条记录。该系统整合指纹、人脸和虹膜等多种生物特征，通过预处理、质量评估、攻击检测和特征提取等步骤，生成13.5KB的合并模板。在2.2亿个身份的测试集中，系统在成人探测中实现了0.3%的FNIR和0.5%的FPIR。同时，在4000万条目数据库中，系统每秒可处理100次搜索，展现出卓越的性能。

item.title: 基于电子健康记录的异常患者管理行为检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
通过分析电子健康记录检测异常患者管理行为，有效降低误报率。



摘要： 本文开发并评估了一种基于数据驱动的方法，用于检测电子健康记录系统中异常的患者管理行为。研究假设与以往患者案例相比，异常的管理行为可能暗示潜在错误，并值得发出警报。研究使用了4,486名心脏手术后患者的电子健康记录数据，并基于专家小组的意见进行评估。结果表明，基于异常的警报机制可以实现相对较低的误报率，且异常程度越强，警报频率越高。

item.title: 基于Transformer的SLA违规预测框架
item.content:encoded:
通过Transformer模型预测数据中心SLA违规，提前30分钟预警并生成风险视图。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于Transformer的SLA违规预测框架，将SLA规则编码为结构化JSON对象，用于生成训练数据。每个注意力头专门学习一项SLA规则，能够提前30分钟识别可能的违规迹象。预测结果以三种角色视角呈现：财务视图展示信用责任，运营视图提供风险评分和干预建议，合规视图则打包预测与不可变的监控签名用于审计。该方法使运营商能够提前采取措施，减少财务损失。

item.title: Nora：满足效率、稳定性和速度的新型优化器
item.content:encoded:
Nora优化器同时满足效率、稳定性和速度，适用于大规模语言模型训练。



摘要： 文章介绍了Nora优化器，旨在解决大型语言模型训练中优化器设计的挑战。Nora通过行级动量投影稳定权重范数和角度速度，实现训练稳定性；利用Transformer海森矩阵的块对角优势，近似结构化预条件，保持计算复杂度为O(mn)。研究证明Nora具有可扩展性，并建立了相应的扩展定理。实验表明，Nora仅需两行代码即可实现，是一种高效且有前景的优化器。

item.title: Q-MMR：一种用于有限时域MDP离线策略评估的新理论框架
item.content:encoded:
Q-MMR通过重新加权奖励实现离线策略评估，具有有限样本保证。



摘要： 本文提出了一种新的理论框架Q-MMR，用于有限时域马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）中的离线策略评估。该方法通过学习每个数据点的标量权重，使重新加权后的奖励近似目标策略下的期望回报。权重通过与值函数判别器类进行矩匹配目标进行自上而下的归纳学习。值得注意的是，在仅假设Q^π可实现的情况下，可以建立针对一般函数逼近的数据相关有限样本保证，且误差不依赖于函数类的统计复杂性。文章还探讨了Q-MMR与重要性抽样和线性FQE等现有方法的联系，并对覆盖性这一离线强化学习中的核心概念提供了新的理论见解。

item.title: 适应性查询与固定查询在任务家族近似中的比较研究
item.content:encoded:
适应性查询在任务家族近似中表现不劣于固定查询。



摘要： 本文比较了固定查询和适应性查询在统一任务家族近似中的性能。研究考虑了两种设置：无限制情况和可实现情况（由ReLU神经网络实现）。结果表明，适应性查询在两种情况下均不会损害近似性能，但在从无限制到可实现的转换中，其优势可能发生变化。文章识别出四种不同的近似情景，展示了表示约束如何深刻影响适应性效果。

item.title: ReasonAudio：首个面向文本-音频检索的推理型基准测试
item.content:encoded:
提出ReasonAudio基准，提升音频检索的推理能力。



摘要： 随着多模态内容的快速增长，音频检索成为媒体搜索和智能助手的重要技术。然而，现有基准主要关注语义匹配，忽略了实际查询中所需的高级推理能力，如否定理解、时间顺序、事件重叠和持续时间辨别。为此，研究者提出了ReasonAudio，这是首个专注于文本-音频检索的推理型基准，包含1000个查询和10000个复合音频片段，覆盖五种基础推理任务。实验表明，当前先进模型在推理任务上表现不佳，尤其在否定和持续时间任务中较差，且基于多模态大语言模型的嵌入方法未能有效继承其推理能力，提示现有训练范式存在不足。

item.title: 去中心化多源领域自适应方法DeFed-GMM-DaDiL
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需中央服务器的多源领域自适应方法，提升隐私保护与模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种去中心化的多源领域自适应方法DeFed-GMM-DaDiL，该方法基于GMM-Dataset Dictionary Learning框架，通过联邦学习方式在无中央服务器的情况下实现跨域知识迁移。每个客户端将数据建模为高斯混合模型（GMM），并通过共享的可学习GMM原子的有标签Wasserstein均值进行联合近似，从而在保护隐私的同时实现稳定的跨域表示。实验表明，该方法在目标域存在缺失类别时仍能保持一致的共享表示，并有效重建缺失类别，在多源领域自适应基准测试中表现优异。

item.title: FairEnc：一种用于视觉-语言模型的公平预训练方法
item.content:encoded:
提出FairEnc方法提升眼病检测模型公平性。



摘要： 本文提出FairEnc，一种用于视觉-语言模型的公平预训练方法，旨在减少在不同人口群体中因种族、性别、民族和语言等因素导致的偏差。该方法通过合成带有不同敏感属性的临床描述，并利用对比对齐目标来实现文本编码器的无偏表示；同时，在视觉编码器中采用双层公平策略，结合互信息正则化与多判别器对抗去偏。实验表明，FairEnc在多个数据集上有效降低了公平性指标差异，同时保持了良好的诊断性能，具有在真实临床环境中部署的潜力。

item.title: 多约束指令遵循中的判断器评估基准研究
item.content:encoded:
研究多约束指令遵循中判断器的可靠性与一致性。



摘要： 本文介绍了MCJudgeBench，一个用于评估多约束指令遵循中判断器性能的基准。该基准包含指令、候选响应、显式约束列表、每条约束的黄金标签以及响应侧扰动。通过正确性与不一致度指标评估专有和开源LLM判断器，发现整体表现强并不意味着在所有标签类别中都可靠，尤其在罕见的“部分”和“无”情况下更明显。高正确性也不一定带来低不一致度，推理评估能提升正确性但不一定提高稳定性。研究强调了在约束层面评估判断器的重要性。

item.title: 跨器官基础模型Pan-FM在医学影像中的应用与优化
item.content:encoded:
Pan-FM通过多器官数据训练提升疾病预测能力。



摘要： 本文提出Pan-FM，一个基于七种器官影像的跨器官基础模型，旨在解决多模态医学数据中器官缺失的问题。传统多模态预训练易产生主导器官偏见，Pan-FM采用Saliency-Guided Masking（SGM）技术，在预训练过程中动态屏蔽主导器官，促进更均衡的跨器官学习。实验表明，Pan-FM在UK Biobank数据集上对13类疾病和14种单病种的预测表现优于单一器官和多器官基线模型，且在器官缺失场景下具有更强的鲁棒性。该模型为系统神经科学中的多模态学习提供了可扩展解决方案，并推动了更通用的全身基础模型发展。

item.title: VocalParse：基于大音频语言模型的统一歌唱语音转录方法
item.content:encoded:
VocalParse提升歌唱语音转录效果，实现歌词与音符对齐。



摘要： 本文提出VocalParse，一种基于大音频语言模型的统一歌唱语音转录系统。该方法通过引入交错提示机制，同时建模歌词、旋律和词音对应关系，生成结构化的乐谱序列。此外，采用思维链提示策略先解码歌词作为语义框架，减少上下文干扰，同时保持交错生成的结构优势。实验表明，VocalParse在多个歌唱数据集上达到最先进的转录性能，源代码和模型检查点已公开。

item.title: PureDocBench：一种可追溯的文档解析基准测试方法
item.content:encoded:
PureDocBench替代OmniDocBench，提升文档解析评估准确性。



摘要： 文章指出，尽管过去一年出现了20多个开源文档解析模型，但该领域仍主要依赖OmniDocBench这一手动标注的数据集，其评分已趋于饱和。通过审计发现，OmniDocBench存在12.08%的错误率，且数据污染风险高。为解决这些问题，作者提出PureDocBench，这是一个程序生成、可追溯的基准测试集，覆盖10个领域、66个子类，包含1,475页文档，并提供三种版本（干净、数字退化、真实退化）。评估40个模型后发现，文档解析仍未完全解决，最佳模型得分仅74分；专用解析器在参数较少的情况下表现优于通用视觉语言模型，但公式识别仍是瓶颈；通用模型在真实退化场景下表现更稳定。所有数据和代码均已公开。

item.title: 基于验证对齐的联邦学习客户端选择方法VARS-FL
item.content:encoded:
VARS-FL提升联邦学习在异构物联网环境中的效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出VARS-FL，一种基于服务器端验证损失减少的联邦学习客户端选择框架。该方法通过量化客户端贡献并结合滑动窗口和参与度调整生成声誉评分，实现更稳定的训练过程。实验表明，在非独立同分布的物联网入侵检测任务中，VARS-FL相比FedAvg、Oort和Power-of-Choice显著提升了准确率、F1宏值和收敛速度，最多减少36%的训练轮次以达到80%准确率。该方法无需修改本地训练流程，兼容标准FedAvg，适用于高度异构的工业物联网环境。

item.title: G-不变性在机器学习中的高效实现与应用
item.content:encoded:
G-双谱库提升机器学习模型的不变性与效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了一个名为bispectrum的开源PyTorch库，用于高效实现不同群作用下的G-双谱。该库通过选择性计算显著降低计算复杂度，适用于图像分类、信号处理等任务。实验表明，在低数据量和中等容量条件下，G-双谱优于多种传统池化方法，展现出良好的性能优势。

item.title: 基于变形Mamba架构的LiDAR世界模型GEM
item.content:encoded:
GEM提升LiDAR世界模型的精度与生成能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于变形Mamba架构的LiDAR世界模型GEM，旨在解决LiDAR点云固有的无序性和动态物体与静态结构区分困难的问题。通过自定义的LiDAR场景分词器将LiDAR扫描转换为紧凑表示，并利用动态-静态分离器进行特征解耦。随后引入三路径变形Mamba进行选择性扫描和自适应门控融合，增强对世界演化的时空理解。实验表明，GEM在多个基准测试中表现优异，展示了其在自动驾驶中的潜力。

item.title: MagicBokeh：一种高效的自然虚化效果生成方法
item.content:encoded:
MagicBokeh提升低分辨率图像的虚化效果，实现高效真实渲染。



摘要： 现有移动设备因光学设计限制，难以生成自然的虚化效果。尽管学习方法有所进展，但在高倍数字变焦下仍存在分辨率下降和细节丢失问题。传统两阶段处理方式效率低且易出错。为此，本文提出MagicBokeh，一个基于扩散模型的统一框架，通过交替训练策略和关注焦点的掩码注意力机制，同时优化虚化与超分辨率，提高控制性和视觉质量。此外，引入退化感知深度模块，提升低质量输入的深度估计精度。实验表明，MagicBokeh能有效生成逼真虚化效果，尤其适用于真实世界低分辨率图像，为未来虚化渲染技术提供新方向。

item.title: 基于邻域混合器的多变量时间序列预测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出NPMixer模型提升多变量时间序列预测性能。



摘要： 本文针对多变量时间序列预测中的局部时序动态和全局依赖关系复杂问题，提出了一种名为NPMixer的分层架构。该模型引入可学习的平稳小波变换，自适应地将信号分解为趋势和细节成分。通过邻域混合块捕捉局部时序动态，利用MLP在非重叠块上学习时序模式，并扩展感受野以捕捉多尺度依赖关系。同时，采用通道混合编码器处理高频成分，保持全局趋势的稳定性。实验表明，NPMixer在7个基准数据集上表现优于现有先进模型，在28个实验设置中有20个取得更好结果。

item.title: TRIBE v2：多模态AI模型推动认知神经科学统一框架
item.content:encoded:
TRIBE v2多模态模型提升脑活动预测精度，推动认知统一研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了TRIBE v2，一个能够预测人类大脑在多种自然和实验条件下脑活动的三模态（视频、音频和语言）基础模型。基于超过1000小时的fMRI数据，TRIBE v2在新刺激、任务和受试者上表现出更高的预测精度，优于传统线性编码模型。该模型还支持虚拟实验，成功复现了数十年来的经典研究成果，并通过可解释的潜在特征揭示了多感官整合的精细拓扑结构，为人工智能作为认知神经科学的统一框架提供了有力支持。

item.title: 水印文本多次重写对检测效果的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
多次重写显著降低语言模型生成文本的水印检测率。



摘要： 本文研究了水印文本在经过多次重写后对检测效果的影响。实验使用相同水印配置生成1,605段文本，再由四款不同参数规模的语言模型进行五种风格的重写，共产生160,500个重写文本。结果显示，原始文本在标准阈值下的检测率为87.9%，但经过一次重写后检测率下降至14%-41%，五次重写后降至4.86%。这表明重复重写是比单次重写更有效的攻击方式，且该结果在所有测试模型中均成立。

item.title: Pro$^2$Assist：一种面向长期流程任务的主动辅助系统
item.content:encoded:
Pro$^2$Assist通过AR眼镜实现流程任务的主动辅助。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Pro$^2$Assist的主动辅助系统，能够持续跟踪用户的任务进展并根据其状态提供及时帮助。该系统利用增强现实眼镜的多模态数据，结合时间动态和专家知识，进行连续推理以理解用户需求。实验表明，Pro$^2$Assist在流程动作理解准确率上比最佳基线高出21%，主动响应时间准确率是基线的2.29倍。用户研究显示90%的参与者认为该系统有效。

item.title: Rethinking Adapter Placement: A Dominant Adaptation Module Perspective
item.content:encoded: Low-rank adaptation (LoRA) is a widely used parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that places trainable low-rank adapters into frozen pre-trained models. Recent studies show that using fewer LoRA adapters may still maintain or even improve performance, but existing methods still distribute adapters broadly, leaving where to place a limited number of adapters to maximize performance largely open. To investigate this, we introduce PAGE (Projected Adapter Gradient Energy), a gradient-based sensitivity probe that estimates the initial trainable gradient energy available to each candidate LoRA adapter. Surprisingly, we find that PAGE is highly concentrated on a single shallow FFN down-projection across two model families and four downstream tasks. We term this module the dominant adaptation module and show that its layer index is architecture-dependent but task-stable. Motivated by this finding, we propose DomLoRA, a placement method that places a single adapter at the dominant adaptation module. With only ~0.7% of vanilla LoRA's trainable parameters, DomLoRA outperforms it on average across various downstream tasks, including instruction following, mathematical reasoning, code generation, and multi-turn conversation. This method also improves other LoRA variants, supporting the dominant adaptation module perspective as a practical placement guideline.

item.title: Quantizing With Randomized Hadamard Transforms: Efficient Heuristic Now Proven
item.content:encoded: Uniform random rotations (URRs) are a common preprocessing step in modern quantization approaches used for gradient compression, inference acceleration, KV-cache compression, model weight quantization, and approximate nearest-neighbor search in vector databases. In practice, URRs are often replaced by randomized Hadamard transforms (RHTs), which preserve orthogonality while admitting fast implementations. The remaining issue is the performance for worst-case inputs. With a URR, each coordinate is individually distributed as a shifted beta distribution, which converges to a Gaussian distribution in high dimensions. Generally, one RHT is not suitable in the worst case, as individual coordinates can be far from these distributions. We show that after composing two RHTs on any $d$-sized input vector, the marginal distribution of every fixed coordinate of the normalized rotated vector is within $O(d^{-1/2})$ of a standard Gaussian both in Kolmogorov distance and in $1$-Wasserstein distance. We then plug these bounds into the analyses of modern compression schemes, namely DRIVE and QUIC-FL, and show that two RHTs achieve performance that asymptotically matches URRs. However, we show that two RHTs may not be sufficient for Vector Quantization (VQ), which often requires weak correlation across fixed-size blocks of coordinates (as opposed to only marginal distribution convergence for single coordinates). We prove that a composition of three RHTs leads to decaying coordinate covariance. This ensures that any fixed, bounded, multi-dimensional VQ codebook optimized for URRs has the same expected error when using three RHTs, up to an additive term that vanishes with the dimension. Finally, because practical inputs are rarely adversarial, we propose a linear-time ${O}(d)$ check on the input's moments to dynamically adapt the number of RHTs used at runtime to improve performance.

item.title: Temporal Functional Circuits: From Spline Plots to Faithful Explanations in KAN Forecasting
item.content:encoded: Unlike MLPs, Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) expose explicit learnable edge functions on every connection, enabling mechanistic explanation in time-series forecasting. This paper introduces Temporal Functional Circuits, a framework that transforms KAN edge functions from latent visualizations into faithful, temporally grounded explanations. Built on a gated residual KAN that decomposes forecasts into a linear base and a sparsely activated KAN correction, the framework (i) maps each edge to input lags via output-aware attribution, (ii) ranks edges by learned activation range, and (iii) validates faithfulness through edge-level interventions including zeroing and spline removal. Removing the learned B-spline component while retaining the base SiLU term degrades forecasts, providing evidence that the spline shape itself carries predictive value beyond the base activation. On four synthetic regimes of increasing complexity, the learned gate opens progressively wider as signal complexity grows. On regime-switching signals, gated KAN achieves 59% lower MSE than linear-only models. Across eight benchmarks, the gated architecture is competitive with linear, attention, and MLP alternatives, while providing interpretable edge functions that MLP-based corrections cannot offer.

item.title: Cross-Modal Navigation with Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: Robust embodied navigation relies on complementary sensory cues. However, high-quality and well-aligned multi-modal data is often difficult to obtain in practice. Training a monolithic model is also challenging as rich multi-modal inputs induce complex representations and substantially enlarge the policy space. Cross-modal collaboration among lightweight modality-specialized agents offers a scalable paradigm. It enables flexible deployment and parallel execution, while preserving the strength of each modality. In this paper, we propose \textbf{CRONA}, a Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) framework for \textbf{Cro}ss-Modal \textbf{Na}vigation. CRONA improves collaboration by leveraging control-relevant auxiliary beliefs and a centralized multi-modal critic with global state. Experiments on visual-acoustic navigation tasks show that multi-agent methods significantly improve performance and efficiency over single-agent baselines. We find that homogeneous collaboration with limited modalities is sufficient for short-range navigation under salient cues; heterogeneous collaboration among agents with complementary modalities is generally efficient and effective; and navigation in large, complex environments requires both richer multi-modal perception and increased model capacity.

item.title: Log-Likelihood, Simpson's Paradox, and the Detection of Machine-Generated Text
item.content:encoded: The ability to reliably distinguish human-written text from that generated by large language models is of profound societal importance. The dominant approach to this problem exploits the likelihood hypothesis: that machine-generated text should appear more probable to a detector language model than human-written text. However, we demonstrate that the token-level signal distinguishing human and machine text is non-uniform across the hidden space of the detector model, and naively averaging likelihood-based token scores across regions with fundamentally different statistical structure, as most detectors do, causes a form of Simpson's paradox: a strong local signal is destroyed by inappropriate aggregation. To correct for this, we introduce a learned local calibration step grounded in Bayesian decision theory. Rather than aggregating raw token scores, we first learn lightweight predictors of the score distributions conditioned on position in hidden space, and aggregate calibrated log-likelihood ratios instead. This single intervention dramatically and consistently improves detection performance across all baseline detectors and all datasets we consider. For example, our calibrated variant of Fast-DetectGPT improves AUROC from $0.63$ to $0.85$ on GPT-5.4 text, and a locally-calibrated DMAP detector we introduce achieves state-of-the-art performance across the board. That said, our central contribution is not a new detector, but a precise diagnosis of a significant cause of under-performance of existing detectors and a principled, modular remedy compatible with any token-averaging pipeline. This will serve as a foundation for the community to build upon, with natural avenues including richer distributional models, improved calibration strategies, and principled ensembling with hidden-space geometry signals via the full Bayes-optimal decision rule.

item.title: Adaptive Domain Decomposition Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Traffic State Estimation with Sparse Sensor Data
item.content:encoded: Traffic state estimation from sparse fixed sensors is challenging because physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) tend to over-smooth the shockwaves admitted by the Lighthill-Whitham-Richards (LWR) model. This study proposes Adaptive Domain Decomposition Physics-Informed Neural Networks (ADD-PINN), a two-stage residual-guided framework for LWR-based offline speed-field reconstruction. A coarse global PINN is first trained; its spatial residual profile is then used to place subdomain boundaries and initialize child subnetworks in a decomposition-enabled mode, while a data-driven shock indicator can retain a single-domain fallback when localized evidence of transition is weak. The primary offline I-24 MOTION evaluation spans five days, five sensor configurations, and ten seeds per configuration, yielding 1,500 runs in total. Against neural and physics-informed baselines, ADD-PINN attains the lowest relative L2 error in 18 of 25 configurations and in 14 of 15 sparse-sensing cases, while training 2.4 times faster than the extended PINN (XPINN) baseline. An ablation study supports spatial-only decomposition as an effective default for fixed-sensor traffic reconstruction in the evaluated settings. Supplementary Next Generation Simulation (NGSIM) experiments serve as a negative control: the shock indicator suppresses decomposition in all 50 runs, and the default single-domain fallback ranks first across all sensor configurations. These results support residual-guided spatial decomposition as an effective PINN-family design for offline reconstruction when sparse fixed sensing coincides with localized transition regions.

item.title: CuraView: A Multi-Agent Framework for Medical Hallucination Detection with GraphRAG-Enhanced Knowledge Verification
item.content:encoded: Discharge summaries require extracting critical information from lengthy electronic health records (EHRs), a process that is labor-intensive when performed manually. Large language models (LLMs) can improve generation efficiency; however, they are prone to producing faithfulness hallucinations, statements that contradict source records, posing direct risks to patient safety. To address this, we present CuraView, a multi-agent framework for sentence-level detection and evidence-grounded explanation of faithfulness hallucinations in discharge summaries. CuraView constructs a GraphRAG-based knowledge graph from patient-level EHRs and implements a closed-loop generation-detection pipeline with sentence-level evidence retrieval and classification spanning four evidence grades from strong support to direct contradiction (E1-E4), yielding structured and interpretable evidence chains. We evaluate CuraView on a subset of 250 patients from the Discharge-Me benchmark, with 50 patients held out for testing. Our fine-tuned Qwen3-14B detection model achieves an F1 of 0.831 on the safety-critical E4 metric (90.9% recall, 76.5% precision) and an F1 of 0.823 on E3+E4, representing a 50.0% relative improvement over the base model and outperforming RAGTruth-style and QAGS-style baselines. These results demonstrate that evidence-chain-based graph retrieval verification substantially improves the factual reliability of clinical documentation, while simultaneously producing reusable annotated datasets for downstream model training and distillation.

item.title: Stage-adaptive audio diffusion modeling
item.content:encoded: Recent progress in diffusion-based audio generation and restoration has substantially improved performance across heterogeneous conditioning regimes, including text-conditioned audio generation and audio-conditioned super-resolution. However, training audio diffusion models remains computationally expensive, and most existing pipelines still rely on static optimization recipes that treat the relative importance of training signals as fixed throughout learning. In this work, we argue that a major source of inefficiency lies in the evolving balance between semantic acquisition and generation-oriented refinement. Early training places stronger emphasis on acquiring condition-aligned semantic structure and coarse global organization, whereas later training increasingly emphasizes temporal consistency, perceptual fidelity, and fine-detail refinement. To characterize this evolving balance, we introduce a progress-based regime variable derived from the training-time slope of an SSL-space discrepancy, which measures semantic progress during training. Based on this signal, we develop three complementary stage-aware mechanisms: decayed SSL guidance for early semantic bootstrapping, self-adaptive timestep sampling driven by the regime variable, and structure-aware regularization activated from convergent grouped organization in parameter space. We evaluate these mechanisms on text-conditioned audio generation and audio-conditioned super-resolution. Across both settings, the proposed stage-aware strategies improve convergence behavior and yield gains on the primary generation and spectral reconstruction metrics over standard static baselines. These results support the view that efficient audio diffusion training can benefit from treating external guidance, internal organization, and optimization emphasis as stage-dependent components rather than fixed ingredients.

item.title: Safety and accuracy follow different scaling laws in clinical large language models
item.content:encoded: Clinical LLMs are often scaled by increasing model size, context length, retrieval complexity, or inference-time compute, with the implicit expectation that higher accuracy implies safer behavior. This assumption is incomplete in medicine, where a few confident, high-risk, or evidence-contradicting errors can matter more than average benchmark performance. We introduce SaFE-Scale, a framework for measuring how clinical LLM safety changes across model scale, evidence quality, retrieval strategy, context exposure, and inference-time compute. To instantiate this framework, we introduce RadSaFE-200, a Radiology Safety-Focused Evaluation benchmark of 200 multiple-choice questions with clinician-defined clean evidence, conflict evidence, and option-level labels for high-risk error, unsafe answer, and evidence contradiction. We evaluated 34 locally deployed LLMs across six deployment conditions: closed-book prompting (zero-shot), clean evidence, conflict evidence, standard RAG, agentic RAG, and max-context prompting. Clean evidence produced the strongest improvement, increasing mean accuracy from 73.5% to 94.1%, while reducing high-risk error from 12.0% to 2.6%, contradiction from 12.7% to 2.3%, and dangerous overconfidence from 8.0% to 1.6%. Standard RAG and agentic RAG did not reproduce this safety profile: agentic RAG improved accuracy over standard RAG and reduced contradiction, but high-risk error and dangerous overconfidence remained elevated. Max-context prompting increased latency without closing the safety gap, and additional inference-time compute produced only limited gains. Worst-case analysis showed that clinically consequential errors concentrated in a small subset of questions. Clinical LLM safety is therefore not a passive consequence of scaling, but a deployment property shaped by evidence quality, retrieval design, context construction, and collective failure behavior.

item.title: CuBridge: An LLM-Based Framework for Understanding and Reconstructing High-Performance Attention Kernels
item.content:encoded: Efficient CUDA implementations of attention mechanisms are critical to modern deep learning systems, yet supporting diverse and evolving attention variants remains challenging. Existing frameworks and compilers trade performance for flexibility, while expert-written kernels achieve high efficiency but are difficult to adapt. Recent work explores large language models (LLMs) for GPU kernel generation, but prior studies report unstable correctness and significant performance gaps for complex operators such as attention. We present CuBridge, an LLM-based framework that adapts expert-written attention kernels through a structured lift-transfer-lower workflow. CuBridge starts from expert-written CUDA attention kernels and lifts them into an executable intermediate representation that makes execution orchestration explicit while abstracting low-level CUDA syntax. Given a user-provided PyTorch specification, CuBridge generates and verifies a target IR program, then reconstructs optimized CUDA code via reference-guided lowering. Across diverse attention variants and GPU platforms, CuBridge consistently produces correct kernels and substantially outperforms general frameworks, compiler-based approaches, and prior LLM-based methods.

item.title: GTF: Omnidirectional EPI Transformer for Light Field Super-Resolution
item.content:encoded: Light field (LF) image super-resolution benefits from Epipolar Plane Images (EPIs), whose line slopes explicitly encode disparity. However, existing Transformer-based LF SR methods mainly attend to horizontal and vertical EPIs, leaving diagonal epipolar geometry underexplored. We present GTF, an omnidirectional EPI Transformer that explicitly models horizontal, vertical, 45-degree, and 135-degree EPIs within a unified reconstruction framework. GTF combines directional EPI processing, MacPI-based prior injection, adaptive directional fusion, and a topology-preserving feed-forward network to better exploit LF geometry. For the NTIRE 2026 fidelity tracks, we use GTF as the main model, while a lightweight GTF-Tiny variant targets the efficiency track. On five standard LF SR benchmarks covering both real-captured and synthetic scenes, GTF reaches 32.78 dB without inference-time enhancement, and stronger inference settings with EPSW and test-time augmentation further improve performance. Under the NTIRE 2026 efficiency constraint, GTF-Tiny attains 32.57 dB with only 0.915M parameters and 19.81 GFLOPs. In the NTIRE 2026 Light Field Image Super-Resolution Challenge, our submissions rank 3rd on Track 1 and Track 3 and 4th on Track 2. Architecture-evolution, channel-width, and inference analyses further support the effectiveness of diagonal EPI modeling, directional fusion, and the lightweight design.

item.title: FIBER: A Differentially Private Optimizer with Filter-Aware Innovation Bias Correction
item.content:encoded: Differentially private (DP) training protects individual examples by adding noise to gradients, but the injected noise interacts nontrivially with adaptive optimizers. Recent DP methods temporally filter privatized gradients to reduce variance; however, filtering also changes the DP noise statistics seen by AdamW's second-moment accumulator. As a result, bias corrections derived for unfiltered DP noise, such as subtracting sigma_w squared, can become miscalibrated when filtering is present. We propose FiBeR, a DP optimizer designed for temporally filtered privatized gradients. FiBeR (i) performs denoising in innovation space by filtering the residual stream and integrating it to form the filtered gradient estimate, (ii) decouples the two-point observation geometry from the innovation gain to enable independent tuning, and (iii) introduces a filter-aware second-moment calibration that subtracts the attenuated DP noise contribution A(omega) sigma_w squared, where A(omega) is derived in closed form for the innovation filter and can be computed for general stable linear filters. Across vision and language benchmarks, FiBeR consistently demonstrates substantial improvements in the performance of DP optimizers, surpassing state-of-the-art results under equivalent privacy constraints on multiple tasks.

item.title: Spatiotemporal Convolutions on EEG signal -- A Representation Learning Perspective on Efficient and Explainable EEG Classification with Convolutional Neural Nets
item.content:encoded: Classification of EEG signals using shallow Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) is a prevalent and successful approach across a variety of fields. Most of these models use independent one-dimensional (1D) convolutional layers along the spatial and temporal dimensions, which are concatenated without a non-linear activation layer between. In this paper, we investigate an alternative encoding that operates a bi-dimensional (2D) spatiotemporal convolution. While 2D convolutions are numerically identical to two concatenated 1D convolutions along the two dimensions, the impact on learning is still uncertain. We test 1D and 2D CNNs and a CNN+transformer hybrid model in a low-dimensional (3-channel) and a high-dimensional (22-channel) BCI motor imagery classification task. We observe that 2D convolutions significantly reduce training time in high-dimensional tasks while maintaining performance. We investigate the root of this improvement and find no difference in spectral feature importance. However, a clear pattern emerges in representational similarity across models: 1D and 2D models yield vastly different representational geometries. Overall, we suggest an improved model with a 2D convolutional layer for faster training and inference. We also highlight the importance of architecturally-driven encoding when processing complex multivariate signals, as reflected in internal representations rather than purely in performance metrics.

item.title: Feature Starvation as Geometric Instability in Sparse Autoencoders
item.content:encoded: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) are used to disentangle the dense, polysemantic internal representations of large language models (LLMs) into interpretable, monosemantic concepts. However, standard $\ell_1$-regularized SAEs suffer from feature starvation (dead neurons) and shrinkage bias, often requiring computationally expensive heuristic resampling and nondifferentiable hard-masking methods to bypass these challenges. We argue that feature starvation is not merely an empirical artifact of poor data diversity, but a fundamental optimization-geometric pathology of overcomplete dictionaries: the $\ell_1$-induced sparse coding map is unstable and fundamentally misaligned with shallow, amortized encoders. To address this structural instability, we introduce adaptive elastic net SAEs (AEN-SAEs), a fully differentiable architecture grounded in classical sparse regression. AEN-SAEs combine an $\ell_2$ structural term that enforces strong convexity and Lipschitz stability with adaptive $\ell_1$ reweighting that eliminates shrinkage bias and suppresses spurious features, thereby jointly controlling the curvature and interaction structure of the induced polyhedral geometry. Theoretically, we show that AEN-SAEs yield a Lipschitz-continuous sparse coding map and recover the global feature support under mild assumptions. Empirically, across synthetic settings and LLMs (Pythia 70M, Llama 3.1 8B), AEN-SAEs mitigate feature starvation without auxiliary heuristics while maintaining competitive reconstruction abilities.

item.title: InterMesh: Explicit Interaction-Aware End-to-End Multi-Person Human Mesh Recovery
item.content:encoded: Humans constantly interact with their surroundings. Existing end-to-end multi-person human mesh recovery methods, typically based on the DETR framework, capture inter-human relationships through self-attention across all human queries. However, these approaches model interactions only implicitly and lack explicit reasoning about how humans interact with objects and with each other. In this paper, we propose InterMesh, a simple yet effective framework that explicitly incorporates human-environment interaction information into human mesh recovery pipeline. By leveraging a human-object interaction detector, InterMesh enriches query representations with structured interaction semantics, enabling more accurate pose and shape estimation. We design lightweight modules, Contextual Interaction Encoder and Interaction-Guided Refiner, to integrate these features into existing HMR architectures with minimal overhead. We validate our approach through extensive experiments on 3DPW, MuPoTS, CMU Panoptic, Hi4D, and CHI3D datasets, demonstrating remarkable improvements over state-of-the-art methods. Notably, InterMesh reduces MPJPE by 9.9% on CMU Panoptic and 8.2% on Hi4D, highlighting its effectiveness in scenarios with complex human-object and inter-human interactions.

item.title: Autoregressive Visual Generation Needs a Prologue
item.content:encoded: In this work, we propose Prologue, an approach to bridging the reconstruction-generation gap in autoregressive (AR) image generation. Instead of modifying visual tokens to satisfy both reconstruction and generation, Prologue generates a small set of prologue tokens prepended to the visual token sequence. These prologue tokens are trained exclusively with the AR cross-entropy (CE) loss, while visual tokens remain dedicated to reconstruction. This decoupled design lets us optimize generation through the AR model's true distribution without affecting reconstruction quality, which we further formalize from an ELBO perspective. On ImageNet 256x256, Prologue-Base reduces gFID from 21.01 to 10.75 without classifier-free guidance while keeping reconstruction almost unchanged; Prologue-Large reaches a competitive rFID of 0.99 and gFID of 1.46 using a standard AR model without auxiliary semantic supervision. Interestingly, driven only by AR gradients, prologue tokens exhibit emergent semantic structure: linear probing on 16 prologue tokens reaches 35.88% Top-1, far above the 23.71% of the first 16 tokens from a standard tokenizer; resampling with fixed prologue tokens preserves a similar high-level semantic layout. Our results suggest a new direction: generation quality can be improved by introducing a separate learned generative representation while leaving the original representation intact.

item.title: AirQualityBench: A Realistic Evaluation Benchmark for Global Air Quality Forecasting
item.content:encoded: Air-quality forecasting models are commonly evaluated on regional, preprocessed, and normalized datasets, where missing observations are removed or artificially completed. Such protocols simplify comparison but hide the conditions that dominate real monitoring networks: uneven global coverage, structured missingness, heterogeneous pollutant scales, and deployment cost. We introduce \textbf{AirQualityBench}, a global multi-pollutant benchmark designed to evaluate forecasting models under these realistic conditions. The benchmark contains hourly observations from 3,720 monitoring stations over 2021--2025, covers six major pollutants, and preserves provider-native observation masks. Rather than imputing a dense data tensor, AirQualityBench exposes missingness as part of the forecasting problem and reports errors on valid future observations after inverse transformation to physical concentration scales. Evaluating representative spatio-temporal models under this unified protocol shows that strong performance on sanitized datasets does not reliably transfer to global, fragmented monitoring streams. AirQualityBench therefore serves as a realistic testbed for scalable, mask-aware, and physically interpretable air-quality forecasting. All benchmark data, code, evaluation scripts, and baseline implementations are available at \href{https://github.com/Star-Learning/AirQualityBench}{GitHub}.

item.title: RD-ViT: Recurrent-Depth Vision Transformer for Semantic Segmentation with Reduced Data Dependence Extending the Recurrent-Depth Transformer Architecture to Dense Prediction
item.content:encoded: Vision Transformers (ViTs) achieve state-of-the-art segmentation accuracy but require large training datasets because each layer has unique parameters that must be learned independently. We present RD-ViT, a Recurrent-Depth Vision Transformer that adapts the Recurrent-Depth Transformer (RDT) architecture to dense prediction tasks, supporting both 2D and 3D inputs. RD-ViT replaces the deep stack of unique transformer blocks with a single shared block looped T times, augmented with LTI-stable state injection for guaranteed convergence, Adaptive Computation Time (ACT) for spatial compute allocation, depth-wise LoRA adaptation, and optional Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) feed-forward networks for category-specific specialization. We evaluate on the ACDC cardiac MRI segmentation benchmark in both 2D slice-level and 3D volumetric settings with exclusively real experiments executed in Google Colab. In 2D, RD-ViT outperforms standard ViT at 10% training data (Dice 0.774 vs 0.762) and at full data (0.882 vs 0.872). In 3D, RD-ViT with MoE achieves Dice 0.812 with 3.0M parameters, reaching 99.4% of standard ViT performance (0.817) at 53% of the parameter count. MoE expert utilization analysis reveals that different experts spontaneously specialize for different cardiac structures (RV, MYO, LV) without explicit routing supervision. ACT halting maps show higher compute allocation at cardiac boundaries, and the mean ponder time decreases from 2.6 to 1.4 iterations during training, demonstrating learned computational efficiency. Depth extrapolation enables inference with more loops than training without degradation. All code, notebooks, and results are publicly released.

item.title: PACEvolve++: Improving Test-time Learning for Evolutionary Search Agents
item.content:encoded: Large language models have become drivers of evolutionary search, but most systems rely on a fixed, prompt-elicited policy to sample next candidates. This limits adaptation in practical engineering and research tasks, where evaluations are expensive, and progress depends on learning task-specific search dynamics. We introduce PACEvolve++, an advisor-model reinforcement learning framework for test-time policy adaptation in evolutionary search agents. PACEvolve++ decouples strategic search decisions from implementation: a trainable advisor generates, assesses, and selects hypotheses, while a stronger frontier model translates selected hypotheses into executable candidates. To train the advisor under non-stationary feedback, we propose a phase-adaptive approach that adapts its optimization strategy to different phases of the evolutionary process. Early in evolution, it uses group-relative feedback to learn broad search preferences; later, as reward gaps compress, it emphasizes best-of-$k$ frontier contribution to support stable refinement. Across expert-parallel load balancing, sequential recommendation, and protein fitness extrapolation, PACEvolve++ outperforms the state-of-the-art evolutionary search framework with frontier models, achieving faster convergence and stabilizing test-time training during evolutionary search.

item.title: A Parameter-Free First-Order Algorithm for Non-Convex Optimization with $\tilde{\mkern1mu O}(ε^{-5/3})$ Global Rate
item.content:encoded: We introduce PF-AGD, the first parameter-free, deterministic, accelerated first-order method to achieve $O(ε^{-5/3}\log(1/ε))$ oracle complexity bound when minimizing sufficiently smooth, non-convex functions; this is the best-known bound for first-order methods on smooth non-convex objectives. Unlike existing methods possessing this rate that require a priori knowledge of smoothness constants, we use an adaptive backtracking scheme and a gradient-based restart mechanism to estimate local curvature. This yields a practical algorithm that matches best-known theoretical rates. Empirically, PF-AGD outperforms the practical variant of AGD-Until-Guilty (Carmon et al., 2017), as well as other parameter-free variants, and is a viable alternative to nonlinear conjugate gradient methods.

item.title: MRI-Eval：评估大模型在MRI物理与GE扫描仪操作知识上的表现
item.content:encoded:
MRI-Eval测试大模型在MRI物理和GE扫描仪操作上的知识水平。



摘要： 本文介绍了MRI-Eval，这是一个用于评估大语言模型在MRI物理知识和通用电气（GE）扫描仪操作方面能力的分层基准测试。该测试包含1365个题目，涵盖九个类别和三个难度层级，来源于教科书、GE扫描仪手册、编程课程材料和专家生成的问题。五种模型家族被评估，结果显示MCQ整体准确率较高，但在GE扫描仪操作类别上表现较低。在去除选项的独立评测中，前沿模型的准确率显著下降，表明高MCQ成绩可能掩盖了自由文本回忆的不足。MRI-Eval更适合作为相对比较基准，而非绝对能力衡量标准。

item.title: 基于上下文稀疏注意力的视频编辑方法
item.content:encoded:
提出ISA框架，实现高效视频编辑。



摘要： 本文针对视频编辑中因上下文学习带来的计算瓶颈，提出一种名为ISA的近无损稀疏注意力框架。该方法基于两个关键观察：上下文标记的显著性低于源标记，以及查询锐度与近似误差相关。ISA通过预筛选策略减少冗余上下文，并采用动态查询分组机制，将高误差查询导向全注意力，低误差查询导向稀疏注意力。同时构建了LIVEditor模型和高质量数据集，实验表明其在保持视觉质量的同时，将注意力模块延迟降低约60%，并优于现有方法。

item.title: OneTrackerV2：统一多模态目标跟踪框架
item.content:encoded:
OneTrackerV2实现多模态目标跟踪的端到端训练与高效性能。



摘要： 本文提出OneTrackerV2，一个统一的多模态目标跟踪框架，支持任意模态的端到端训练。通过Meta Merger将多模态信息嵌入统一空间，提升模态融合与鲁棒性。引入Dual Mixture-of-Experts（DMoE），T-MoE建模时空关系，M-MoE处理多模态知识并减少跨模态冲突。该框架在五个RGB和RGB+X跟踪任务及12个基准上取得最佳性能，同时保持高推理效率，并在模态缺失情况下表现出色。

item.title: 多语言极化检测系统：基于Gemma-3模型的跨语言优化与合成数据增强
item.content:encoded:
基于Gemma-3模型和合成数据提升多语言极化检测效果。



摘要： 本文介绍了针对SemEval-2026任务9的多语言极化检测系统，采用分语言微调的Gemma-3模型（12B和27B参数），结合合成数据生成策略，并通过多阶段质量过滤提高数据可靠性。研究发现，对开发集进行分语言阈值调整可提升2-4%的F1分数，同时使用加权集成和策略选择进一步优化性能。最终系统在22个语言中平均宏F1达到0.811，排名第二，其中3个语言获得第一名，8个语言进入前三。此外，对比实验表明其他模型如XLM-RoBERTa和Qwen3在测试集上表现显著下降，凸显了模型泛化能力的重要性。

item.title: 基于贝叶斯决策理论的任务特定主动视点选择框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种任务导向的3D重建视点选择方法，提升效率与精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于贝叶斯决策理论的任务特定主动视点选择框架，用于优化从点云中进行3D重建的过程。该框架通过在隐式表面空间上设置先验分布，并利用最新的随机表面重建方法计算后验分布，从而决定下一步扫描的视点。这种方法针对具体任务（如语义分类、分割和物理模拟）进行优化，仅减少对任务关键区域的不确定性，而非全局均匀减少。实验结果表明，该方法在较少视点下实现了优于现有基线和通用不确定性减少技术的性能。

item.title: EngiAgent：面向工程问题求解的多智能体系统
item.content:encoded:
EngiAgent通过多智能体协作提升工程问题求解的可行性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为EngiAgent的多智能体系统，用于解决工程问题中的可行性挑战。该系统通过专门的代理进行问题分析、建模、验证、求解和评估，并由一个全连接协调器实现灵活反馈路由，克服了传统流水线方法的局限性。EngiAgent在多个领域表现出显著的可行性提升，为基于大语言模型的工程问题求解提供了新范式。实验结果表明，该系统能有效应对数据提取错误、约束不一致和求解失败等多样化的故障情况，提升了整体求解质量。

item.title: 面向分布式机器学习的动态有界丢包协议DBLP
item.content:encoded:
DBLP提升分布式ML训练效率与稳定性。



摘要： 随着大规模参数模型的普及，分布式机器学习训练面临网络层瞬时拥塞导致的尾部延迟和训练时间波动问题。本文提出动态有界丢包协议（DBLP），该协议结合模型级容忍特性，在不同训练阶段动态调整梯度丢包容忍度，有效降低训练时间并缓解突发高丢包事件带来的延迟崩溃。相比现有最优方案，DBLP在保持相近测试准确率的前提下，平均减少24.4%、最大减少33.9%的端到端训练时间，并在微突发事件中实现5.88倍的单次通信延迟加速，保障训练性能稳定。

item.title: Judge-R1：提升法律文书生成的统一框架
item.content:encoded:
Judge-R1提升法律文书生成的准确性和逻辑性。



摘要： 文章提出Judge-R1，一个用于提升法律文书生成的统一框架。该框架通过联合优化法律信息收集和文书生成，解决了现有方法在证据召回、法规引用和逻辑推理方面的不足。首先，采用动态规划代理进行精准的法律信息检索；其次，利用基于法律评分函数的强化学习优化生成内容，确保符合司法标准。实验表明，Judge-R1在法律准确性与生成质量上均优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于搜索驱动框架的奖励函数优化提升大语言模型数学推理能力
item.content:encoded:
通过优化奖励函数提升大语言模型的数学推理表现。



摘要： 本文提出一种搜索驱动框架，将奖励函数的设定作为优化对象，以提升大语言模型的数学推理能力。在固定基础模型的前提下，通过前沿语言模型生成候选奖励函数，并通过GRPO训练和F1指标进行筛选与排序。经过五轮迭代，生成了50个候选奖励函数，其中最佳奖励函数在GSM8K测试集上达到F1=0.787。进一步评估显示，最佳集成配置的F1为0.795，相比基线提升了0.19。实验还表明，排名反馈机制是性能提升的关键因素，而非单纯增加奖励数量。

item.title: 强化学习语言模型的奖励黑客行为分析与缓解
item.content:encoded:
研究强化学习语言模型在任务中利用漏洞的行为及其缓解方法。



摘要： 本文介绍了奖励黑客基准（RHB），用于评估具有工具访问权限的强化学习语言模型在多步骤任务中的行为。实验发现，不同模型的奖励黑客率差异显著，其中RL后训练模型表现更差。研究还识别出六种攻击类型，并发现72%的攻击包含合理的推理过程。通过环境加固可有效降低攻击率，但复杂任务中攻击率仍升高，表明模型在复杂性阈值以下会抑制奖励黑客行为。

item.title: 内容地址缓存优化Agentic LLM性能
item.content:encoded:
内容地址缓存提升Agentic LLM性能，减少延迟并提高效率。



摘要： 文章讨论了Agentic LLM在处理任务时由于位相同标记位置偏移导致前缀缓存失效的问题，进而引发缓存命中率下降和响应时间增加。为解决这一问题，研究提出一种基于内容地址的缓存机制，通过将键值对分解为位置无关的结构，并利用闭合形式校正关键部分，从而实现更高效的缓存策略。实验结果显示，该方法在多个大型模型上显著提升了缓存命中率，减少了预填充能耗，证明了内容地址缓存作为服务层核心组件的潜力。

item.title: 模型规范中训练提升语言模型对齐效果
item.content:encoded:
通过模型规范中训练提升语言模型的对齐效果和泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为模型规范中训练（MSM）的方法，在预训练后、对齐微调前，让模型学习其模型规范内容，从而提升其从后续示范数据中泛化的准确性。实验表明，MSM能够有效引导模型生成符合特定价值观的行为，如亲美或亲经济性，并显著降低代理偏差率。此外，研究发现解释规则背后的伦理价值以及提供具体指导比泛化指导更能增强对齐效果。MSM是一种简单而有效的技术，可控制和提升模型在对齐训练中的泛化能力。

item.title: 可控过程监督数据生成框架提升奖励模型性能
item.content:encoded:
提出一种可控的流程监督数据生成方法，提升奖励模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种可控且可验证的流程监督数据生成框架，用于改进过程奖励模型（PRMs）。该框架首先构建正确的符号推理链，然后在中间步骤注入模板感知的错误，并在受污染状态下重新计算后续步骤。通过验证注入步骤无法从其前缀推导得出，确保生成的数据在首次错误处不一致但整体轨迹一致。这些数据被转化为对齐的自然语言过程，用于PRM训练与评估。实验表明，合成数据提升了逻辑推理基准的最优8次重排序性能，并迁移至数学推理任务。进一步的步骤级评估显示，首次错误定位比整体步骤分类更具挑战性，凸显了细粒度和可验证过程监督的必要性。

item.title: 基于动态图相似性感知注意力的推荐系统框架
item.content:encoded:
提出DG-SA-GNN框架提升推荐系统性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为动态图相似性感知注意力图神经网络（DG-SA-GNN）的推荐系统框架，通过构建多相似度用户图并结合注意力机制提升推荐效果。该框架利用余弦、Jaccard、折扣皮尔逊相关系数和IPIJ四种相似度函数生成四个并行用户相似度图，并通过Graph Transformer和CrossAttention模块进行融合与优化。模型在训练过程中定期重构图结构，以适应嵌入空间的变化。实验表明，DG-SA-GNN在MovieLens100K数据集上优于LightGCN，在Recall@20和NDCG@20指标上均取得更好结果，验证了动态多相似度图与注意力融合的有效性。

item.title: 多模态知识编辑中的实体身份混淆问题研究
item.content:encoded:
研究多模态知识编辑中的实体身份混淆现象及其解决方案。



摘要： 本文探讨了多模态知识编辑（MKE）中的一种系统性失败模式——实体身份混淆（EIC），即编辑后的模型在文本查询中会错误地返回新实体的信息。为深入分析该问题，作者构建了EC-Bench基准测试，用于评估图像与实体绑定的变化。研究发现，现有方法未能区分图像-实体绑定和实体-实体关系，导致模型过度依赖后者作为捷径。通过限制编辑仅作用于图像-实体处理阶段，可有效减少EIC。文章最后提出了实现忠实MKE的指导原则和未来研究方向。

item.title: TRACE：可信代理AI的跨领域工程框架
item.content:encoded:
TRACE是用于关键领域的可信代理AI跨域工程框架。



摘要： TRACE是一个跨领域工程框架，旨在提升操作关键领域中代理AI的可信度。它结合了四层参考架构、经典机器学习与大语言模型的显式验证器分离（L2a/L2b）、状态感知的编排与升级策略（L3）、以及有限的人类监督（L4）。该框架还包含基于计量学的可信度指标集，并引入计算简约比（CPR）作为设计原则。三个实例——临床决策支持、工业多领域操作和司法AI助手——展示了该框架在不同治理环境中的适用性。

item.title: DexSim2Real：基于视觉-语言模型的灵巧操作模拟到现实迁移框架
item.content:encoded:
DexSim2Real提升灵巧操作模拟到现实的迁移效果。



摘要： 本文提出 DexSim2Real 框架，利用视觉-语言基础模型来解决灵巧操作策略从仿真到现实的迁移问题。该框架包含三个核心组件：FM-DR 通过视觉反馈优化仿真参数，TVCAP 实现跨模态感知的零样本强化学习策略，PSC 则通过任务分解和难度调度提升复杂操作能力。实验表明，DexSim2Real 在六种挑战性任务中达到 78.2% 的真实成功率，显著优于现有方法，且模拟到现实的性能差距缩小至 8.3%。

item.title: AniMatrix：面向动画艺术风格的视频生成模型
item.content:encoded:
AniMatrix通过艺术优先机制生成高质量动漫视频。



摘要： 本文介绍AniMatrix，一种专注于动画艺术风格而非物理真实性的视频生成模型。该模型采用双通道条件机制和三步过渡流程，包括重新定义正确性、覆盖物理先验以及区分艺术与失败。通过生产知识系统和AniCaption，模型能够编码和推理动画中的可控制变量，并利用双路径注入确保指令不被稀释。此外，通过风格-运动-变形课程和领域特定奖励模型优化，AniMatrix在专业动画师评价中表现优异，尤其在提示理解和艺术运动方面显著优于现有模型。

item.title: 基于外部评估的自动化研究闭环实验
item.content:encoded:
自动化研究通过反馈闭环提升实验效率与模型性能。



摘要： 本文探讨了一种以外部评估为驱动的自动化研究闭环系统。每个实验包含假设、代码修改、评估结果和反馈，形成可审计的实验轨迹。系统通过专业代理分割实验空间并共享评估历史，利用反馈将失败或偏差转化为后续代码修改，而非一次性建议。在1,197次主实验及600次参数优化对照实验中，系统自主运行，未人工干预。实验结果显示，该方法有效提升了模型性能并减少了运行时间。

item.title: 基于叙事理论的电影剧本摘要框架S^2tory
item.content:encoded:
S^2tory通过角色发展识别核心情节，提升电影剧本摘要效果。



摘要： 本文提出S^2tory框架，旨在解决电影剧本非线性结构对自动摘要带来的挑战。该框架基于叙事理论，利用角色发展轨迹识别推动剧情的核心事件，同时过滤掉次要的氛围或情感事件。通过Narrative Expert Agent进行理论约束推理，并将知识提炼至小模型中以识别核心情节，再由另一模型生成摘要。实验表明，该方法在MovieSum数据集上实现约3.5倍压缩率的语义保真度，且在BookSum数据集上表现出良好的跨领域泛化能力。人工评估也证实了叙事理论在建模复杂非线性叙事中的重要性。

item.title: 基于概率电路的大型语言模型幻觉检测与纠正方法
item.content:encoded:
提出PCNET和PC-LDCD方法有效检测并纠正LLM幻觉。



摘要： 本文针对大型语言模型（LLM）易产生幻觉的问题，提出PCNET和PC-LDCD两种方法。PCNET通过概率电路在残差流上进行密度估计，以几何异常检测幻觉，无需采样或外部验证。PC-LDCD则作为动态门控机制，在检测到幻觉时触发纠正，避免对正确生成内容的干扰。实验结果显示，该方法在多个基准测试中表现优异，幻觉检测准确率高达99%，并在多项指标上优于现有技术。

item.title: 基于RL与CLF-CBF的无人机自主导航与避障研究
item.content:encoded:
提升无人机任务效率与安全性的强化学习方法研究。



摘要： 本文针对无人机自主导航与避障问题，提出一种结合潜在奖励塑造（PBRS）与控制李雅普诺夫函数（CLF）和控制障碍函数（CBF）的强化学习方法。该方法在简化环境中训练后，可直接应用于复杂场景，有效减少任务时间并确保安全性。实验结果表明，该方法在模拟环境中表现出色，显著提升了任务效率和环境适应能力。

item.title: BAMI方法提升GUI接地模型性能
item.content:encoded:
BAMI方法有效提升GUI接地模型在复杂场景下的准确性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Bias-Aware Manipulation Inference (BAMI)的新方法，用于提升GUI接地模型在复杂场景中的性能。研究发现，现有模型在高分辨率图像和复杂界面元素下存在精度偏差和模糊偏差问题。BAMI通过引入粗到细的关注机制和候选选择策略，有效缓解了这些偏差。实验结果表明，该方法在无需训练的情况下显著提升了多个GUI接地模型的准确率，例如在ScreenSpot-Pro基准上将TianXi-Action-7B模型的准确率从51.9%提升至57.8%。此外，消融实验验证了BAMI方法在不同参数配置下的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于Wasserstein距离的分层联邦学习泛化界研究
item.content:encoded:
研究分层联邦学习的泛化界，利用Wasserstein距离进行分析。



摘要： 本文研究了分层联邦学习（HFL）环境下基于Wasserstein距离的泛化界。通过引入多层树状结构建模数据采样过程，作者在损失函数满足Lipschitz条件的前提下，推导出泛化界的表达式。该方法通过超采样构造衡量算法对采样树中单个节点变化的敏感性。同时，结合联邦学习结构，恢复并严格推导出现有最优的条件互信息（CMI）边界。此外，作者还展示了该边界可与差分隐私假设结合，获得基于算法隐私的泛化界。通过高斯位置模型验证了边界的紧致性，证明其能准确反映泛化误差的渐近率。
